Feature: Running-app enumeration
User Story: As a Mac user, I want Waves to automatically list the apps currently running on my system, so that I can pick any of them to control its volume without manually adding anything.
Expected Behavior:
• On start() and refresh() the backend builds a snapshot by enumerating NSWorkspace.shared.runningApplications via discoverRunningApps
• Waves itself is excluded by skipping the current process PID and any app whose bundleIdentifier equals currentBundleID
• Apps with activationPolicy == .prohibited or an empty/nil localizedName are filtered out
• Each discovered app becomes an AudioApp with id/logicalID, pid, bundleID, displayName, inferred category, a category icon name, and a rendered 64x64 PNG icon (iconTIFFData) drawn from the app/bundle icon
• The list is sorted case-insensitively by display name before being mapped
• sourceInventorySummary / the 'Running app inventory' count reflect visibleApps.count
• refresh() shows a toast '<n> app(s) detected' and start() shows 'Waves is ready'
Acceptance Criteria:
• Given several GUI apps are running, when Waves starts, then each appears once in the Running list with its real name and icon
• Given Waves is running, when the list is built, then Waves does not appear in its own list
• Given an app with activationPolicy .prohibited or no localized name, when discovery runs, then it is not listed
• Given the user clicks Refresh, then the list rebuilds and a toast reports the detected app count
Impl Notes: Solid and well-guarded. Note discovery is poll/refresh-based: there is no NSWorkspace didLaunch observer, so a newly launched app only appears after a manual refresh, a device-change rebuild, or another snapshot trigger (didTerminate is observed, but launch is not). Icons are re-rendered to PNG on every snapshot build, which is mild repeated work per refresh.
Errors Found:
• [MEDIUM/inconsistency] Refresh toast counts session.apps (incl. hidden system apps), not the visible inventory (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:249) — refresh() reports the detected count as session.apps.count (AppStore.swift:249), but the list, sidebar Running count (MainWindowView.swift:175) and sourceInventorySummary (AppStore.swift:156) all use visibleApps.count. With default showSystemProcesses == false, system-category apps (Finder, System Settings, etc.) are present in session.apps but filtered out of visibleApps, so the toast over-counts versus every other surface. Re-verified: visibleApps filters .system unless showSystemProcesses (AppStore.swift:104-105). | Fix: Use visibleApps.count in the refresh toast detail so the number matches sourceInventorySummary and the source-list count.
• [LOW/edge-case] Newly launched apps don't appear until a manual refresh (no didLaunch observer) (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:886) — Discovery runs only on start()/refresh(). The store observes didActivateApplicationNotification (AppStore.swift:889, calls only checkAutoPauseMusic) and didTerminateApplicationNotification (AppStore.swift:901), but never didLaunchApplicationNotification. A user who launches a new app after Waves is open won't see it in the mixer until Refresh or a device-change rebuild fires. The implNote acknowledges this. | Fix: Add an NSWorkspace.didLaunchApplicationNotification observer that triggers a debounced refresh() so newly launched apps appear without manual refresh.
• [LOW/performance] Per-app icons re-rendered to 64x64 PNG on every snapshot build (Sources/Waves/Services/Audio/WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1187) — discoverRunningApps maps every representative through iconTIFFData() (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1187 -> iconPNGData at 1221), which lockFocus/draw/encodes a fresh 64x64 PNG per app on every buildSnapshot (start/refresh/device-change). Re-verified the work runs on Task.detached (line 993), so it is not a main-thread hazard, just repeated CPU/allocation work; icons rarely change between refreshes. | Fix: Cache rendered PNG data keyed by logicalID (or bundleID + icon identity) and reuse it across snapshots, re-rendering only when missing.

Feature: Audio-aware live vs. ready classification
User Story: As a user, I want apps that are actually producing sound to be marked Live and floated to the top, so that I can immediately find what I'm hearing and adjust it.
Expected Behavior:
• getAudibleProcessPIDs() reads the Core Audio kAudioHardwarePropertyProcessObjectList and keeps PIDs whose kAudioProcessPropertyIsRunningOutput is true
• An app is marked isAudible (routingState .live) when its process family PIDs intersect the audible set; otherwise it is .monitorOnly ('Ready')
• isActive is set when the app is audible or frontmost
• liveApps surfaces .live apps plus managed unmuted apps with measurable level; recentApps is everything visible that is not live (only when showRecentApps is on)
• Activity sort ranks live (0), managed-audible (1), active (2), managed (3), others (4)
• The menu bar Live/Recent sections and main-window Frontmost/Recent filters are populated from these computed lists
Acceptance Criteria:
• Given an app is emitting audio, when discovery runs, then it shows routingState .live and appears in the Live/Frontmost section
• Given an app is running but silent, then it shows as Ready (monitorOnly) and falls into Recent (if recent apps are enabled)
• Given the Core Audio process list cannot be read, then no app is falsely marked audible and discovery still succeeds (audiblePIDs empty)
• Given activity sort is selected, then live apps are ordered above ready/idle apps
Impl Notes: Live detection degrades gracefully: if getAudibleProcessPIDs returns empty (permission/OS issue) isAudible is forced false via the !audiblePIDs.isEmpty guard, so nothing is mislabeled live but a genuinely-playing app may then sit in Recent until levels arrive. isActive also folds in frontmost, so a silent foreground app counts as active — intentional but can surprise.
Errors Found: • [LOW/dead-code] !supportsPerAppRouting branch that wipes live classification is unreachable (deployment target is 14.2) (Sources/Waves/Services/Audio/WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1037) — buildSnapshot's per-index loop (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1037) forces routingState = .monitorOnly and notes = 'Per-app route requires macOS 14.2+' when !supportsPerAppRouting. Re-verified: supportsPerAppRouting (line 310) returns false only below macOS 14.2, and Package.swift sets the platform floor to .macOS("14.2"), so #available(macOS 14.2,*) is always true at runtime and this branch never executes. The note and the monitorOnly override (which would have erased fresh .live state) are dead code. | Fix: Remove the !supportsPerAppRouting branch (and the related compatibility/notes overrides) or gate the property behind the actual minimum so the dead path is dropped.

Feature: Process consolidation & helper filtering (logical identity)
User Story: As a user, I want each app to appear as a single row instead of a clutter of helper/renderer processes, so that the mixer stays clean and one slider controls the whole app.
Expected Behavior:
• isManageableApp filters out processes whose name/bundle contains excludedProcessMarkers (daemon, updater, launcher, agent, service, crashpad, login item, xpc, helper, web content, renderer, gpu, etc.)
• logicalAppID returns the bundle ID for ordinary apps, a per-PID suffixed ID for companion/helper audio processes, and a name- prefixed slug when no bundle ID exists
• Multiple running processes that map to the same logicalID are collapsed to one representative chosen by score() (favoring .regular activation, active, real .app bundle, has icon, non-companion, penalizing daemon/agent/etc.)
• processFamily groups a representative with its bundle-family and same-logicalID siblings so audibility is evaluated across the whole family (bundleFamilyMatches, with sibling-helper roots for Firefox/Zen/Mozilla)
• resolveProcessObjectIDs later re-expands the family PIDs to translate to Core Audio process objects when a route is applied
Acceptance Criteria:
• Given Chrome with several helper/renderer processes, when discovery runs, then only one Chrome row appears
• Given a process named '... Renderer'/'... Helper', then it is excluded from the manageable list
• Given two processes share a bundle ID, then exactly one representative row is shown and it is the higher-scored one
• Given a Firefox-family helper is audible, then the parent Firefox row is marked live via family matching
Impl Notes: Logic is thorough and unit-tested. Risk: excludedProcessMarkers are broad substring matches on name+bundleID, so legitimately-named apps could be hidden — e.g. anything containing 'agent', 'service', or 'launcher' (a hypothetical 'Service Station', 'Agent' game, etc.) is silently dropped with no UI affordance to reveal it. There is no exception list. AppDiscoveryPolicy lives under Sources/WavesAudioCore/Backend/ (codeRefs use the source path).
Errors Found: • [LOW/edge-case] Broad substring exclusion markers can silently hide legitimately named apps with no escape hatch (Sources/WavesAudioCore/Backend/AppDiscoveryPolicy.swift:133) — isManageableApp (AppDiscoveryPolicy.swift:75-83) drops any app whose lowercased name+bundleID contains a marker from excludedProcessMarkers (line 133), including the short generic substrings 'agent', 'service', and 'launcher'. A real, user-facing app named e.g. 'Service Station', 'Agent', or one with 'launcher' in its bundle ID is filtered out in discoverRunningApps (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1147) before it ever becomes an AudioApp. Re-verified there is no escape hatch: the 'Show system processes' toggle only un-hides the .system category in visibleApps; it does NOT re-admit apps removed by isManageableApp, and the per-app exclusions feature is the inverse (user-driven exclusion). | Fix: Tighten matching (whole-word/bundle-suffix patterns instead of naked substrings, as already done for 'arc' in inferCategory) and/or add an allowlist or a 'show hidden processes' override so a false-positive app can be recovered.

Feature: Category inference & per-app icon
User Story: As a user, I want each discovered app tagged with a category and a recognizable icon, so that I can scan, sort, and group the list quickly.
Expected Behavior:
• inferCategory maps tokens to browser/conferencing/media/communication/system/unknown (Safari/Chrome/Arc-as-word/browser; zoom/meet/teams/webex/facetime; spotify/music/vlc/podcast/tv; discord/slack/messages/telegram; com.apple.* => system)
• 'arc' is matched only as a whole word so Archive Utility / Monarch are not misclassified as browsers
• iconName(for:) maps each category to an SF Symbol used as a fallback glyph
• The app's real icon is captured as a 64x64 PNG (iconTIFFData) from app.icon, bundleURL, or urlForApplication
• Category drives Category sort order and the conferencing detection used by auto-pause
Acceptance Criteria:
• Given Safari, Zoom, Spotify, Discord and Finder, when discovery runs, then they are categorized browser/conferencing/media/communication/system respectively
• Given 'Archive Utility', then it is NOT categorized as a browser
• Given an app with no embedded icon, then a category SF-Symbol fallback name is still provided
• Given Category sort, then rows group by category then name
Impl Notes: Heuristics are keyword-based and intentionally rough. The media token 'music' matches any app whose name/bundle contains 'music' (Apple Music, but also unrelated apps), 'tv' and 'meet' are short substrings that can false-positive, and there is no user override to recategorize an app. Acceptable for an inference layer but worth noting as a UX edge.
Errors Found: • [LOW/edge-case] Category inference uses short ambiguous substrings ('tv', 'meet', 'music') with no user override (Sources/WavesAudioCore/Backend/AppDiscoveryPolicy.swift:38) — inferCategory (AppDiscoveryPolicy.swift:27-61) matches media via token.contains("tv") and contains("music") (lines 44-45) and conferencing via contains("meet") (line 38). These are short, unanchored substrings that false-positive on unrelated names/bundle IDs, and there is no way to recategorize a mis-tagged app. Because category drives Category sort (AppStore.swift:1292) and conferencing-based auto-pause (checkAutoPauseMusic at AppStore.swift:950-953), a misclassification can mis-sort or wrongly auto-pause an app. The named ACs (Safari/Zoom/Spotify/Discord/Finder, Archive Utility) all pass; 'arc' is correctly whole-word anchored. | Fix: Anchor the risky tokens as whole words via \b...\b regex (as done for 'arc'), and consider a per-app category override stored in preferences.

Feature: Snapshot merge, persistence & retention of quit apps
User Story: As a returning user, I want my per-app volume/mute/boost/output choices to stick to the right app across refreshes and relaunches even if it briefly isn't running, so that I don't have to reconfigure every session.
Expected Behavior:
• buildSnapshot merges each freshly-discovered app with the previous snapshot by logicalID, carrying over desiredVolume, appliedVolume, isMuted, isPinned, compatibility, volumeBoost, muteSource and targetDeviceUID
• Previously-known apps not in the current scan are retained (as monitorOnly or managed if a controller is still active) only while isStillRunning confirms they're alive; otherwise they drop off
• On launch the AppStore loads a cached session from disk and re-merges it with the freshly built live session (mergedSession) so saved settings reattach to rediscovered apps
• cleanupStaleEntries prunes pending volume targets and paused-music IDs for apps no longer present
• handleAppTermination + releaseControllers immediately demote a quit app to monitorOnly and tear down its route without waiting for a refresh
• Each snapshot build is persisted via sessionStore
Acceptance Criteria:
• Given an app had a custom volume, when the list refreshes, then the same app keeps that volume (matched by logicalID, not pid)
• Given the app quits, when termination fires, then its row goes inactive/monitorOnly and its controller is released promptly
• Given a previously-seen app is no longer running at all, then it is removed from the list
• Given Waves relaunches, then cached per-app settings reattach to the rediscovered apps
Impl Notes: Merge keys on logicalID, which is the bundle ID for ordinary apps, so identity is stable. For bundle-less apps logicalID is a name slug, so a renamed/localized app could lose its saved settings — a narrow edge. Retention correctly hinges on isStillRunning so the list doesn't accumulate dead apps.

Feature: Empty, loading & filtered-list states
User Story: As a user, I want clear feedback when the list is loading or when no apps match the current view or search, so that an empty mixer never looks broken.
Expected Behavior:
• The live backend seeds from .empty (no fabricated apps/volumes/error) so nothing fake appears before the first real scan
• While loading, both the main window and menu bar show a 'Refreshing' progress indicator (isLoading true while session.apps is empty)
• The main window shows a ContentUnavailableView with per-filter empty titles/messages (No Running Apps / No Pinned Apps / No Live Apps / No Recent Apps), and a search-specific 'Try a different search term.' message
• The menu bar shows a 'No audio apps detected' empty card with Refresh and Settings buttons when pinned, live and recent are all empty
• visibleApps respects showSystemProcesses (default off => system-category apps hidden) and recentApps respects showRecentApps
• Search filters across all visible apps (not just the active scope) by name or category
Acceptance Criteria:
• Given no cached session, when Waves first launches, then the list is empty with a loading indicator and no fake rows
• Given no apps are detected, when the menu bar opens, then a 'No audio apps detected' card with Refresh/Settings is shown
• Given a filter/search yields no rows, then a descriptive empty state (not a blank list) is shown
• Given showSystemProcesses is off, then com.apple.* system apps are hidden from the list
Impl Notes: Empty/loading/search states are all real and wired. Minor: isLoading is only true when session.apps is empty, so a refresh that already has a populated list shows no spinner in the main window (the menu bar guards the same way) — acceptable but means subsequent refreshes feel instantaneous with no progress cue.
Errors Found:
• [MEDIUM/ux] Loading indicator and empty-state card render simultaneously on first launch (Sources/Waves/Features/Mixer/MenuBarMixerView.swift:52) — On first launch with no cached session, session.apps is empty so isLoading is true (AppStore.swift:194), while pinnedApps/liveApps/recentApps are all empty so the empty-state UI also renders. Re-verified in the menu bar: the 'Refreshing audio sessions' spinner (MenuBarMixerView.swift:30) and the 'No audio apps detected' card (line 52) are gated independently and both show. In the main window the 'Refreshing' pill (MainWindowView.swift:64) overlays the SourceListView 'No Running Apps' ContentUnavailableView (line 336, gated only by apps.isEmpty). The user is simultaneously told the app is loading and that nothing was found. | Fix: Suppress the empty-state card/ContentUnavailableView while store.isLoading is true (show only the spinner until the first scan completes), or merge them into a single 'Loading…' state.
• [MEDIUM/inconsistency] Menu bar shows 'No audio apps detected' and hides running apps when 'Show recent apps' is off (Sources/Waves/Features/Mixer/MenuBarMixerView.swift:52) — The menu bar renders only Pinned, Live and Recent sections — there is no 'Running' section (MenuBarMixerView.swift:42-50). recentApps returns [] whenever preferences.showRecentApps is false (AppStore.swift:125). The empty-card condition is pinnedApps.isEmpty && liveApps.isEmpty && recentApps.isEmpty (line 52). So with the user-exposed 'Show recent apps' toggle off, any time nothing is pinned and nothing is live, the menu bar shows 'No audio apps detected' even though silent running apps exist — and those apps are unreachable from the menu bar entirely (the main window's Running scope still lists them, so the two surfaces disagree). | Fix: Base the empty card on store.visibleApps.isEmpty rather than the Live/Recent/Pinned sections, and/or keep a minimal running list visible in the menu bar when Recent is disabled.
• [LOW/ux] Subsequent refreshes show no progress cue once the list is already populated (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:234) — isLoading is computed as session.apps.isEmpty in both start() (AppStore.swift:194) and refresh() (line 234), so once at least one app is present a Refresh never sets isLoading true. The main-window 'Refreshing' pill (MainWindowView.swift:64) and the menu-bar spinner (MenuBarMixerView.swift:30) are both gated on isLoading, so a refresh over an already-populated list shows no progress indication while the async snapshot rebuild runs. isRefreshing is tracked (line 233) but never surfaced in the UI. | Fix: Drive the progress indicators from isRefreshing (or an explicit isBusy = isLoading || isRefreshing) so a refresh over a populated list still shows the spinner/pill.
• [LOW/accessibility] Loading ProgressViews have no accessibility label, announcing bare 'in progress' (Sources/Waves/Features/Mixer/MainWindowView.swift:65) — Both loading spinners are bare ProgressView() with the descriptive text as a sibling Text node, not combined: menu bar (MenuBarMixerView.swift:31-37) and main window (MainWindowView.swift:65-71). VoiceOver announces the ProgressView as an unlabeled 'in progress' indicator separate from the 'Refreshing'/'Refreshing audio sessions' caption, so the loading state is not conveyed as a single coherent element. Lower-impact than the visual contradictions above but a real a11y gap in the same loading UI. | Fix: Wrap each spinner+caption in an .accessibilityElement(children: .combine) container or add an .accessibilityLabel to the ProgressView so the loading state reads as one element.

Feature: Per-app volume slider (0-100%)
User Story: As a macOS user running several audio apps, I want a per-app volume slider in the mixer, so that I can set each app's loudness independently without touching the system volume.
Expected Behavior:
• Each mixer row shows a Slider bound to app.desiredVolume over the range 0...1, plus a monospaced '<n>%' label (Int(desiredVolume*100)) (MixerRowView.swift:57-91, 283-304)
• Dragging the slider calls store.setDesiredVolume on every tick, which clamps to [0,1], updates session.apps[index].desiredVolume and appliedVolume (0 if muted), records a pendingVolumeTarget, and invalidates the visible-apps cache (AppStore.swift:356-382)
• On drag end (onEditingChanged false) store.commitDesiredVolume schedules an immediate backend apply; mid-drag ticks are debounced via scheduleVolumeApply with a 50ms delay (MixerRowView.swift:65-69; AppStore.swift:384-393, 395-462, 51)
• Backend setDesiredVolume locates the app by logicalID/id, clamps to [0,1], applies the route, and sets appliedVolume = muted ? 0 : target, routingState=.managed, clears notes/lastError (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:53-83)
• The realtime IO proc scales the tapped buffer in place by effectiveVolume = volume*volumeBoost; volume==0 zeroes the output buffer entirely (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1700-1713, 1879-1938; TapDSP.swift:21-51)
• Adjusting the slider on a monitor-only app enrolls it into a managed route (creates tap + aggregate device) on first apply (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:360-387; help text 'Adjust to enroll ... in managed routing' MixerRowView.swift:211-215)
• Successful apply shows a 'Managed route active — <App> set to <n>%' success toast; failure shows a 'Volume change failed' error toast and refreshes the session (AppStore.swift:432-460)
Acceptance Criteria:
• Setting the slider to 50% results in app.desiredVolume==0.5 and the label reading '50%'
• Values passed outside 0...1 (including via URL scheme) are clamped to [0,1] before apply
• A non-finite volume (NaN/Inf) is rejected by the backend and the app's desiredVolume defaults to 1.0 without throwing (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:59-63)
• Setting volume to 0% produces silence for that app (IO proc zeroes output) while leaving other apps unaffected
• After a successful change, routingState becomes .managed and a success toast appears; on backend failure routingState becomes .error and an error toast appears
• Rapid slider dragging coalesces into debounced applies (50ms) rather than one backend call per pixel
Code Refs:
• MixerRowView.swift:57-91
• MixerRowView.swift:283-304
• AppStore.swift:356-382
• AppStore.swift:384-462
• WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:53-83
• WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1879-1938
• TapDSP.swift:21-51
Impl Notes: Core path is complete and wired end-to-end with real Core Audio taps, debouncing, clamping, NaN-guarding, and error surfacing. Concern: NaN handling in the backend (line 59-63) returns early WITHOUT applying any route or clearing a prior error — desiredVolume is set to 1.0 in the snapshot but appliedVolume and routingState are left stale, so UI and audio can diverge for that one call. Minor UX: every committed change emits a transient toast, which can be noisy for keyboard/slider nudges.
Errors Found:
• [MEDIUM/dead-code] Slider-drag debounce (50ms) is dead code; backend apply only fires on drag end (AppStore.swift:395-420) — Verified in code. The SwiftUI Slider's set closure (MixerRowView.swift:60-62 main row; 286-288 compact row) calls only store.setDesiredVolume, which updates session/pendingVolumeTargets and (optionally) the in-memory device preset, but never schedules an apply. The sole call to scheduleVolumeApply is from commitDesiredVolume with immediate:true (AppStore.swift:392), invoked only on drag end (onEditingChanged==false, MixerRowView.swift:66-68). Confirmed by grep: scheduleVolumeApply is called exactly once, always immediate:true, so the `if !immediate { try await Task.sleep(for: volumeApplyDelay) }` branch (AppStore.swift:418-420) and volumeApplyDelay=50ms (AppStore.swift:51) are never exercised. The VoiceOver adjust path also commits immediately. Backend gain therefore changes only on slider release. | Fix: Either (a) call scheduleVolumeApply(forAppID:appKey, immediate:false) from setDesiredVolume so mid-drag ticks coalesce into 50ms-debounced live applies, or (b) delete the immediate parameter, the sleep branch, and volumeApplyDelay, and correct the spec to state audio changes only on drag end.
• [LOW/error-handling] Backend NaN guard leaves appliedVolume/routingState/lastError stale and is unreachable in practice (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:59-63) — Verified. WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.setDesiredVolume rejects non-finite input by setting desiredVolume=1.0 and returning early WITHOUT calling applyRoute, updating appliedVolume, setting routingState, or clearing backendStatus.lastError (lines 59-63). A prior error chip/note would persist while desiredVolume silently jumps to 1.0. I independently confirmed the guard is effectively unreachable: in Swift max(0,min(1,NaN))==1.0 and max(0,min(1,+Inf))==1.0 (verified by running it), so the store pre-clamps NaN/Inf before the backend ever sees it (AppStore.swift:365); the URL path rejects NaN because `NaN >= 0` is false (AppStore.swift:297-298); and persisted presets cannot encode NaN through JSON Codable, so the restore loops can't deliver it either. Downgraded from medium to low: the inconsistent-state branch is real but cannot be reached by any live caller. | Fix: In the NaN branch, treat 1.0 as the target (set appliedVolume = isMuted ? 0 : 1.0, set routingState consistently, clear notes/lastError, apply the route) — or remove the redundant guard and document that upstream clamping makes NaN impossible.

Feature: Per-app mute / unmute
User Story: As a user, I want a one-click mute toggle on each app row, so that I can instantly silence or restore a specific app without losing its volume setting.
Expected Behavior:
• Each row has a borderless speaker button toggling app.isMuted; icon switches between speaker.wave.2.fill and speaker.slash.fill with a bounce symbol effect (suppressed under Reduce Motion) and selection sensory feedback (MixerRowView.swift:96-107, 313-324)
• store.setMuted optimistically sets session isMuted, sets appliedVolume to 0 when muted (else desiredVolume), stamps muteSource=.user, removes the app from pausedMusicApps on unmute, then calls backend.setMuted (AppStore.swift:491-542)
• Backend setMuted applies the route with the new muted flag; on mute it forces peakLevel/rmsLevel and appliedVolume to 0, sets routingState=.managed and clears errors (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:85-111)
• In the realtime IO proc, isMuted!=0 zeroes the output buffer so the app is fully silenced while still tapped (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1695-1698)
• The level meter overlay is hidden while muted (showsLevelMeter requires !app.isMuted) (MixerRowView.swift:173-175)
• Toggling mute on a monitor-only app enrolls it into managed routing (same applyRoute path); help text says 'Mute to enroll ...' (MixerRowView.swift:217-221)
• Success shows 'App muted'/'App unmuted' toast; failure re-merges backend state and shows 'Mute toggle failed' (AppStore.swift:521-541)
Acceptance Criteria:
• Clicking mute silences the app's audio (output zeroed) and the icon shows speaker.slash.fill
• Unmuting restores audio at the previously set desiredVolume (appliedVolume returns to desiredVolume)
• Muting sets appliedVolume to 0 and zeroes the displayed level meter/peak/rms
• A user mute sets muteSource=.user so auto-resume logic won't override it
• Mute/unmute on a not-yet-managed app establishes a managed route
• Backend failure leaves UI consistent with the backend snapshot and surfaces an error toast
Impl Notes: Fully wired with optimistic UI, mute-source tracking, level zeroing, and error recovery. The tap uses CATapMuteBehavior.mutedWhenTapped so the original system stream is muted and Waves re-renders the scaled/muted copy — correct. Minor: optimistic UI updates session before the async backend call resolves, so a failed mute briefly shows the wrong icon until the catch-branch re-merge runs.
Errors Found: • [LOW/inconsistency] On backend mute failure the UI keeps the muted icon while audio is not actually silenced (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:101-110) — Verified. Backend setMuted sets snapshot.apps[index].isMuted = isMuted (line 90) BEFORE applyRoute and does not revert it in the catch (lines 101-110); the catch only sets routingState=.error, peak/rms=0, appliedVolume=0. The store's catch then calls mergeAppState (AppStore.swift:535-536), which copies the backend app verbatim (mergeAppState line 1425), so isMuted stays true. The full row renders speaker.slash.fill (MixerRowView.swift:100) plus the red error chip and inline note (line 111-116), even though the tap/route failed and the app's audio is in fact NOT muted. The level meter is also hidden (showsLevelMeter requires !isMuted), reinforcing the false 'muted' impression. | Fix: In the backend setMuted catch branch, revert snapshot.apps[index].isMuted to its pre-call value so a failed mute does not present as applied, or have the store reconcile the icon to the actual route outcome rather than the requested flag.

Feature: Per-app volume boost (1x-4x gain above 100%)
User Story: As a user with an app that is too quiet even at 100%, I want to apply a 2x/3x/4x boost, so that I can amplify just that app beyond its normal maximum.
Expected Behavior:
• Each row has a BoostMenu offering 1x/2x/3x/4x; the current multiplier is shown as '<n>x' (MixerRowView.swift:93-94, 310-311, 361-388)
• store.setVolumeBoost clamps to [1.0,4.0], updates session volumeBoost optimistically, and calls backend.setVolumeBoost; success/failure toasts shown (AppStore.swift:544-588)
• Backend setVolumeBoost clamps to [1,4], updates the live controller via controller.setVolumeBoost, then re-applies the route (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:113-140)
• The IO proc multiplies volume by boost: effectiveVolume = volume*volumeBoost, applied per-sample by TapDSP.scale (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1899, 1934)
• TapDSP.scale clamps every scaled sample to the format's valid range (±1.0 float, Int16/Int32 min/max) so boost cannot emit out-of-range samples / hard wrap-around clipping (TapDSP.swift:21-51, 105-117)
• Boost persists across snapshot rebuilds (merged from previous app) and is saved into per-device volume presets when that preference is on (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1010; AppStore.swift:555-565)
Acceptance Criteria:
• Selecting 2x makes the app's effective gain 2.0 (volume*2), audibly louder than 100%
• Boost values are clamped to the 1x-4x range at both store and backend layers
• Boosted samples are clamped to the format range (no integer wrap-around producing harsh artifacts)
• Changing boost on a managed app updates the live controller without tearing down the tap (controller.setVolumeBoost on existing controller)
• The selected boost multiplier is reflected in the row label and survives a session refresh
Impl Notes: Complete and a genuine differentiator (true >100% gain via DSP). Correctness note: clamping each sample to ±1.0 means boost on already-loud content effectively flat-tops (soft-ish) at full scale — louder but with clipping distortion on peaks, which is inherent to naive gain and not a soft limiter. setVolumeBoost(backend) updates the controller AND re-applies the route, which for a live controller calls apply() a second time with the same values — harmless but slightly redundant.

Feature: Global keyboard shortcuts for frontmost-app volume/mute
User Story: As a user, I want global hotkeys (⌘⌥↑/↓ for volume, ⌘⌥M for mute) that act on the frontmost app, so that I can adjust the app I'm using without opening the Waves menu.
Expected Behavior:
• increase/decreaseVolumeForFrontmostApp pick activeApps.first (fallback visibleApps.first), clamp the step to [0.01,0.5], nudge desiredVolume by ±step within [0,1], then setDesiredVolume + commitDesiredVolume and show an info toast (AppStore.swift:1210-1246)
• toggleMuteForFrontmostApp flips isMuted on the frontmost app and shows a Muted/Unmuted info toast (AppStore.swift:1248-1262)
• All three early-return unless preferences.enableKeyboardShortcuts is true (AppStore.swift:1211,1230,1249)
• The VoiceOver accessibilityAdjustableAction on each slider nudges by a fixed 0.05 step and commits immediately (MixerRowView.swift:78-80, 223-238, 305-307, 343-358)
• Default keystrokes documented as ⌘⌥↑ / ⌘⌥↓ / ⌘⌥M (README.md:122-124)
Acceptance Criteria:
• With shortcuts enabled, ⌘⌥↑ raises the frontmost app's volume by one step (default 0.1) up to 100%
• ⌘⌥↓ lowers it by one step down to 0%; ⌘⌥M toggles mute
• With shortcuts disabled, the hotkey handlers do nothing
• When no app is active/visible, the handlers no-op safely (guard let app)
• VoiceOver increment/decrement on a slider changes volume by 0.05 and applies it
• Each shortcut action surfaces a brief confirmation toast
Impl Notes: Handlers, clamping, and the enable-flag gate are all present and reuse the same setDesiredVolume/setMuted path, so correctness mirrors VOL-01/02. I verified the store methods and the gating flag but did not locate the actual hotkey registration (NSEvent/global monitor) in the read files — registration is assumed to live elsewhere and should be confirmed for full end-to-end coverage. The slider's VoiceOver step (0.05) differs from the hotkey default (0.1); intentional but worth noting for consistency.
Errors Found:
• [HIGH/correctness] Hotkeys target activeApps.first (sort-order dependent), not the actual frontmost app (AppStore.swift:1212) — Verified end-to-end. increase/decrease/toggleMute resolve the target as `activeApps.first ?? visibleApps.first` (AppStore.swift:1212,1231,1250). activeApps = visibleApps.filter(\.isActive) (AppStore.swift:113-114), and isActive is set to `isAudible || isFrontmost` during discovery (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1189). The list is ordered by sortMode (sortedApps, AppStore.swift:1278): with .name it is alphabetical; with .activity an audible app ranks 0 while a merely-frontmost app ranks 2 (activityRank, AppStore.swift:1303-1320). So whenever any background app is playing audio, activeApps.first is that audible app, not the app the user is in. The global NSEvent monitor (WavesApp.swift:152,179-185) fires while a different app is frontmost but passes no identifier; the store never consults NSWorkspace.shared.frontmostApplication to pick the hotkey target (it only reads frontmostApplication in checkAutoPauseMusic, AppStore.swift:945, not in the hotkey handlers). | Fix: Resolve the target by matching NSWorkspace.shared.frontmostApplication.bundleIdentifier (or pid) against session.apps, falling back to activeApps.first only when no match exists. The frontmost app is known where the event is handled (WavesApp), so pass its identifier into the store methods.
• [MEDIUM/ux] Hotkey shows a success toast even when the resolved app is excluded (no-op) (AppStore.swift:1210-1262) — Verified. increase/decreaseVolumeForFrontmostApp and toggleMuteForFrontmostApp call setDesiredVolume/setMuted, which early-return when isExcluded(app) is true (AppStore.swift:357,492), but the handlers have no exclusion guard and unconditionally show a 'Volume increased/decreased' or 'Muted/Unmuted' info toast (AppStore.swift:1221-1226,1240-1245,1256-1261). For the volume handlers the toast even reports the would-be new percentage computed locally, while app.desiredVolume never changed. If the frontmost-resolved app is excluded, nothing changes yet the user sees a confirmation toast implying it did. | Fix: Add `guard !isExcluded(app) else { return }` (or show an explanatory toast) at the top of each frontmost-app handler before mutating and before showing the success toast.
• [LOW/ux] A single volume hotkey press emits two stacked toasts (also affects mute) (AppStore.swift:1221) — Verified. increaseVolumeForFrontmostApp shows a 'Volume increased' info toast (AppStore.swift:1221) AND calls commitDesiredVolume -> scheduleVolumeApply(immediate:true), whose success path shows a second 'Managed route active — <app> set to <n>%' toast (AppStore.swift:433-438). With maxToasts=3 both appear; same for decrease. I also confirmed the same double-toast for mute: toggleMuteForFrontmostApp shows 'Muted/Unmuted' (AppStore.swift:1256) while setMuted's success path shows 'App muted/App unmuted' (AppStore.swift:528-533). So every Cmd-Opt-Up/Down/M press stacks two near-identical confirmations (and on failure, the handler's success-style toast appears alongside the error toast). | Fix: Suppress the per-apply/per-mute toast when the change originates from a keyboard shortcut (pass a silent/source flag through commitDesiredVolume/setMuted/scheduleVolumeApply), or drop the handler's own toast and rely on the apply toast.
Fix Notes: tier1: FU-FRONTMOST-RESOLUTION | tier2: FU-EXCLUDED-FRONTMOST-TOASTS | tier3: FU-HOTKEY-DOUBLE-TOAST

Feature: Exclude-from-Waves disables all per-app controls
User Story: As a user, I want to exclude certain apps from Waves, so that their audio is left untouched and I can't accidentally change their volume, mute, or boost.
Expected Behavior:
• The row context menu and accessibility action offer 'Exclude from Waves' / 'Manage with Waves' toggling store.setExcluded (MixerRowView.swift:159-168)
• Excluded rows dim to 0.55 opacity, show an 'Excluded' badge, hide the route-state chip, and disable the slider, boost menu, and mute button (MixerRowView.swift:29-37, 81, 94, 107, 109, 273-279, 308-311, 324, 326)
• store.setDesiredVolume, setMuted, setVolumeBoost, and setOutputDevice all early-return when isExcluded(app) is true (AppStore.swift:357, 492, 545, 650)
• On exclusion the store cancels any pending volume-apply task and clears the pending volume target for that app (AppStore.swift:709-710)
• Excluded apps are skipped by bulk/auto operations (e.g. boost-all and auto-mute restore loops) (AppStore.swift:826, 959, 973)
Acceptance Criteria:
• Excluding an app dims its row, shows the Excluded badge, and disables its slider/boost/mute controls
• Calls to setDesiredVolume/setMuted/setVolumeBoost/setOutputDevice for an excluded app are no-ops
• Excluding an app cancels its in-flight debounced volume apply and clears its pending target
• Re-selecting 'Manage with Waves' restores the controls
• Excluded apps are not affected by bulk boost or auto-mute/auto-resume routines
Code Refs:
• MixerRowView.swift:159-168
• MixerRowView.swift:273-279
• AppStore.swift:357
• AppStore.swift:492
• AppStore.swift:545
• AppStore.swift:686-710
Impl Notes: Exclusion is consistently enforced at both the UI (disabled controls) and store (early-return) layers, plus pending-task cleanup, so it can't be bypassed via the slider. It does not by itself tear down an already-active managed controller in this guard path — exclusion relies on the controls being disabled; verify whether a previously-managed app keeps its tap alive after exclusion (the setExcluded path at 686-710 cancels pending applies but the read excerpt does not show explicit controller disposal/route restoration to original audio).
Errors Found: • [MEDIUM/logic] Per-device volume preset restore re-applies routes to excluded apps (AppStore.swift:848-862) — Verified. setDesiredVolume/setMuted/setVolumeBoost/setOutputDevice and the auto-mute/auto-resume loops all skip excluded apps (AppStore.swift:357,492,545,650,959,973), and reapplyRestoredAudioState guards with `!isExcluded` (line 826). However restoreDeviceVolumePresets iterates every app and calls backend.setDesiredVolume/setMuted/setVolumeBoost with no isExcluded check (AppStore.swift:848-862). When the output device changes (handleDeviceChange -> restoreDeviceVolumePresets, AppStore.swift:802-803) and a stored preset exists for an excluded app, Waves re-taps and re-applies volume/mute/boost to an app the user explicitly excluded — establishing a managed route on it. Note a preset is recorded for any app the user touched before excluding it, so this is reachable. Raised severity to medium: it violates the core exclusion guarantee ('excluded apps are never tapped') by silently establishing a tap, not merely a cosmetic glitch. | Fix: Add `guard !isExcluded(app) else { continue }` inside the restoreDeviceVolumePresets loop before applying any backend changes, mirroring reapplyRestoredAudioState.

Feature: Monitor-only vs managed route state (honest control gating)
User Story: As a user, I want the row to clearly tell me whether Waves is merely watching an app or actually controlling its audio, so that I understand when a volume change will or won't take effect.
Expected Behavior:
• Apps start as .live (currently audible) or .monitorOnly (visible but not managed); only after a volume/mute/boost change does applyRoute create a tap and promote the app to .managed (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1178, 1036-1055, 360-387)
• RoutingStateIndicator/Dot render per-state icon, color, and help text: managed=green check 'Managed route is active', live=accent waveform, monitorOnly hidden chip with hint 'Move the slider or mute the app to start per-app control', error=red triangle 'Route setup failed' (MixerRowView.swift:390-511)
• On an unsupported OS (<macOS 14.2) all apps are forced to .monitorOnly with note 'Per-app route requires macOS 14.2+' and compatibility .planned (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1037-1042, 310-316)
• If a managed route's controller is no longer active the app is demoted back to .monitorOnly on the next snapshot (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1044-1054, 1104-1124)
• When routingState==.error, the row displays the failure note text inline beneath the controls (MixerRowView.swift:111-116)
• slider/mute help text changes based on canControlAudio (routingState==.managed) to explain that adjusting will enroll the app (MixerRowView.swift:207-221)
Acceptance Criteria:
• A freshly listed audible app shows a 'live' chip and a not-yet-managed app shows no chip with a hint to start control
• After the first volume/mute/boost change, the app shows the green 'managed' chip
• On macOS older than 14.2, every app stays monitor-only with the explanatory note and no route is attempted
• A managed app whose controller dies reverts to monitor-only on the next refresh
• A route setup failure shows the red error chip plus the inline error note
Impl Notes: This is the 'honest routing' promise from PRODUCT.md and is genuinely implemented: control is lazily enrolled on first interaction and state is recomputed each snapshot. Edge case worth a test: an app that is denied audio-capture (TCC) will repeatedly fail applyRoute (3 retries with backoff) and land in .error rather than monitor-only, so the user sees a red chip + 'couldn't take over audio' message rather than a permission-specific prompt — capture permission is surfaced separately in diagnostics, not on the row itself (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:389-427, 288-299).
Errors Found: • [MEDIUM/edge-case] On macOS <14.2, moving the slider attempts a route and flashes a red error chip + generic failure toast instead of staying monitor-only (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:53-83) — Verified. The store layer has no OS gate (grep confirms no supportsPerAppRouting/@available/14.2 check in AppStore before invoking the backend; backendStatus.isAudioComponentInstalled is only read for onboarding/diagnostics). On an unsupported OS setDesiredVolume/setMuted/setVolumeBoost still call the backend; applyRoute throws BackendError.unsupportedOperation immediately (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:361-363); the backend setter catch sets routingState=.error with the note and rethrows (e.g. lines 76-81). The store then shows a 'Volume change failed' error toast (AppStore.swift:447) and only the subsequent refresh() rebuilds the snapshot, forcing the app back to .monitorOnly with the 'Per-app route requires macOS 14.2+' note (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1037-1041). So a route IS attempted and the user transiently sees a red error chip plus a generic failure toast rather than the calm monitor-only explanation. Upgraded confidence from likely to confirmed: every step is directly verifiable in code. | Fix: Gate control entry on OS support before invoking the backend (expose supportsPerAppRouting or check backendStatus.isAudioComponentInstalled in the store), or special-case BackendError.unsupportedOperation to set .monitorOnly with the 14.2 note and suppress the error toast, so unsupported OSes never flash an error state.

Feature: Manual per-app mute/unmute toggle
User Story: As a macOS user running several audio apps, I want to mute or unmute a specific app from its mixer row, so that I can silence one app instantly without quitting it or touching system volume.
Expected Behavior:
• Each mixer row (full and compact) shows a speaker button: filled speaker.slash.fill icon when muted, speaker.wave.2.fill when not (MixerRowView.swift:100, :317).
• Tapping the button calls store.setMuted(!app.isMuted, for: app) (MixerRowView.swift:97, :314).
• setMuted updates session.apps[index].isMuted and sets appliedVolume to 0 when muted, else desiredVolume (AppStore.swift:497-498).
• The backend silences the app: the IO render callback zeroes the output buffer whenever isMuted != 0 (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1695-1698), and the process tap is created with muteBehavior=.mutedWhenTapped so the app's normal path is muted too (line 450).
• A success toast 'App muted'/'App unmuted' with the app name is shown on success (AppStore.swift:528-533); a 'Mute toggle failed' error toast on backend failure (line 539).
• The change is persisted to the session store after applying (AppStore.swift:527).
• Muting bounces the speaker symbol and emits selection sensory feedback unless Reduce Motion is on (MixerRowView.swift:98, :101, :106).
• Accessibility label flips between 'Mute <app>' and 'Unmute <app>' (MixerRowView.swift:105, :322).
Acceptance Criteria:
• Clicking the mute button on an unmuted managed app silences its audio and shows the filled slash icon.
• Clicking again restores audio at the app's desiredVolume and shows the wave icon.
• After a mute, the app's appliedVolume reads 0; after unmute it equals desiredVolume.
• On a backend error the row state is re-synced from the backend snapshot and an error toast appears.
• VoiceOver announces 'Mute <app>' / 'Unmute <app>' matching the current state.
Impl Notes: Fully wired end-to-end: UI -> store -> actor backend -> DSP zeroing, with optimistic UI update, persistence, error rollback (re-merge from backend snapshot), and accessibility. Mute is enforced both by zeroing the re-rendered tap output and by the tap's mutedWhenTapped behavior, so audio truly stops. No correctness concerns found in the manual path itself.
Errors Found: • [MEDIUM/error-handling] On backend applyRoute failure the row shows the muted icon while audio is NOT silenced (Sources/Waves/Services/Audio/WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:90) — WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.setMuted assigns snapshot.apps[index].isMuted = isMuted at line 90 BEFORE the do/catch and never reverts it in the catch (lines 101-109). On a failed mute (tap creation fails: permission revoked mid-session, device unplugged, app quit) the backend keeps isMuted == true with routingState == .error. mergeAppState copies that whole app back into the store (AppStore.swift:1425). The mixer row icon is driven purely by app.isMuted (MixerRowView.swift:100, :317), so it renders speaker.slash.fill (muted) even though no tap was established and the app is still playing at full volume. There is an error notes line (MixerRowView.swift:111) and an error toast, but the mute glyph itself contradicts the real audio state. Verified: the catch block leaves isMuted untouched and only flips routingState/notes/levels. | Fix: In the setMuted catch block, capture the prior isMuted before line 90 and restore snapshot.apps[index].isMuted to it on failure, or introduce a distinct 'mute could not be applied' presentation so the icon does not assert a silence that did not happen.

Feature: Mute provenance — never auto-resume a user-muted app
User Story: As a user who deliberately muted an app, I want Waves to remember that I muted it myself, so that automatic features (like call auto-pause) never silently un-mute it behind my back.
Expected Behavior:
• AudioApp carries a muteSource: MuteSource field, either .user or .autoConferencing (AudioApp.swift:24-27, :193-198).
• Any direct user mute/unmute via setMuted always stamps muteSource = .user (AppStore.swift:500-501).
• Auto-pause stamps muteSource = .autoConferencing only when Waves itself mutes a media app for a call (AppStore.swift:964).
• Auto-resume only un-mutes apps whose muteSource is still .autoConferencing, explicitly excluding user-muted apps (AppStore.swift:972-973).
• muteSource is Codable and persisted, and is carried across relaunch merges (AppStore.swift:1403) and live backend snapshot merges (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1011); legacy session files without the field decode as .user (AudioApp.swift:139).
• Toggling a preset/URL/keyboard mute also flows through setMuted, so it is treated as user-sourced.
Acceptance Criteria:
• If the user mutes a media app, then a conferencing app becomes frontmost and later un-frontmost, the user-muted app stays muted (it is not in the resumable set).
• An app auto-paused by Waves has muteSource == .autoConferencing until the user touches it.
• A session file written before muteSource existed decodes with muteSource == .user (covered by AppDiscoveryPolicyTests.swift:62-67).
• muteSource round-trips through encode/decode (AppDiscoveryPolicyTests.swift:69-72).
Impl Notes: The core provenance guarantee is correctly implemented: auto-resume strictly filters on muteSource == .autoConferencing, so a user-muted app is never auto-resumed, and the field survives relaunch and legacy decode. Minor wrinkle: on auto-resume the code writes the unmuted app's muteSource back to .user (AppStore.swift:977, :992) rather than a neutral value; harmless because the app is being unmuted, but it means an unmuted app's provenance is always .user (the default), which is fine. Covered by unit tests for decode default and round-trip.
Errors Found: • [HIGH/correctness] Any whole-session backend re-sync clobbers .autoConferencing provenance with backend's stale .user, breaking auto-resume (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1425) — Auto-pause records provenance ONLY in the store session (AppStore.swift:992 sets muteSource = .autoConferencing). It is never written into the backend snapshot: the backend's setMuted/applyPreset never assign .autoConferencing, and the only backend handling is app.muteSource = previous.muteSource (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1011), which for an auto-paused app always resolves to the .user default. The store and backend therefore diverge (store=.autoConferencing, backend=.user). Numerous common operations replace the whole store session from the backend and thus overwrite .autoConferencing with .user: mergeAppState after a volume edit (AppStore.swift:429) or boost edit (:571), and full replacements at refresh() (:242), togglePinned (:596), device change (:799), applyPreset (:1010), and recoverRoutes (:1184). After any of these while the conferencing app is still frontmost, muteSource(app) == .user, so the auto-resume filter `$0.muteSource == .autoConferencing` (AppStore.swift:973) no longer matches and the music stays muted with no indication Waves muted it. (Note: the post-refresh checkAutoPauseMusic at :248 cannot re-tag because previousFrontmostApp is unchanged and the guard at :947 short-circuits.) | Fix: Propagate the mute source into the backend (have backend.setMuted accept/store a source and have auto-pause push .autoConferencing into the backend snapshot), OR preserve the store's muteSource across every backend re-merge (e.g. carry session muteSource forward in mergeAppState and in the cached/live merge instead of taking the backend's value).

Feature: Auto-pause media apps during conference calls
User Story: As someone who joins video calls with music playing, I want Waves to automatically mute my media apps when a conferencing app comes to the front and restore them afterward, so that I don't blast music into a meeting or have to scramble for the mute button.
Expected Behavior:
• Behavior is gated by preferences.autoPauseMusicForConferencing (default true), toggleable in Settings (AppStore.swift:941; SettingsView.swift:91-93; UserPreferences.swift:10).
• On every frontmost-app change (and at startup/refresh/preset apply) checkAutoPauseMusic runs; it only acts when the frontmost bundle actually changed (AppStore.swift:946-948).
• When the new frontmost app is categorized as conferencing, all visible media-category apps that are currently unmuted and not excluded are muted via the backend and tagged .autoConferencing, and tracked in pausedMusicApps (AppStore.swift:957-969).
• When the frontmost app is not conferencing, apps Waves auto-paused (muteSource == .autoConferencing, not excluded) are un-muted and pausedMusicApps is cleared (AppStore.swift:970-984).
• Only the affected apps' mute state is mutated in place (not a whole-session replace), to avoid wiping launch-restored state (AppStore.swift:986-997).
• Frontmost detection uses the live NSWorkspace.frontmostApplication rather than the periodically-refreshed snapshot (AppStore.swift:945).
Acceptance Criteria:
• With the preference on, switching focus to a conferencing app mutes a playing, unmuted media app and tags it .autoConferencing.
• Switching focus away from conferencing (to a non-conferencing app) un-mutes only those Waves-auto-paused apps.
• A media app the user had already muted is not touched by auto-resume (overlaps MUTE-02).
• Excluded apps are never auto-paused (filter excludes isExcluded).
• With the preference off, no automatic mute/unmute occurs.
Impl Notes: Works and is observer-driven off NSWorkspace.didActivateApplication. Edge-case gaps: (1) auto-pause only fires on a frontmost-app CHANGE — enabling the preference (or launching) while a conferencing app is already frontmost will not pause music until focus changes, because the previousFrontmostApp guard short-circuits (line 947). (2) 'conferencing' is inferred purely from the frontmost app's category, not from whether a call/mic is actually active, so simply focusing (e.g.) a Zoom window with no call mutes media. (3) Resume happens on focusing ANY non-conferencing app, so quickly tabbing away mid-call resumes music. These are product-behavior nuances rather than crashes; the core mute/restore plumbing is sound.
Errors Found:
• [MEDIUM/concurrency] Rapid frontmost changes interleave the pause and resume tasks, leaving music stuck muted (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:955) — checkAutoPauseMusic updates previousFrontmostApp synchronously then spawns a Task that performs per-app `await backend.setMuted` and only writes the resulting mute state / muteSource into session AFTER all awaits (AppStore.swift:989-995). AppStore is @MainActor and backend is an actor, so each `await backend.setMuted` genuinely suspends the task on the main actor. Two quick frontmost changes (conferencing -> non-conferencing) enqueue Task A (pause) and Task B (resume). While Task A is suspended on its await — before it has written .autoConferencing into session (line 992) — Task B runs, computes resumable from the still-unpaused session (sees nothing), calls pausedMusicApps.removeAll() (line 983), and returns. Task A then resumes, finishes, and writes the app as muted/.autoConferencing while the user is already on a non-conferencing app, with no resume pending. The interleave is structurally guaranteed by the deferred session write, not merely possible. | Fix: Serialize auto-pause handling: cancel the prior in-flight checkAutoPauseMusic task before starting a new one, or funnel pause/resume through a single async sequence, and apply session/pausedMusicApps mutations atomically relative to the backend calls (e.g. mark intent in session before awaiting).
• [LOW/edge-case] Enabling auto-pause while a conferencing app is already frontmost does not pause music until focus changes (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:947) — checkAutoPauseMusic short-circuits when currentFrontmostApp == previousFrontmostApp (AppStore.swift:947). The Settings 'Auto-pause music during calls' toggle setter only writes the preference and persists (SettingsView.swift:92-95); it never calls checkAutoPauseMusic and never resets previousFrontmostApp. So if a conferencing app was already frontmost when the user turns the preference on, no auto-pause happens until the user switches focus away and back. Verified the toggle setter has no side-effect call into the store's auto-pause routine. | Fix: From the SettingsView auto-pause toggle setter, when turning the preference on, reset previousFrontmostApp to nil and call store.checkAutoPauseMusic() so the current frontmost app is evaluated immediately.

Feature: Mute state persists and is re-applied across relaunch
User Story: As a user who muted certain apps, I want those mutes to still be in effect after I quit and reopen Waves (or the app), so that my routing setup is durable and I don't have to redo it every launch.
Expected Behavior:
• The session snapshot (including isMuted, appliedVolume, muteSource) is saved on every mute change (AppStore.swift:527, persistSessionSnapshot -> sessionStore.save).
• On launch the cached session is loaded and merged with the freshly-built live snapshot, preserving isMuted, appliedVolume, and muteSource per logical app (AppStore.swift:1383-1405).
• After merge, reapplyRestoredAudioState re-applies saved mute/volume/boost to the backend for any 'customized' app (isMuted true, boost>1, non-default volume, or pinned device) so audible output matches the restored UI (AppStore.swift:820-845, esp. :829, :834).
• Apps not running this session are skipped gracefully (caught error, saved state left intact) (AppStore.swift:840-843).
• The backend's own buildSnapshot merge also carries isMuted and muteSource forward from the previous snapshot (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1007, :1011).
Acceptance Criteria:
• Muting app A, quitting Waves, and relaunching shows app A still muted in the mixer.
• After relaunch, app A's audio is actually silenced (backend route re-applied), not just visually muted.
• A user-muted app restored from disk retains muteSource == .user so auto-resume won't unmute it.
• If app A is not running on relaunch, its saved muted state is preserved without error.
Impl Notes: Persistence and restore are wired and re-apply audio (not just UI) on launch. One subtlety: reapplyRestoredAudioState calls backend.setVolumeBoost/setMuted/setDesiredVolume which each create or reuse a managed route — for a muted app this re-establishes a tap purely to keep it silenced, which is correct but means a previously-untapped, user-muted app becomes 'managed' on relaunch. Restore is best-effort and silently skips apps that aren't running. No data-loss issues observed.

Feature: Mute via global keyboard shortcut and URL scheme automation
User Story: As a power user, I want to toggle mute for the frontmost app with ⌘⌥M or via a waves:// URL, so that I can control audio hands-free or from scripts and automations.
Expected Behavior:
• ⌘⌥M toggles mute for the frontmost (or first visible) app when keyboard shortcuts are enabled (WavesApp.swift:174-185; AppStore.swift:1248-1262).
• The global key monitor ignores the shortcut while typing in a text field and requires both Command and Option (WavesApp.swift:168-177).
• The shortcut is no-op when preferences.enableKeyboardShortcuts is false (AppStore.swift:1249); the monitor is installed/removed based on that preference (AppStore.swift:1272-1276).
• Space and 'm' also toggle mute for the focused row inside the main window list (MainWindowView.swift:381-382).
• waves://mute?app=ID&muted=true|false validates inputs (app id <=256 chars, muted parses as Bool), looks up the app, and calls setMuted; invalid input or unknown app shows a warning toast (AppStore.swift:312-328).
• URL-scheme commands are gated by preferences.enableURLScheme and rate-limited to 10 well-formed commands per 60s (AppStore.swift:256-273, :345-354).
• All three paths flow through setMuted, so muteSource becomes .user (overlaps MUTE-02).
Acceptance Criteria:
• Pressing ⌘⌥M with shortcuts enabled toggles mute on the frontmost app and shows a 'Muted'/'Unmuted' toast.
• Pressing ⌘⌥M while focused in a text field does nothing.
• With keyboard shortcuts disabled, ⌘⌥M does nothing.
• A valid waves://mute?app=...&muted=true URL mutes the named app; a malformed one shows 'URL command blocked'.
• URL mute is rejected when URL schemes are disabled or the rate limit is exceeded.
Impl Notes: All three alternate mute entry points are wired and converge on setMuted with input validation and rate-limiting on the URL path. The frontmost-app target for the keyboard shortcut is 'activeApps.first ?? visibleApps.first', which is a heuristic — if no app is flagged active it falls back to the alphabetically-first visible app, which may not be the app the user is actually looking at. Minor UX risk, not a defect in the mute mechanism itself.
Errors Found:
• [MEDIUM/ux] Global ⌘⌥M shows a misleading 'Muted'/'Unmuted' toast even when the target app is excluded (no-op) (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1256) — toggleMuteForFrontmostApp picks activeApps.first ?? visibleApps.first (AppStore.swift:1250). visibleApps only filters by category/system, not exclusion (AppStore.swift:103-107), so the resolved frontmost app can be excluded. setMuted is a guarded no-op for excluded apps (AppStore.swift:492, returns before touching state or backend), but toggleMuteForFrontmostApp unconditionally shows a 'Muted'/'Unmuted' toast afterward (AppStore.swift:1256-1261). The user gets positive feedback that the app was muted when nothing happened. | Fix: Resolve the target app, then `guard !isExcluded(app) else { showToast('Cannot mute excluded app'...) ; return }` before mutating or showing the success toast.
• [LOW/inconsistency] ⌘⌥M emits two overlapping toasts for one action (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1256) — toggleMuteForFrontmostApp shows an immediate 'Muted'/'Unmuted' info toast (AppStore.swift:1256-1261), then setMuted asynchronously shows its own 'App muted'/'App unmuted' success toast (AppStore.swift:528-533). A single ⌘⌥M press therefore produces two near-simultaneous toasts with different wording, unlike the in-window and URL paths which produce one. Confirmed both toast call sites fire for a non-excluded app. | Fix: Remove the toast from toggleMuteForFrontmostApp and rely on setMuted's toast, or add a suppressToast flag to setMuted so the caller owns the single message.

Feature: Mute interaction with excluded apps and menu-bar mute indicator
User Story: As a user who excluded a sensitive app (DAW, conferencing tool) from Waves, I want mute controls disabled for it and its mute state cleared, and I want the menu-bar icon to reflect when anything is muted, so that Waves never tampers with excluded apps and I can see mute state at a glance.
Expected Behavior:
• setMuted is a guarded no-op for excluded apps (AppStore.swift:492); the mute button and slider are disabled and the row is dimmed for excluded apps (MixerRowView.swift:107, :109, :324).
• Excluding an app tears down its route, sets isMuted = false and muteSource = .user, and stops showing Waves-applied mute state (AppStore.swift:696-712).
• Presets strip excluded apps before apply/save so a preset can never re-mute/re-tap an excluded app (AppStore.swift:1001-1007, :1037-1039).
• The menu-bar icon becomes speaker.slash.fill whenever any visible app is muted, else reflects live/idle state (AppStore.swift:143-153).
• An empty mixer shows a speaker.slash placeholder with Refresh/Settings (MenuBarMixerView.swift:52-72) — this is an empty-state glyph, not a mute control.
Acceptance Criteria:
• The mute button is disabled (and row dimmed) for an excluded app.
• Calling setMuted on an excluded app does not change its state or call the backend.
• Excluding a currently-muted app clears its muted state and releases its route.
• The menu-bar icon shows the slashed-speaker symbol whenever at least one visible app is muted.
• Applying a preset never mutes an app that is in the exclusion list.
Impl Notes: Exclusion correctly blocks and clears mute, presets are sanitized against the exclusion list, and the menu-bar icon honestly reflects aggregate mute state. The menu-bar slash speaker at MenuBarMixerView.swift:54 is purely the 'no apps detected' empty-state icon and is not interactive — worth noting so it isn't mistaken for a global mute toggle. No correctness issues found.
Errors Found:
• [MEDIUM/correctness] Excluding a muted app clears mute only in the store, not in the backend snapshot, so a later re-sync resurrects the stale mute (Sources/Waves/Services/Audio/WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1117) — setExcluded clears the mute in the STORE session (AppStore.swift:706-707: isMuted = false, muteSource = .user) and calls backend.releaseControllers (line 702). But releaseControllers (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1117-1123) only resets routingState/isActive/appliedVolume/peak/rms — it does NOT reset isMuted or muteSource in the backend snapshot. So the backend still holds isMuted == true for that app. Any subsequent whole-session pull from the backend re-imports that stale value: e.g. un-excluding then togglePinned does `session = await backend.currentSnapshot()` (AppStore.swift:596), and refresh()/applyPreset/device-change similarly replace the session. Because backend.buildSnapshot carries previous.isMuted forward (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1007), the row reappears as muted even though the user cleared it by excluding. State desync between the store-local mutation and the backend snapshot. | Fix: In releaseControllers (or in a backend call invoked from setExcluded) also reset snapshot.apps[index].isMuted = false and muteSource = .user, so the backend snapshot agrees with the store after exclusion.
• [LOW/inconsistency] A muted, non-live app is unreachable from the menu bar when 'Show recent apps' is off (Sources/Waves/Features/Mixer/MenuBarMixerView.swift:46) — The menu-bar panel renders only Pinned, Live (store.liveApps), and Recent (only when showRecentApps is on) sections (MenuBarMixerView.swift:42-50). liveApps excludes muted apps (`!app.isMuted`, AppStore.swift:120), and recentApps returns [] when showRecentApps is off (AppStore.swift:125). So an app the user muted from the main window — if it isn't pinned and isn't producing level — disappears entirely from the menu bar when recent apps are hidden. The menu-bar icon correctly turns to speaker.slash.fill (AppStore.swift:146-147) signaling something is muted, but there is no row to unmute it from the menu, creating a menu-bar vs. main-window state mismatch. | Fix: Include muted managed apps in a menu-bar section regardless of showRecentApps (e.g. surface muted apps under Live or a dedicated 'Muted' group) so they can be unmuted from the menu.
• [LOW/accessibility] Menu-bar icon's mute state is not exposed to VoiceOver (Sources/Waves/App/WavesApp.swift:57) — The MenuBarExtra uses a static title 'Waves' with systemImage bound to store.menuBarIconName (WavesApp.swift:57-60). The mute/live/idle distinction is carried solely by the SF Symbol variant (speaker.slash.fill vs wave.3 vs wave.2). There is no accessibility label conveying 'muted'/'playing', so a VoiceOver user always hears 'Waves' regardless of state and cannot perceive the at-a-glance mute indicator the story promises. | Fix: Provide a dynamic accessibility label for the MenuBarExtra reflecting the current state (e.g. 'Waves — muted', 'Waves — playing', 'Waves — idle') derived from the same logic as menuBarIconName.

Feature: Per-app boost selection (1x/2x/3x/4x)
User Story: As a Waves user with a too-quiet app, I want to pick a 2x, 3x, or 4x boost for that specific app from its mixer row, so that I can make it louder than the system maximum without touching other apps.
Expected Behavior:
• Each mixer row (full and compact) shows a BoostMenu with options 1x, 2x, 3x, 4x (MixerRowView.swift:366,370-374).
• The menu label shows the app's current boost as an integer 'Nx' using monospaced digits (MixerRowView.swift:376).
• Selecting an option calls store.setVolumeBoost(boost, for: app) (MixerRowView.swift:371-372).
• store.setVolumeBoost optimistically updates session.apps[index].volumeBoost, clamps to [1.0, 4.0], then calls backend.setVolumeBoost and merges the returned snapshot (AppStore.swift:548-571).
• On success a 'Boost updated' toast shows the app name and 'Nx' for ~1.1s; on failure a 'Boost update failed' error toast shows the error (AppStore.swift:574-585).
• The backend marks the app routingState .managed on success and .error with notes on failure (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:129-138).
• Boost controls are disabled for excluded apps (Waves itself), both in UI (.disabled(isExcluded), MixerRowView.swift:94,311) and in the store guard (AppStore.swift:545).
Acceptance Criteria:
• Opening the boost menu lists exactly 1x, 2x, 3x, 4x.
• Choosing 3x updates the row label to '3x' and shows a 'Boost updated: <app>: 3x' toast.
• A boost value passed outside [1.0,4.0] is clamped to that range before reaching the backend.
• For an excluded app the boost control is disabled and setVolumeBoost is a no-op.
• On backend failure the app shows an error state and an error toast appears.
Impl Notes: Fully wired UI->store->backend with optimistic update, clamping, toasts, and error handling. UX concern: the Menu does not mark the currently-selected option (no checkmark/state), so the dropdown gives no visual cue of which boost is active; the current value only appears on the closed label (MixerRowView.swift:369-376). Minor: the success toast fires from store-side clampedBoost even though the authoritative value comes from the backend snapshot merge.
Errors Found: • [LOW/accessibility] Boost menu gives no indication of the currently-selected option inside the dropdown (Sources/Waves/Features/Mixer/MixerRowView.swift:369) — BoostMenu (MixerRowView.swift:368-387) builds the menu as four plain Buttons ('1x'..'4x') with no checkmark, .pickerStyle, or selected-state marker. When the menu is open, neither a sighted user nor VoiceOver can tell which boost is currently active from within the list — every option looks identical. The active value is only exposed on the closed label (Text + accessibilityValue at MixerRowView.swift:376,386), so the in-menu experience is ambiguous. This is a minor accessibility/UX gap, not a functional defect; the first tester noted it as a BOOST-01 implNote but did not file it. | Fix: Render the active option with a checkmark (e.g. Button with a leading Image(systemName: boost == app.volumeBoost ? "checkmark" : "") or use a Picker bound to the boost) so the open menu reflects the current selection for both sighted and VoiceOver users.

Feature: Realtime gain application with clip protection
User Story: As a user who has set a boost, I want the boosted gain applied to the app's live audio without harsh clipping, so that the app is audibly louder but does not distort into out-of-range garbage.
Expected Behavior:
• The tap render path computes effectiveVolume = volume * volumeBoost and passes it as the gain to TapDSP.scale (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1899,1934).
• TapDSP.scale multiplies each sample in place and clamps: float32 to [-1.0, 1.0], int16/int32 saturate to their type min/max instead of overflowing (TapDSP.swift:33-47,105-117).
• A gain of exactly 1.0 is a no-op early return, so 1x boost at full volume leaves samples untouched (TapDSP.swift:27).
• Peak/RMS levels are computed from the original (pre-scale) input, so meters reflect source level not boosted level (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1924-1932).
• controller.apply(volume:volumeBoost:muted:) and setVolumeBoost write the boost into a lock-free TapRenderState read on the audio thread (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1651-1670).
• Boost has no effect when muted or volume==0 because the render path zeroes output before scaling (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1695-1705).
Acceptance Criteria:
• A float32 sample of 0.5 at 4x gain emits 1.0 (saturated), and -0.5 emits -1.0 (verified by TapDSPTests.float32BoostClampsToValidRange).
• An int16 sample of 20000 at 4x saturates to Int16.max rather than wrapping (TapDSPTests.int16ScalingSaturatesInsteadOfOverflowing).
• Gain of 1.0 leaves the buffer byte-identical (TapDSPTests.gainOfOneIsNoOp).
• Level meters do not jump when boost increases, since levels are read pre-scale.
• An unknown sample format results in no scaling (samples unchanged).
Impl Notes: Core DSP is allocation-free, clamps correctly, and is unit-tested across float32/int16/int32. Correctness concern: boost is applied by hard clamping (limiting) rather than any soft limiter/headroom, so loud source material at 3x/4x will flat-top at full scale and distort audibly — this is the expected behavior of the code, and the README/Settings copy acknowledges it by advising users to reserve 2x-4x for quiet apps (SettingsView.swift:202). TapSampleFormat.unknown silently skips scaling, so a tap whose format isn't recognized plays at unity regardless of boost (TapDSP.swift:48-50).
Errors Found: • [LOW/concurrency] setVolumeBoost reads volume/mute off-queue, risking a stale read-modify-write of the render state (Sources/Waves/Services/Audio/WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1660) — PerAppTapController.setVolumeBoost (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1660-1670) reads currentState = stateBox.read() on the CALLING thread, then enqueues an async block on callbackQueue that calls writeVolumeAndMute(volume: currentState.volume, volumeBoost: clampedBoost, muted: currentState.isMuted != 0). If a concurrent apply(volume:volumeBoost:muted:) (line 1651) enqueues a newer volume/mute between the read and the async write, setVolumeBoost's block runs afterward and reverts volume/mute to the captured (stale) values. TapRenderStateBox has no boost-only writer (only write/writeVolumeAndMute/writeLevels at lines 1550/1567/1578), so the boost write must re-supply volume+mute. The window is narrow and self-correcting because backend.setVolumeBoost immediately follows with applyRoute -> controller.apply, which re-applies the correct volume/mute; audible impact is minimal. | Fix: Add a writeBoost(_:) to TapRenderStateBox that takes the lock and mutates only state.pointee.volumeBoost (and the shadow stateBox.volumeBoost), and have setVolumeBoost enqueue that instead of re-reading and re-writing volume/mute. This removes the off-queue read entirely.

Feature: Keyboard boost cycling
User Story: As a keyboard-driven user, I want to press 'b' on the selected mixer row to cycle the boost, so that I can adjust loudness without reaching for the mouse menu.
Expected Behavior:
• Pressing 'b' while a row is selected calls cycleBoost on that app (MainWindowView.swift:385).
• cycleBoost advances 1->2->3->4 and wraps 4->1 via app.volumeBoost >= 4 ? 1 : (app.volumeBoost + 1).rounded() (MainWindowView.swift:422-424).
• The cycle routes through store.setVolumeBoost, so it shares the same clamping, persistence, toast, and backend-apply path as the menu (MainWindowView.swift:424).
• handleKey is a no-op (.ignored) when no row is selected (MainWindowView.swift:408-413).
Acceptance Criteria:
• With a row at 1x selected, pressing 'b' four times produces 2x, 3x, 4x, then back to 1x.
• Pressing 'b' with no selection does nothing and does not error.
• Each 'b' press produces the same 'Boost updated' toast as the menu.
Impl Notes: Cleanly reuses the store path. Note the .rounded() guards against any fractional boost coming from per-device presets so the cycle stays on integer steps; an excluded selected app silently does nothing because store.setVolumeBoost guards on isExcluded.

Feature: Boost persistence and restore across relaunch / per-device presets
User Story: As a returning user, I want each app's boost to be remembered and re-applied when I relaunch Waves or switch output devices, so that I don't have to re-set boosts every session.
Expected Behavior:
• volumeBoost is encoded/decoded on AudioApp, PresetEntry, and AppVolumeSettings, defaulting to 1.0 when absent (AudioApp.swift:138, Preset.swift:56, UserPreferences.swift:85).
• setVolumeBoost is persisted via persistSessionSnapshot and, when enablePerDeviceVolumePresets is on, saved per (appKey, deviceID) (AppStore.swift:555-565,573).
• On relaunch, reapplyRestoredAudioState treats volumeBoost > 1.0 as a 'customized' app and re-applies it to the backend (AppStore.swift:829-833).
• Switching device restores saved per-device boost via restoreDeviceVolumePresets, calling backend.setVolumeBoost (AppStore.swift:854,859).
• applyPreset writes entry.volumeBoost and re-routes each app (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:164-171).
• Cached/merged app state preserves volumeBoost when reconciling backend snapshots (AppStore.swift:1402).
Acceptance Criteria:
• Setting 3x, quitting, and relaunching shows the app at 3x and re-applies it audibly once the app is running.
• An app not running at relaunch keeps its saved boost without error (restore is skipped per-app on failure).
• With per-device presets enabled, the same app can hold different boosts on different output devices, restored on device switch.
• Loading a preset created without a volumeBoost field defaults that entry to 1x.
• A persisted boost is clamped to [1.0,4.0] on decode (PresetEntry/AppVolumeSettings).
Impl Notes: Persistence is consistently plumbed through model, presets, and per-device settings with safe defaults. Concern: clamp ranges are inconsistent — AudioApp clamps volumeBoost to [0.0, 10.0] (AudioApp.swift:87-88) while every user-facing path (store, backend, controller, Preset, UserPreferences) clamps to [1.0, 4.0]. A decoded AudioApp with a boost of 0.0 or e.g. 7.0 would survive in the model and, until a setVolumeBoost call re-clamps it, the restore path in reapplyRestoredAudioState passes app.volumeBoost into backend.setVolumeBoost which then re-clamps to [1.0,4.0] — so audible output is safe, but the in-model value can momentarily be out of the user-facing range. A boost of 0.0<boost<1.0 would also fail the 'volumeBoost > 1.0' customized check and silently not be restored.
Errors Found:
• [HIGH/correctness] mergedSession drops cached-only apps at relaunch, so an app not running at relaunch loses its saved boost on the first launch (then it is persisted to disk) (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1390) — This is the root-cause version of the first tester's refresh() finding, and it triggers with no user action. At launch session is loaded from disk WITH the app's saved boost (AppStore.swift:83), but start() then does session = mergedSession(with: built, cached: warmSnapshot) (AppStore.swift:211). mergedSession (AppStore.swift:1383-1415) builds mergedApps = liveSession.apps and only copies cached values onto apps that ALSO appear in the live (backend) snapshot; it never appends cached-only apps.
