• An NSSavePanel opens as a sheet on the main window, restricted to the .json content type, with the default filename pre-filled as '<preset name>.json' and directory creation allowed (AppStore.swift:1086-1096).
• If the user confirms (response == .OK) the JSON is written to the chosen URL atomically and a success toast 'Preset exported / Saved to <filename>' is shown for ~2s (AppStore.swift:1097-1105).
• If no main window is available, a 'Export failed / No main window available' error toast is shown and nothing is written (AppStore.swift:1091-1094).
• Any thrown error (encode or write failure) is surfaced as an 'Export failed' toast with the error's localizedDescription (AppStore.swift:1106-1108).
Acceptance Criteria:
• Given a saved preset, when I press its Export button and pick a location, then a .json file containing that preset's name, entries (appID, desiredVolume, isMuted, volumeBoost), id and timestamps is written to disk.
• The save panel's default filename equals the preset name with a .json extension.
• On a successful save a success toast naming the file appears; cancelling the panel writes nothing and shows no toast.
• If the write fails or no main window exists, an error toast appears and no partial file replaces an existing one (write is atomic).
Impl Notes: Fully wired and functional. Correctness concern: export uses a raw JSONEncoder on a bare Preset, so the exported file has NO schemaVersion envelope, whereas the on-disk presets.json is wrapped by PersistedSchema (schemaVersion:1). The two formats are therefore not interchangeable and exported files carry no version for forward-compat. Minor leak of internal state: the exported JSON includes the preset's id, createdAt and updatedAt, which are meaningless/ignored on the recipient's machine (import reassigns them) — harmless but noisy for a 'share' format. No filename sanitization, but NSSavePanel handles that.
Errors Found:
• [HIGH/ux] Export save panel attaches to NSApp.mainWindow, not the Settings window hosting the button (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1091) — exportPreset() guards on NSApp.mainWindow and presents the NSSavePanel via beginSheetModal(for:) on it. The Export button lives in PresetSettingsView inside the Settings scene, which can be opened directly from the menu bar (MenuBarMixerView.swift:65,84) without the mixer Window ever being created, and the mixer Window can be closed independently. When only Settings is open, NSApp.mainWindow may be nil -> guard fires and shows 'Export failed / No main window available', making Export unusable. When the mixer window is open behind Settings, the panel attaches to the mixer window the user isn't looking at. | Fix: Target the window hosting the control: prefer NSApp.keyWindow ?? NSApp.mainWindow, or pass the hosting window down from SettingsView. Only show the error path when no usable window exists at all.
• [MEDIUM/inconsistency] Exported file is a bare unversioned Preset, diverging from the versioned presets.json envelope (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1082) — exportPreset() encodes a bare Preset with a plain JSONEncoder (1082-1084). PresetStore persists via PersistedSchema.encode, which wraps an ARRAY of presets in a VersionedPayload envelope ({schemaVersion, payload}) (PresetStore.swift:74, PersistedSchema.swift:17-18). The exported share file therefore carries no schemaVersion (no forward-compat marker) and is a single bare object rather than the versioned-array shape of presets.json, so the two formats are not interchangeable. | Fix: Wrap the export in a versioned envelope (VersionedPayload or a dedicated single-preset share envelope carrying schemaVersion) and teach the importer to accept it.
• [LOW/accessibility] Per-row Export button has no accessibility label identifying its preset (Sources/Waves/Settings/SettingsView.swift:246) — In PresetSettingsView the Delete button gets .accessibilityLabel("Delete preset \(preset.name)") (SettingsView.swift:259) but the sibling Export button (246-250) has none. VoiceOver announces every row's Export button simply as 'Export', so a user cannot tell which preset will be exported. | Fix: Add .accessibilityLabel("Export preset \(preset.name)") to the Export button.

Feature: Import a preset from a JSON file
User Story: As a Waves user, I want to import a preset from a JSON file, so that I can use a mix someone shared with me or restore one I backed up.
Expected Behavior:
• An 'Import' button sits in the Presets settings header (SettingsView.swift:221-224).
• Tapping Import opens an NSOpenPanel sheet limited to .json files, single selection, files only (no directories) (AppStore.swift:1114-1118).
• If no main window is available, an 'Import failed / No main window available' toast is shown (AppStore.swift:1120-1123).
• On confirm, the file is decoded into a Preset with a plain JSONDecoder (AppStore.swift:1135-1137).
• If the file's name matches an existing preset case-insensitively, the existing preset is overwritten in place (keeping the existing id, name, createdAt; updatedAt set to now) (AppStore.swift:1150-1156).
• Otherwise the imported preset is appended with a fresh UUID and createdAt/updatedAt set to now, so it never collides with an existing preset's identity (AppStore.swift:1157-1165).
• The updated presets array is persisted via presetStore.save and a 'Preset imported / <name>' success toast is shown for ~2s (AppStore.swift:1167-1173).
• Imported presets immediately appear in the library and become applicable from the menu bar and main window preset lists (MenuBarMixerView.swift:165-167, MainWindowView.swift:263-265).
Acceptance Criteria:
• Given a valid preset JSON whose name does not exist in my library, when I import it, then a new preset is added with a fresh id and now timestamps and appears in the list.
• Given a valid preset JSON whose name matches (case-insensitively) an existing preset, when I import it, then the existing preset is replaced in place (same id/name/createdAt, entries updated, updatedAt bumped) rather than duplicated.
• After a successful import, applying the imported preset from the menu bar or main window applies its entries.
• Cancelling the open panel or having no main window leaves the library unchanged (the latter shows an error toast).
Impl Notes: Wired end-to-end and imported presets are usable. Format-mismatch concern: import decodes a bare Preset with a raw JSONDecoder (no PersistedSchema). It will accept exactly what SHARE-01 exports, but it will NOT accept a presets.json copied from the app-support directory because that file is a VersionedPayload envelope wrapping an ARRAY of presets — so 'restore from a backup of presets.json' silently fails decoding (DecodingError → generic 'Import failed' toast). Also, because de-dup is by name only, importing a preset whose name collides overwrites the user's existing preset's entries without confirmation — potential surprise data loss; a renamed copy would be safer.
Errors Found:
• [HIGH/ux] Import open panel attaches to NSApp.mainWindow, so Import fails/mis-attaches when the mixer window is closed (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1120) — importPreset() guards on NSApp.mainWindow and presents the NSOpenPanel via beginSheetModal(for:) on it (1120-1125). The Import button lives in the Settings header (SettingsView.swift:221), and Settings can be opened from the menu bar with the mixer Window never created or closed. When only Settings is open, NSApp.mainWindow may be nil -> Import aborts with 'Import failed / No main window available'; when the mixer window is open behind Settings, the open panel attaches to the wrong window. Same defect as SHARE-01. | Fix: Target the Settings/key window (NSApp.keyWindow ?? NSApp.mainWindow) for beginSheetModal, falling back to the error path only when no window exists.
• [HIGH/error-handling] Importing a presets.json backup silently fails with a cryptic generic decode error (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1135) — The user story explicitly covers 'restore one I backed up.' importPreset() decodes a bare Preset with a plain JSONDecoder (1135-1137). The app's own presets.json backup is a VersionedPayload<[Preset]> envelope (PresetStore.swift:74) — an object with schemaVersion/payload wrapping an array, not a single Preset. Decoding it throws a DecodingError caught at 1174-1175 and surfaced as error.localizedDescription, i.e. the cryptic 'The data couldn't be read because it isn't in the correct format.' The user gets no hint this file type is unsupported. | Fix: Make the importer accept multiple shapes: try VersionedPayload<[Preset]>/[Preset] (importing each with name de-dup) and fall back to a single bare Preset; show a clearer message when none match.
• [MEDIUM/inconsistency] Import name handling is not trimmed unlike savePreset, defeating de-dup and allowing blank-looking names (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1150) — importPreset() compares and stores preset.name raw (empty check at 1140, dedup caseInsensitiveCompare at 1150). savePreset() trims with trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines) before comparison and storage (1041-1042). Consequences: (1) a whitespace-only name ('   ') passes preset.name.isEmpty and creates a visibly blank preset; (2) importing 'Work ' (trailing space) does NOT match existing 'Work' (caseInsensitiveCompare differs by length), so it is appended as a near-duplicate instead of overwriting in place as the story requires. | Fix: Trim preset.name before the empty check and before the caseInsensitiveCompare dedup, and store the trimmed name, mirroring savePreset.
• [LOW/inconsistency] Import success toast announces the imported file's name, not the name actually stored on overwrite (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1170) — On the overwrite path the stored name is kept as the existing preset's name (imported.name = presets[existingIndex].name, line 1153), but the success toast reports detail: preset.name (the imported file's name) at 1170. If the imported file's name differs only by case or whitespace from the existing preset (e.g. existing 'work', imported 'WORK'), the toast says 'WORK' while the library still shows 'work', mismatching what the user sees in the list. | Fix: Use the stored name (the value placed in presets[existingIndex] / the appended preset) in the success toast detail.

Feature: Validation and error handling on import
User Story: As a Waves user, I want imports of malformed, oversized, or hostile JSON files to be rejected with a clear message, so that a bad share file can't corrupt my preset library or hurt the app.
Expected Behavior:
• Before decoding, the file's size is read and any file larger than 10MB is rejected with an 'Import failed / File exceeds 10MB limit' toast (AppStore.swift:1128-1133).
• After decoding, a preset whose name is empty is rejected with 'Import failed / Preset name cannot be empty' (AppStore.swift:1140-1143).
• A preset with more than 1000 entries is rejected with 'Import failed / Preset has too many entries (max 1000)' (AppStore.swift:1145-1148).
• Any decode/read error throws into the catch block and shows 'Import failed' with the error's localizedDescription (AppStore.swift:1174-1176).
• Per-entry values are sanitized at the model layer regardless of file contents: appID is truncated to 256 chars, desiredVolume is clamped to [0,1], volumeBoost is clamped to [1,4], and a missing volumeBoost defaults to 1.0 (Preset.swift:32-57).
Acceptance Criteria:
• Importing a >10MB file shows the size-limit error and changes nothing.
• Importing JSON that decodes to a preset with an empty name shows the empty-name error and changes nothing.
• Importing a preset with >1000 entries shows the too-many-entries error and changes nothing.
• Importing non-Preset or truncated JSON shows a generic 'Import failed' error and changes nothing.
• Importing a preset with out-of-range desiredVolume/volumeBoost or an over-long appID results in clamped/truncated values being stored, not the raw file values.
Impl Notes: Reasonable defense-in-depth: size cap, name/entries-count checks, and model-level clamping of every numeric field via PresetEntry.init (which the custom decoder routes through). Gaps: (1) the size limit is read from file attributes before reading bytes, but a symlink/race is not considered (minor, local-only). (2) Decode errors surface the raw localizedDescription, which for JSON is cryptic ('The data couldn't be read because it isn't in the correct format'), giving the user little guidance — notably this is exactly what they'll hit if they try to import a presets.json backup (see SHARE-02). (3) There is no de-duplication or cap on the NUMBER of presets, only entries per preset.
Errors Found:
• [MEDIUM/error-handling] Decode failures surface a cryptic Foundation message instead of guidance (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1174) — On any decode/read failure the catch at 1174-1175 shows error.localizedDescription. For JSON decode errors this is the unhelpful 'The data couldn't be read because it isn't in the correct format.' Users importing a wrong-but-plausible file (e.g. a presets.json backup per SHARE-02) get no actionable explanation of why it failed. | Fix: Map DecodingError to a fixed friendly detail string (e.g. 'This file isn't a valid Waves preset.') rather than passing through localizedDescription.
• [LOW/edge-case] Empty-name validation accepts whitespace-only names (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1140) — The post-decode validation rejects only preset.name.isEmpty (1140). A name of spaces/tabs/newlines ('   ') is not empty, so it passes and an effectively-nameless preset is stored, contradicting the spirit of acceptance criterion 2 ('empty name shows the empty-name error and changes nothing'). | Fix: Validate preset.name.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines).isEmpty.
• [LOW/edge-case] File-size check stats a possibly-symlinked path separately from the byte read (minor TOCTOU) (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1129) — The size guard uses FileManager.attributesOfItem(atPath:) (1129-1130), which resolves symlinks and reads metadata, then Data(contentsOf:) reads the bytes separately (1135). A symlink can point the size check at a small file while the read target differs, or the file can change between the two calls. Low impact because import is a local, user-initiated file pick, but the 10MB guard is not airtight. | Fix: Read the bytes once and enforce the cap on data.count, or stat the open file descriptor, rather than statting a path that may be a symlink.

Feature: Empty-state guidance for presets/import
User Story: As a new Waves user with no presets, I want the Presets settings pane to tell me how to get one, so that I understand I can save a mix or import a shared file.
Expected Behavior:
• When store.presets is empty, the Presets pane shows the message 'No presets yet. Save a mix from the main window, or import one.' instead of a populated list (SettingsView.swift:227-232).
• The Import button remains available in the header even when the library is empty, so import is reachable from the empty state (SettingsView.swift:221-224).
Acceptance Criteria:
• With zero saved presets, the Presets settings pane displays the 'No presets yet…' guidance text.
• The Import button is visible and functional while the list is empty.
• Once at least one preset exists (saved or imported), the guidance text disappears and the list of presets with per-row Export/Delete buttons is shown.
Impl Notes: Simple and correct empty-state copy that points users toward both creation paths (save and import). The empty List is still rendered below the message, which on macOS shows an empty bordered list area — slightly redundant alongside the explicit empty-state text, but not a functional issue.
Errors Found: • [LOW/ux] Empty state shows the guidance text AND an empty bordered List, contradicting 'instead of a populated list' (Sources/Waves/Settings/SettingsView.swift:234) — The story expects the 'No presets yet…' message 'instead of a populated list.' In PresetSettingsView the empty-state Text is rendered (227-232) but the List (234-263) is still always rendered below it. When store.presets is empty, macOS draws an empty bordered list area beneath the guidance text, which is visually redundant and not what 'instead of a list' implies. | Fix: Wrap the List in `if !store.presets.isEmpty { ... }` (or use an else branch) so the empty bordered list is not drawn in the empty state.

Feature: Pin / unpin an app
User Story: As a Waves user, I want to pin (and unpin) an important app, so that I can mark it as a favorite I keep an eye on across all my running apps.
Expected Behavior:
• Right-clicking a mixer row shows a context-menu item labeled "Pin" when the app is not pinned and "Unpin" when it is (MixerRowView.swift:136).
• Selecting the item calls store.togglePinned(app), which computes willPin = !app.isPinned and calls backend.pinApp(willPin, appID:) then re-reads the snapshot (AppStore.swift:590-609).
• The real backend flips snapshot.apps[index].isPinned for the matching logicalID/id, throwing BackendError.appNotFound if no match (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:148-154).
• On success an info toast "Pinned"/"Unpinned" with the app name shows for ~1.2s; on failure an error toast "Pinning failed" with the error description shows (AppStore.swift:599-607).
• The same toggle is reachable via a VoiceOver accessibility action named "Pin"/"Unpin" (MixerRowView.swift:163-165) and via the "p" key on the selected row in the main window list (MainWindowView.swift:386, handleKey at 408-414).
• Pinning is independent of exclusion and audio control: togglePinned has no isExcluded guard, so even excluded apps can be pinned.
Acceptance Criteria:
• Right-clicking an unpinned app shows "Pin"; after invoking it the app's isPinned becomes true and the menu now shows "Unpin".
• Invoking the toggle on an app the backend cannot find surfaces a "Pinning failed" error toast and leaves isPinned unchanged.
• Pressing "p" with a row selected in the main window toggles that row's pinned state.
• A successful pin shows an info toast containing the app's display name.
• The VoiceOver action label matches the current state ("Pin" when unpinned, "Unpin" when pinned).
Impl Notes: Toggle is fully wired through store -> backend -> snapshot with toasts and three entry points (menu, a11y action, keyboard). Minor concern: togglePinned replaces the whole session with backend.currentSnapshot() (AppStore.swift:596) rather than merging a single app like setMuted/setVolumeBoost do; if an in-flight volume/mute apply hasn't merged yet this could momentarily clobber locally-set state, though pinning itself stays correct. Unlike volume/mute/boost/output, pinning has no isExcluded() guard, so an excluded app can still be pinned — harmless (pinning doesn't tap audio) but slightly inconsistent.
Errors Found: • [MEDIUM/concurrency] togglePinned replaces the whole session, transiently clobbering in-flight optimistic volume/mute state (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:596) — togglePinned does `session = await backend.currentSnapshot()` (AppStore.swift:596), replacing the entire local session, unlike setMuted/setVolumeBoost/setDesiredVolume/setOutputDevice which optimistically mutate only session.apps[index] then call mergeAppState(from:appID:) (AppStore.swift:1417) to merge a SINGLE app. Trigger: the user is dragging app A's slider (setDesiredVolume writes session.apps[A].desiredVolume locally + sets pendingVolumeTargets[A] at AppStore.swift:366-368; the backend apply is deferred until commitDesiredVolume->scheduleVolumeApply), OR app A's mute is mid-flight (setMuted set session.apps[A].isMuted locally at :497 but backend.setMuted at :523 has not returned). Before that backend write lands, the user pins ANY app via 'p' (MainWindowView.swift:386), the context menu, or the a11y action. The await in togglePinned reads a backend snapshot that does NOT yet contain A's new value, so A's optimistic volume/mute reverts in the UI. The durable state self-heals once the pending apply task runs mergeAppState (AppStore.swift:429), but the user sees a transient revert/flicker of A's slider or mute glyph. Verified: mergeAppState only overwrites the one matching app, whereas togglePinned overwrites the whole session. | Fix: Mirror the other mutators: after backend.pinApp succeeds, call mergeAppState(from: await backend.currentSnapshot(), appID: app.logicalID) instead of overwriting `session` wholesale; or set session.apps[index].isPinned optimistically before the await and merge only that app on completion.

Feature: Pinned indicator on the mixer row
User Story: As a user, I want a clear visual marker on pinned apps, so that I can tell at a glance which apps I have flagged as important.
Expected Behavior:
• A pinned app's row shows a small filled pin glyph ("pin.fill") next to its name in the full mixer row (MixerRowView.swift:22-27).
• The glyph carries an accessibility label "Pinned" so VoiceOver announces the pinned state (MixerRowView.swift:26).
• The pin glyph is not rendered when isPinned is false (the if app.isPinned block is skipped).
Acceptance Criteria:
• A row whose app.isPinned is true displays the pin.fill symbol beside the app name; an unpinned row does not.
• VoiceOver reports "Pinned" for the indicator on a pinned row.
• Toggling pin state via PIN-01 shows/hides the glyph accordingly.
Impl Notes: Correct and accessible in the full MixerRowView. Note the indicator is absent from CompactMixerRow (MixerRowView.swift:259-327), which is used by the menu-bar sections — but pinned apps there already live under an explicit "Pinned" header (PIN-04), so the missing glyph is reasonable rather than a bug.

Feature: Pinned filter scope in the main window
User Story: As a user, I want a dedicated "Pinned" section in the main window sidebar, so that I can focus on just my favorite apps and quickly find them.
Expected Behavior:
• The sidebar lists a "Pinned" source filter with a pin.fill icon and a live count subtitle like "2 pinned apps" (MainWindowView.swift:150-212, count via store.pinnedApps.count at 177).
• Selecting the Pinned filter scopes the list to store.pinnedApps, which is visibleApps.filter(\.isPinned) (MainWindowView.swift:106-107; AppStore.swift:109-111).
• store.pinnedApps respects the showSystemProcesses preference because it derives from visibleApps (AppStore.swift:103-111).
• When no apps are pinned, the empty state shows title "No Pinned Apps" and message "Pin apps from the source list to keep them here." (MainWindowView.swift:219, 236).
• A search query while in the Pinned scope searches across ALL visible apps, not just pinned ones, so a match in another scope is not hidden (MainWindowView.swift:115-129).
Acceptance Criteria:
• The sidebar always shows a Pinned row whose count equals the number of pinned visible apps.
• Selecting Pinned with at least one pinned app lists exactly the pinned apps.
• Selecting Pinned with zero pinned apps shows the "No Pinned Apps" empty state and its guidance message.
• Pinning a system process does not appear in the Pinned list unless showSystemProcesses is enabled.
• Typing a search term while in Pinned scope can surface non-pinned apps that match.
Impl Notes: Filter, count, icon, and empty state are all present and correct. UX nuance: while in the Pinned scope, an active search silently widens results to all visible apps (MainWindowView.swift:126), so the list can show unpinned apps even though the sidebar selection reads "Pinned" — intentional per the comment but potentially surprising.
Errors Found: • [LOW/ux] Active search in the Pinned scope shows unpinned apps while the sidebar still reads "Pinned" with a pinned-only count (Sources/Waves/Features/Mixer/MainWindowView.swift:177) — filteredApps (MainWindowView.swift:115-130) searches store.visibleApps regardless of the selected scope, so when the Pinned filter is selected and the user types a query, the list can show apps that are not pinned. The sidebar selection still highlights 'Pinned' and its subtitle still reads e.g. '2 pinned apps' because the sidebar count comes from filter.count(in:) -> store.pinnedApps.count (MainWindowView.swift:177), which ignores the search query. (Note: the in-pane OutputSummaryView header at MainWindowView.swift:334/447 DOES reflect the widened filtered count, so the contradiction is specifically between the sidebar label/count and the list, not the pane header — the first tester slightly conflated the two counts.) This is intentional per the inline comment and is encoded as PIN-03 acceptance criterion 5, but there is no on-screen cue that search has widened the scope beyond pinned apps. | Fix: When a search query is active and the result set exceeds the selected scope, show a subtle hint (e.g. set the OutputSummaryView subtitle to 'Searching all apps', or update the sidebar count to reflect the matched set) so the widened scope is discoverable.

Feature: Pinned section in the menu-bar panel
User Story: As a menu-bar user, I want my pinned apps grouped at the top of the quick panel, so that the apps I care about are immediately reachable without opening the main window.
Expected Behavior:
• The menu-bar panel renders a "Pinned" CompactSection above Live and Recent whenever store.pinnedApps is non-empty (MenuBarMixerView.swift:42-44).
• The Pinned section is hidden entirely when no apps are pinned (the if !store.pinnedApps.isEmpty guard).
• A global "No audio apps detected" empty state shows only when pinned, live, and recent are all empty (MenuBarMixerView.swift:52).
Acceptance Criteria:
• With at least one pinned app, the menu-bar panel shows a "Pinned" header listing those apps above the Live section.
• With no pinned apps, no "Pinned" header is rendered.
• The combined empty placeholder appears only when there are no pinned, live, or recent apps.
• Each pinned row in the panel exposes its volume slider, boost, and mute controls (CompactMixerRow).
Impl Notes: Section is correctly gated and ordered first. Concern: pinnedApps, liveApps, and recentApps are not mutually exclusive — liveApps/recentApps filter only on routing/level state and never exclude pinned apps (AppStore.swift:117-128). So a pinned app that is currently playing appears in BOTH the Pinned and Live sections of the menu-bar panel, duplicating the row.
Errors Found:
• [MEDIUM/inconsistency] Pinned apps are duplicated in the menu-bar panel — they also render under Live and/or Recent (Sources/Waves/Features/Mixer/MenuBarMixerView.swift:46) — MenuBarMixerView renders Pinned (MenuBarMixerView.swift:42-44), Live (46), and Recent (48-50) from store.pinnedApps, store.liveApps, and store.recentApps with no cross-section de-duplication. These collections are NOT mutually exclusive: liveApps (AppStore.swift:117-122) filters only on routingState/level and never excludes pinned apps; recentApps = visibleApps minus liveApps (AppStore.swift:124-128), again ignoring isPinned. So a pinned app that is playing renders in BOTH Pinned and Live; a pinned app that is not playing renders in BOTH Pinned and Recent (showRecentApps defaults to true, UserPreferences.swift:5). With default settings every pinned app appears twice, duplicating its CompactMixerRow controls. Confirmed against the source. | Fix: Exclude pinned logicalIDs from liveApps and recentApps when building the menu-bar sections (compute a partition: pinned first, then live-not-pinned, then recent-not-pinned-not-live), e.g. add a pinnedIDs Set and filter both.
• [LOW/accessibility] No way to pin or unpin an app from the menu-bar panel (CompactMixerRow has no pin affordance) (Sources/Waves/Features/Mixer/MixerRowView.swift:259) — The pin/unpin entry points all live in the full MixerRowView: the context-menu Button (MixerRowView.swift:135-138), the VoiceOver accessibilityAction (MixerRowView.swift:163-165), and the 'p' key handler in the main-window list (MainWindowView.swift:386). CompactMixerRow (MixerRowView.swift:259-327), which is the row used by every menu-bar section including the Pinned section, has NO context menu, NO accessibility action, and NO pin glyph (verified: grep shows contextMenu/accessibilityAction/togglePinned only in the MixerRowView range). A user who works primarily from the menu-bar panel therefore cannot pin a new favorite or unpin an existing one without opening the main window, and a VoiceOver user navigating the menu-bar panel has no Pin/Unpin action and no 'Pinned' indicator there. Arguably a deliberate scoping decision (main window is the management surface), hence low severity, but it is an undocumented discoverability/accessibility gap for the menu-bar-first user the PIN-04 story targets. | Fix: Add a context-menu Pin/Unpin item and a matching accessibilityAction to CompactMixerRow (reusing store.togglePinned), so pins can be created/removed and announced from the menu-bar panel.

Feature: Pin state persists across refresh, device changes, and relaunch
User Story: As a user, I want my pins to stick, so that I don't have to re-pin my favorite apps every time the app list refreshes or I restart Waves.
Expected Behavior:
• isPinned is a Codable field on AudioApp that round-trips through encode/decode (defaulting to false for older data) (AudioApp.swift:19, 134, 183).
• The session snapshot persists isPinned to disk via SessionStore.save (SessionStore.swift:73) and is reloaded on launch (AppStore.swift:83).
• On every backend snapshot rebuild, a rediscovered app inherits previous.isPinned from the prior snapshot keyed by logicalID (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1008).
• AppStore.mergedSession also carries cachedApp.isPinned forward when reconciling the warm cached session with a freshly built one at startup (AppStore.swift:1400).
• Pin state is keyed by logicalID, so it tracks an app across PID changes between launches.
Acceptance Criteria:
• Pinning an app, then refreshing the library, keeps the app pinned and still listed in the Pinned scope.
• Quitting and relaunching Waves restores previously pinned apps as pinned.
• A pinned app that briefly stops and restarts (same logicalID) remains pinned after the next snapshot.
• Loading a session file written by an older build without an isPinned key does not crash and treats apps as unpinned.
Impl Notes: Persistence is consistently threaded through the model, disk store, backend merge, and store merge — pins survive refresh and relaunch. One edge case: pin state lives only in the session snapshot, not in UserPreferences; if the cached session file is absent/corrupt and load() returns nil (AppStore.swift:83 falls back to emptySession), all pins are lost on that launch. Acceptable but worth noting as the single point of truth for pins.
Errors Found: • [LOW/edge-case] Pins are a single point of truth in session.json; a corrupt/missing session file silently drops all pins on launch (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:83) — Pin state is persisted only inside the session snapshot (SessionStore.save explicitly re-maps isPinned at SessionStore.swift:73) — it is not duplicated into UserPreferences. On launch the store does `self.session = sessionStore.load() ?? Self.emptySession` (AppStore.swift:83). SessionStore.load() (SessionStore.swift:30-48) returns nil on ANY failure — file absent, over the 10MB cap, unreadable, or a decode error — logging only a warning. When that happens the session falls back to emptySession, so every pin (and every other persisted per-app setting) is silently lost for that launch with no user-facing toast or recovery. The first tester did not list this; the feature-status implNotes call it out as 'acceptable' but it is a real persistence edge case worth flagging given pins are explicitly promised to 'stick' across relaunch (PIN-05 user story). Backward-compat for the missing-key case is fine (decodeIfPresent defaults isPinned to false, AudioApp.swift:134) — the risk is whole-file load failure, not field migration. | Fix: Either mirror pinned logicalIDs into UserPreferences (a tiny, more robust store) as a secondary source of truth, or surface a non-fatal toast when load() returns nil for a file that exists, so the user knows their saved state was dropped rather than silently losing pins.

Feature: Pinned apps sorted to the top of the mixer (intended)
User Story: As a user, I want pinned apps floated to the top of the mixer list, so that my favorites stay at the top regardless of the current sort mode.
Expected Behavior:
• DESIGN.md and the archived design doc describe pinning as floating "favorite apps to the top of the mixer" (designdoc.md:38, 78, 251).
• In the running/main list, ordering is produced solely by sortedApps, which switches on preferences.sortMode (name/activity/category/manual) and never references isPinned (AppStore.swift:1278-1337).
• Therefore pinning has no effect on the position of an app within the Running list or within any section; pinned apps are not floated to the top.
Acceptance Criteria:
• With sortMode = name, pinning an app whose name sorts last does NOT move it to the top of the Running list (documents current behavior — pinning is sort-neutral).
• No code path in sortedApps or its comparators inspects app.isPinned.
• The only positional effect of pinning is membership in the separate Pinned scope/section, not reordering within other lists.
Impl Notes: Intent gap: the design docs repeatedly state pins float favorites to the TOP of the mixer, but the implemented behavior only adds a dedicated Pinned filter/section and a glyph — sortedApps ignores isPinned, so pinned apps keep their normal sorted position in Running/Live/Recent. README's weaker phrasing ("keep them visible") is satisfied, but the "to the top" behavior from DESIGN.md/designdoc.md is not implemented. Either the docs overpromise or a pin-first tiebreak in sortedApps is missing.
Errors Found: • [LOW/inconsistency] Archived design doc promises pinned apps 'float to the top of the mixer'; sortedApps ignores isPinned, so they don't (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1278) — The archived design doc states pinning floats favorites to the top of the mixer ('pin favorite apps to the top of the mixer', designdoc.md:38; 'Pin favorite apps', :78), and PIN-06's user story repeats this want. But sortedApps (AppStore.swift:1278-1301) switches only on preferences.sortMode, and none of its comparators inspect isPinned — displayNameComparator (:1323), activityRank (:1303), and manualOrderComparator (:1327) all ignore it. So pinning has zero positional effect inside Running/Live/Recent; the only positional effect is membership in the separate Pinned scope/section plus the glyph. Confirmed by reading every comparator. Severity is low (not medium) because: (a) PIN-06 is explicitly marked implStatus 'missing' and its acceptanceCriteria are deliberately written to assert the CURRENT sort-neutral behavior, which the code satisfies; (b) the user-facing README and current DESIGN.md use the accurate weaker phrasing ('keep them visible' / lists pin as a component) — only the archived designdoc.md overpromises. So this is a docs-vs-code mismatch confined to an archived doc, with minimal user impact. | Fix: Pick one and make docs and code agree: either add a pin-first tiebreak in sortedApps (sort isPinned descending before applying the active comparator) to honor the design promise, or update the archived designdoc.md to match the shipped 'dedicated Pinned section, sort-neutral' behavior (the current DESIGN.md/README already do).

Feature: Drag-to-reorder rows in Running list
User Story: As a Waves user, I want to drag app rows up and down in the Running list, so that the apps I care about sit where I expect them in the mixer.
Expected Behavior:
• The detail List in the Running scope supports SwiftUI drag-to-reorder via an .onMove handler on the ForEach of MixerRowView rows (MainWindowView.swift:362-376).
• On a successful move, store.reorderApps(from:to:) is called with the source IndexSet and destination index (MainWindowView.swift:369-374).
• reorderApps maps the current visibleApps to their logicalIDs, applies Array.move(fromOffsets:toOffset:) (the standard collection move so downward drags land on the drop target, not one row below), and stores the result as preferences.customAppOrder (AppStore.swift:1345-1351).
• Because the move uses Array.move, a downward drag lands on the intended drop target rather than off-by-one (AppStore.swift:1346-1349).
• The new order is persisted immediately via persistPreferences() so it survives relaunch (AppStore.swift:1352).
Acceptance Criteria:
• In the Running scope with Manual sort and no active search, dragging a row to a new position leaves it at exactly the drop location.
• After dragging and relaunching the app, the customized order is preserved.
• Dragging a row downward past N rows lands it directly below the Nth row, not one position further.
• reorderApps writes the full set of visible logicalIDs into customAppOrder in the new order.
Impl Notes: Core drag path is correctly wired and persists. The off-by-one comment shows a known bug was already fixed by using Array.move. One latent concern: reorderApps rebuilds customAppOrder from visibleApps only (the currently visible, filtered set), so any apps hidden by showSystemProcesses==false are dropped from the saved order on every drag; they will re-collide at Int.max when later shown (see ORDER-04).
Errors Found:
• [HIGH/concurrency] reorderApps re-derives order from a freshly-read visibleApps instead of the snapshot the drop indices were computed against (stale-index trap) (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1345-1349) — The .onMove closure (MainWindowView.swift:369) receives source/destination indices that SwiftUI computed against the rendered `apps` array (filteredApps, which for filter==.running with no search equals store.visibleApps at render time). reorderApps ignores those rows and re-reads `visibleApps` freshly (AppStore.swift:1345), then calls Array.move(fromOffsets:toOffset:). In the common case the handler runs synchronously on the main actor immediately after the drop with no intervening await, so the rendered snapshot and the re-read snapshot match and there is no problem. The latent risk: session.apps is mutated by async work (refresh() AppStore.swift:242, backend snapshot merges, termination/frontmost observers). If SwiftUI's displayed list lags a session mutation that already shrank visibleApps, a source/destination offset can exceed the new array's bounds and Array.move traps (crash); if the set merely reordered, the wrong rows move. Confirmed as a real robustness gap, though the trigger window is narrow. | Fix: Pass the exact ordered identifier list the indices were computed against into reorderApps (e.g. reorderApps(visibleIDs: apps.map(\.logicalID), from:to:)) and apply move() to that array, or clamp/guard source and destination against visibleApps.count and bail if they no longer fit. Do not re-read visibleApps inside reorderApps.
• [MEDIUM/edge-case] Hidden system processes are stripped from customAppOrder on every drag (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1345) — visibleApps filters out system-category apps when preferences.showSystemProcesses == false (AppStore.swift:104-105) before sortedApps. reorderApps overwrites customAppOrder with `visibleApps.map { $0.logicalID }` (AppStore.swift:1345,1351), so any system process hidden at drag time is silently removed from the saved order. When the user later re-enables Show System Processes, those apps are absent from customAppOrder, get Int.max in manualOrderComparator, and collapse to the bottom, losing their previously-customized position. Verified against the filter at lines 104-105 and the overwrite at 1351. (Same root cause as ORDER-04 issue 2.) | Fix: Build the new order by merging the moved visible IDs back into the existing preferences.customAppOrder, preserving entries for currently-hidden apps in their relative positions, rather than overwriting customAppOrder with only the visible subset.

Feature: Manual sort mode gating of reorder
User Story: As a Waves user, I want my drag-to-reorder to take effect only when I have chosen Manual ordering, so that a manual drag does not silently override an automatic sort I selected.
Expected Behavior:
• The .onMove handler only forwards the move to the store when filter == .running AND preferences.sortMode == .manual AND searchText is empty (MainWindowView.swift:372-374).
• If sort mode is Name, Activity, or Category, the drag is accepted by the List UI but the handler does nothing, so the list re-renders back to its sorted order (MainWindowView.swift:372-375).
• Manual sort mode is selectable from Settings > General via the 'Sort apps by' Picker, which writes preferences.sortMode and persists it (SettingsView.swift:162-175).
• reorderApps itself defensively switches sortMode to .manual and persists if it is ever called in a non-manual mode (AppStore.swift:1340-1343).
Acceptance Criteria:
• With sort mode set to Name/Activity/Category, dragging a row produces no lasting reorder (the row snaps back).
• With sort mode set to Manual, dragging a row reorders and persists.
• Selecting Manual in the Settings picker is reflected immediately and persisted.
• Reorder never fires from any scope other than Running.
Impl Notes: Functionally correct but a real UX trap: the README advertises 'Drag-to-Reorder: Customize the order of your app list' with no mention that you must first switch Settings to Manual. In Name/Activity/Category mode the row is draggable (the system shows the drag affordance) but snaps back with no feedback, which reads as a broken feature. The defensive auto-switch-to-manual branch inside reorderApps (AppStore.swift:1340-1343) is effectively dead code because the only caller already requires manual mode before calling. There is also no in-window control to enter Manual mode or to start reordering — it is buried in Settings.
Errors Found:
• [MEDIUM/ux] Rows are draggable in Name/Activity/Category modes but silently snap back with no feedback; feature reads as broken (Sources/Waves/Features/Mixer/MainWindowView.swift:369-375) — The List always attaches .onMove (MainWindowView.swift:369), so macOS presents the drag affordance and accepts the gesture in every sort mode. When sortMode != .manual the handler's guard (line 372) is false and the closure does nothing, so the view re-renders back to the sorted order and the row snaps back with zero explanation. README.md:27 advertises 'Drag-to-Reorder: Customize the order of your app list' with no mention that the user must first switch Settings > General > Sort apps by to Manual. Confirmed: guard at line 372 requires sortMode == .manual; nothing suppresses the affordance otherwise. | Fix: Either (a) only attach .onMove when sortMode == .manual && filter == .running && the trimmed search is empty so the OS presents no reorder affordance otherwise, or (b) when a drag no-ops because the mode isn't Manual, show a toast/inline hint ('Switch Sort to Manual to reorder') and offer to auto-switch. Update README to state the Manual prerequisite.
• [LOW/dead-code] Defensive sortMode-switch branch in reorderApps is unreachable (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1340-1343) — reorderApps begins with `if preferences.sortMode != .manual { preferences.sortMode = .manual; persistPreferences() }` (AppStore.swift:1340-1343). The only caller is the .onMove closure (MainWindowView.swift:373), which is itself gated by `store.preferences.sortMode == .manual` (line 372). grep confirms no other production or test code calls reorderApps. Therefore the branch can never execute. | Fix: Remove the branch, or repurpose it: drop the sortMode==.manual condition from the .onMove guard and let reorderApps own the switch-to-manual, which would let a drag from a currently no-op non-manual mode intentionally engage Manual mode.
• [LOW/ux] No in-window affordance to enter Manual mode or start reordering (Sources/Waves/Settings/SettingsView.swift:162-175) — Manual sort is only selectable from Settings > General > 'Sort apps by' (SettingsView.swift:162-175). The main mixer window has no control to switch to Manual or any cue that the list is reorderable, so discovering drag-to-reorder requires opening Settings and knowing to choose Manual first. Confirmed: the only sortMode write site is the Settings picker; the toolbar/list header has no sort control. | Fix: Add a sort-mode control (menu/segmented control) to the main window toolbar or list header, with Manual as an option, and a subtle 'drag to reorder' hint when Manual is active.

Feature: Reorder disabled while searching or in non-Running scopes
User Story: As a Waves user, I want reordering to be disabled while I am filtering the list or viewing a non-Running scope, so that a partial/subset view can never scramble my real saved order.
Expected Behavior:
• When searchText is non-empty, the .onMove handler refuses to reorder, because a search shows a subset whose indices would not map back to the full list correctly (MainWindowView.swift:370-374).
• Reorder is only honored in the Running scope; Pinned, Live, and Recent scopes (which are filtered derivations of visibleApps) provide no working reorder because the guard requires filter == .running (MainWindowView.swift:372).
• The menu-bar mixer (MenuBarMixerView) renders rows with ForEach but defines no .onMove, so it offers no drag-reorder at all (MenuBarMixerView.swift:190; no onMove present).
Acceptance Criteria:
• With a non-empty search query active, dragging a filtered row does not change customAppOrder.
• Dragging within Pinned, Live, or Recent scopes does not persist a reorder.
• The menu-bar popover offers no drag-to-reorder.
Impl Notes: The guard correctly protects against the subset-index-mapping corruption that a filtered drag would cause, which is the right call. Edge case worth noting: the List still presents a drag handle / accepts the gesture in these disabled situations and silently no-ops, so the user gets no explanation for why the row snapped back. Pinned/Recent/Live rows do reflect the manually-saved order (because they sort through visibleApps) but cannot themselves be reordered.
Errors Found:
• [LOW/inconsistency] Whitespace-only search query blocks reorder even though the list shows the full unfiltered set (Sources/Waves/Features/Mixer/MainWindowView.swift:372) — The .onMove guard checks the raw, un-trimmed `searchText.isEmpty` (MainWindowView.swift:372). But filteredApps trims the query and treats a whitespace-only query as no filter, returning the full scopedApps (MainWindowView.swift:116-117). So if searchText is e.g. two spaces, the list shows the complete unfiltered Running set, yet the guard's `searchText.isEmpty` is false and reorder is refused; the row snaps back with no explanation despite no actual filtering. Confirmed against the trim at line 116 vs the raw check at line 372. | Fix: Guard on the same trimmed value filteredApps uses: `searchText.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines).isEmpty`, or pass a precomputed isFiltering flag down so the guard and the filter share one source of truth.
• [LOW/ux] Dragging in a disabled state (search active, or Pinned/Live/Recent scope) silently no-ops with no explanation (Sources/Waves/Features/Mixer/MainWindowView.swift:369-375) — In Pinned/Live(frontmost)/Recent scopes and while a search is active, the List still attaches .onMove (MainWindowView.swift:369) and presents the drag affordance, but the guard (line 372 requires filter == .running and empty search) makes the handler do nothing. The row snaps back and the user gets no indication of why. Confirmed: scopedApps returns the subset for non-running scopes (MainWindowView.swift:102-113) and the guard blocks them; no corruption occurs because the move is never forwarded. | Fix: Suppress the .onMove affordance entirely when not in the reorderable state (running + manual + no trimmed search) so these scopes/searches present no drag handle.

Feature: Manual order persistence, comparator fallback, and new/stale apps
User Story: As a Waves user, I want my manual order to be remembered across launches and to behave sensibly when apps appear, quit, or are unknown to the saved order, so that customizing the list is durable and predictable.
Expected Behavior:
• sortedApps applies manualOrderComparator when sortMode == .manual, ordering each app by its index in preferences.customAppOrder (AppStore.swift:1298-1300, 1327-1337).
• Apps whose logicalID is absent from customAppOrder are assigned Int.max and therefore sink to the bottom of the manual list (AppStore.swift:1330-1332).
• Apps that tie (e.g. multiple unknown apps all at Int.max) are broken by raw displayName comparison app1.displayName < app2.displayName (AppStore.swift:1335).
• On startup, if sortMode was persisted as .manual but customAppOrder is empty, the store falls back to .name so the user is not left with an empty/degenerate manual order (AppStore.swift:88-90).
• customAppOrder and sortMode are decoded field-by-field with defaults so an older preferences file missing these keys still loads cleanly (UserPreferences.swift:52-53, 40-61).
Acceptance Criteria:
• A manually ordered list reproduces the same order after relaunch.
• A newly launched app not present in customAppOrder appears at the bottom of the manual list rather than at the top or in a random spot.
• Two apps both missing from customAppOrder are ordered alphabetically relative to each other.
• Persisted sortMode==.manual with an empty customAppOrder resolves to Name sort on next launch (no blank/degenerate ordering).
Impl Notes: Persistence and the empty-order fallback are solid. Gaps: (1) customAppOrder is never pruned — quit apps' logicalIDs accumulate in the array indefinitely (unbounded slow growth, and a quit-then-relaunched app keeps its old slot, which may surprise the user). (2) reorderApps rebuilds customAppOrder from visibleApps only, so any system processes hidden at drag time are stripped from the saved order and then collapse to Int.max next time they are shown. (3) The tie-break at AppStore.swift:1335 uses raw '<' on displayName instead of the localizedCaseInsensitiveCompare used everywhere else (displayNameComparator), so unknown/new apps sort case-sensitively and inconsistently with the rest of the app. (4) No UI affordance resets manual order back to a clean state; the only escape is switching sort mode away from Manual.
Errors Found:
• [MEDIUM/inconsistency] Manual-order tie-break uses case-sensitive raw '<' on displayName instead of localizedCaseInsensitiveCompare (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1335) — manualOrderComparator breaks ties (apps both absent from customAppOrder, both Int.max) with `app1.displayName < app2.displayName` (AppStore.swift:1335), a raw lexicographic, case-sensitive, non-locale-aware comparison. Every other sort path uses displayNameComparator (localizedCaseInsensitiveCompare, AppStore.swift:1323-1324). New/unknown apps in Manual mode therefore sort case-sensitively (e.g. 'Zoom' before 'audacity' because 'Z'(0x5A) < 'a'(0x61)), inconsistent with Name mode. Visible immediately when Manual is selected with an empty customAppOrder, since every app is then a tie. Confirmed by direct comparison of lines 1335 vs 1323-1324. | Fix: Replace `return app1.displayName < app2.displayName` with `return displayNameComparator(app1, app2)`.
• [MEDIUM/edge-case] System processes hidden at drag time are stripped from the persisted manual order (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1345) — reorderApps rebuilds customAppOrder solely from visibleApps, which excludes system apps when showSystemProcesses==false (AppStore.swift:104-105, 1345, 1351). Reordering while system processes are hidden discards their saved positions. On re-enabling Show System Processes those apps are no longer in customAppOrder, get Int.max in manualOrderComparator (AppStore.swift:1330-1331), and sink to the bottom. Confirmed (same root cause as ORDER-01 issue 2). | Fix: Merge the moved visible IDs back into the existing customAppOrder, preserving entries for apps not currently in visibleApps, instead of overwriting with the filtered subset.
• [LOW/ux] No way to reset/clear a manual order (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1339-1354) — customAppOrder is only ever written by reorderApps (AppStore.swift:1351) or flipped to .name on startup when empty (AppStore.swift:88-90). There is no UI action to clear customAppOrder or reset Manual ordering. The only escape is switching Sort to a non-manual mode, which leaves the stale customAppOrder in place to reappear if Manual is chosen again. Confirmed: no reset/clear write site exists. | Fix: Add a 'Reset manual order' action (sets preferences.customAppOrder = [] and persists; per the startup rule it then falls back to Name) near the sort-mode picker or in the mixer window.
• [LOW/edge-case] customAppOrder is never pruned; quit apps retain slots and a relaunched app re-seizes its old position (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1327-1337) — customAppOrder is rebuilt only on a drag (AppStore.swift:1351); there is no prune on session refresh. Between drags, a logicalID of a quit app remains in customAppOrder and grows unbounded over time. If that app relaunches before the next reorder, manualOrderComparator (AppStore.swift:1330-1334) finds its old index and restores it to its former slot rather than treating it as new. Confirmed: no pruning call site against the live app set exists. | Fix: Prune customAppOrder against the current known app set when the session refreshes (or deliberately document the keep-slot policy). At minimum drop logicalIDs not seen for some retention window so the array does not grow indefinitely.
• [LOW/accessibility] No keyboard or VoiceOver path to reorder, contradicting the list's 'fully drivable without a mouse' claim (Sources/Waves/Features/Mixer/MainWindowView.swift:369-386) — The list's keyboard handlers cover mute/volume/boost/pin (MainWindowView.swift:381-386) and a comment asserts the mixer is 'fully drivable without a mouse' (MainWindowView.swift:378-380). However reorder is exposed only via SwiftUI .onMove drag (line 369); on macOS this provides no built-in keyboard or VoiceOver reordering action, and there is no key handler to move the selected row up/down, nor an .accessibilityAction. Confirmed: only the six onKeyPress handlers at 381-386 exist; none move a row. | Fix: Add keyboard handlers (e.g. Option+Up/Down on the selected row) that call store.reorderApps with the selected row's index, gated on Manual+Running+no-search, and expose an .accessibilityAction so VoiceOver users can move a row.
• [LOW/edge-case] Selecting Manual in Settings without ever dragging silently reverts to Name on next launch, defeating the chosen ordering (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:88-90) — A user can pick Manual in Settings > General (SettingsView.swift:162-175), which persists sortMode==.manual but leaves customAppOrder empty. On the next launch, init() downgrades sortMode to .name because customAppOrder is empty (AppStore.swift:88-90). So a user who selects Manual (intending a stable name-based baseline to then reorder) but closes/relaunches before dragging finds the mode silently reset to Name with no notice. This is a persistence edge case specific to the manual-order feature that the first tester did not capture in this area. | Fix: Either keep Manual selected and render a stable fallback ordering (e.g. name order) when customAppOrder is empty instead of downgrading the mode, or surface a hint in Settings that Manual takes effect only after the first reorder.

Feature: Choose sort mode
User Story: As a Waves user with many audio apps, I want to choose how the app list is ordered (Activity, Name, Category, or Manual), so that I can find and control the app I care about quickly.
Expected Behavior:
• Settings > General exposes a 'Sort apps by' Picker listing all four SortMode cases via SortMode.allCases, labeled by displayName: Activity, Name, Category, Manual (SettingsView.swift:162-175, UserPreferences.swift:116-136).
• Selecting a mode writes store.preferences.sortMode and immediately calls store.persistPreferences() so the choice survives relaunch (SettingsView.swift:166-169).
• visibleApps applies the chosen mode through sortedApps(_:) every time the list is read, so the mixer list, pinned/live/recent derived lists, and menu-bar sections all reflect the active sort (AppStore.swift:103-128, 1278-1301).
• Default sort mode is .name for a fresh install (UserPreferences.swift:7).
• On launch, if sortMode is .manual but customAppOrder is empty, the mode is downgraded to .name so manual sort never produces an undefined order (AppStore.swift:85-90).
Acceptance Criteria:
• The Settings picker shows exactly four options (Activity, Name, Category, Manual).
• Changing the picker reorders the mixer list without a relaunch.
• Relaunching the app preserves the previously selected sort mode (except the manual-with-empty-order fallback to Name).
• A fresh install defaults to Name sort.
• Launching with sortMode=manual and an empty customAppOrder results in Name sort.
Impl Notes: Fully wired and persisted. UX gap: the sort-mode control lives only in Settings — there is no in-window/menu-bar affordance to change or even see the current sort, so users may not discover it. The picker write happens outside any AppStore mutation method (direct preferences write + manual persistPreferences call), which works but bypasses any validation/side-effects a setter could centralize.
Errors Found: • [LOW/ux] Sort mode is only changeable/visible inside Settings — no in-window or menu-bar affordance or current-mode indicator (Sources/Waves/Settings/SettingsView.swift:162-175) — Re-verified: the only sort control is the 'Sort apps by' Picker in Settings > General (SettingsView.swift:162-175). The main mixer window (MainWindowView.swift) and the menu-bar panel (MenuBarMixerView.swift) neither expose the control nor display which sort is active, and there is no VoiceOver announcement of sort state. A user whose list silently flips to Manual on a first drag (reorderApps auto-switch, AppStore.swift:1340-1343) has no on-list indication of why ordering changed and no quick way to switch back. feature-status.json implNotes explicitly call this a 'UX gap'. Acceptance criteria do not mandate an in-window control, so this is a discoverability/UX observation, not a hard failure. | Fix: Add a small sort menu/segmented control to the mixer toolbar (and optionally the menu-bar header) bound to store.preferences.sortMode, showing the active mode, so users can see and change sort without opening Settings.

Feature: Activity sort (most-active apps first)
User Story: As a user actively mixing audio, I want apps that are currently making sound to rise to the top, so that the apps I am most likely to adjust are always within reach.
Expected Behavior:
• Activity sort ranks each app into five tiers via activityRank: live route (0), managed+unmuted+audible peak/rms>0.001 (1), frontmost/isActive (2), managed but not audible (3), everything else (4) (AppStore.swift:1303-1321).
• Apps within the same activity rank are tie-broken alphabetically by displayName using localizedCaseInsensitiveCompare (AppStore.swift:1283-1290, 1323-1325).
• Activity sort needs no stored order and is preserved across launches unlike manual (AppStore.swift:85-90 comment).
• Ranking reacts to live level metering — peakLevel/rmsLevel updates from the level poller can change an app's tier while sound plays (AppStore.swift:1308, level metering at 611-619).
Acceptance Criteria:
• An app with routingState .live appears above an idle app of any name.
• Two apps in the same tier appear in case-insensitive alphabetical order.
• A managed app that is muted or silent ranks below an audible managed app.
• Switching to Activity sort requires no saved custom order and works on first use.
Impl Notes: Logic is coherent and mirrors the liveApps/activityRank definitions. Correctness nuance: ordering depends on peakLevel/rmsLevel in the session snapshot, which is only refreshed while a mixer surface is visible and the level poller runs; when polling is stopped the audible tier (rank 1) may go stale and apps can appear mis-ranked until the next refresh. Re-sorting on every level tick could also cause rows to visibly jump during playback (potential UX churn), though SwiftUI list diffing softens this.
Errors Found:
• [HIGH/correctness] Activity sort does not react to live metering — audible (rank 1) and frontmost (rank 2) tiers are stale between manual refreshes (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1303-1321) — Re-verified in code. activityRank reads app.peakLevel/app.rmsLevel and app.isActive from session.apps (AppStore.swift:1303-1321). Those fields are only written by a full backend.currentSnapshot()/refresh() (computed at WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1177-1189 for isActive), which runs only on explicit user actions (⌘R, Refresh, onboarding) — there is NO periodic session-refresh timer anywhere in AppStore (the only repeating loop is the level poller at AppStore.swift:619-628). The poller writes the SEPARATE store.liveLevels dictionary via backend.audioLevels(); it never updates session.apps peak/rms. The frontmost observer (AppStore.swift:886-897) only calls checkAutoPauseMusic() and never sets session.apps[].isActive. Therefore an app that starts/stops producing sound, or comes to the foreground, does NOT change its activity tier until the next manual refresh, directly contradicting an explicit expectedBehavior bullet. | Fix: Either (a) merge poller results into session.apps[].peakLevel/rmsLevel and refresh isActive on NSWorkspace.didActivateApplicationNotification, or (b) have activityRank consult store.liveLevels for the audible-tier test (e.g. max(liveLevels[id].peak, liveLevels[id].rms) > 0.001).
• [LOW/inconsistency] liveApps/recentApps audibility test shares the same stale-level flaw (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:117-122) — Re-verified. liveApps (AppStore.swift:117-122) classifies a managed app as live when max(app.peakLevel, app.rmsLevel) > 0.001 using the static session levels, and recentApps is the complement (AppStore.swift:124-128). Because session peak/rms are not refreshed by the poller, the Live and Recent scope tabs and the menu-bar Live/Recent sections only reflect audibility captured at the last full refresh, so an app that goes silent stays in 'Live' until a manual refresh. Severity low because it is the same root cause as the rank-1 staleness above and is cosmetic for the derived lists. | Fix: Use the same live-levels source for the liveApps/recentApps audibility predicate as proposed for activityRank, so all activity-derived lists agree with the meters.

Feature: Name and Category sort
User Story: As a user scanning a long app list, I want to sort alphabetically by name or grouped by category, so that I can locate a specific app predictably.
Expected Behavior:
• Name sort orders all visible apps by displayName using localizedCaseInsensitiveCompare ascending (AppStore.swift:1280-1281, 1323-1325).
• Category sort groups apps by AppCategory.rawValue ascending, then tie-breaks within a category by displayName (AppStore.swift:1291-1297).
• Categories available: browser, conferencing, media, communication, system, unknown (AudioApp.swift:223-247).
• Both sorts are stable across launches and require no stored order.
Acceptance Criteria:
• Name sort lists apps A→Z ignoring case.
• Category sort keeps apps of the same category contiguous, alphabetized within the group.
• Apps with category .unknown sort together as a group.
• Neither mode depends on customAppOrder.
Impl Notes: Works, but category ordering is by enum rawValue string, not by the human-facing displayName. The resulting group order is browser, communication, conferencing, media, system, unknown (alphabetical by raw value), which is NOT the same as alphabetical by the displayed labels (e.g. 'Communication' vs 'Conferencing' happen to align, but the coupling to rawValue is fragile and there are no visible section headers delineating the groups in the main List, so the grouping reads as a plain sorted list to the user).
Errors Found: • [LOW/inconsistency] Stated category group order in the story does not match the code's rawValue ordering (Communication/Conferencing swapped) (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1291-1297) — Re-verified. Category sort orders by AppCategory.rawValue string ascending (AppStore.swift:1291-1297). Enum cases are declared browser, conferencing, media, communication, system, unknown (AudioApp.swift:222-229), but sorting by the STRING rawValue yields alphabetical order: browser, communication, conferencing, media, system, unknown. The story's expectedBehavior asserts 'browser, conferencing, media, communication, system, unknown', which the code does not produce — communication and conferencing are swapped. All acceptance criteria (contiguity, within-group alpha, unknown grouped, no customAppOrder dependency) are still satisfied, so this is a doc/code prose mismatch, not a functional break. | Fix: Decide the intended group order and make it explicit (sort by a defined category ordinal or by category.displayName, ideally with visible section headers), then update the story to match. Without headers, Category sort reads as a plain sorted list to the user.

Feature: Manual drag-to-reorder
User Story: As a power user, I want to drag apps into a custom order and have it stick, so that my most-used apps stay exactly where I put them.
Expected Behavior:
• Dragging a row triggers List.onMove, which calls store.reorderApps(from:to:) only when the scope filter is .running, sortMode is .manual, and the search field is empty (MainWindowView.swift:369-375).
• reorderApps auto-switches sortMode to .manual (and persists) if it was not already, so a first drag converts the list to manual ordering (AppStore.swift:1339-1343).
• The new order is computed by Array.move(fromOffsets:toOffset:) over visibleApps logicalIDs, fixing the off-by-one that a naive remove/insert would cause on downward drags (AppStore.swift:1345-1349).
• customAppOrder (an array of logicalIDs) is saved and persisted after each move (AppStore.swift:1351-1352).
• Manual sort orders apps by their index in customAppOrder; apps not present in the saved order sink to the bottom (firstIndex ?? Int.max) and tie-break by raw displayName comparison (AppStore.swift:1327-1337).
• Drag-to-reorder is documented as a feature in README (README.md:27).
Acceptance Criteria:
• Dragging a row while in Running scope with no search reorders the list and the order persists across relaunch.
• Performing a drag while in Name/Activity/Category mode switches the app into Manual mode.
• A downward drag drops the row on the target position, not one below it.
• Reordering is a no-op while a search query is active or while in a non-Running scope.
• A newly launched app not in customAppOrder appears at the end of the manual list.
Impl Notes: Core reorder works and the off-by-one is handled. Two real gaps: (1) reorderApps rebuilds customAppOrder from visibleApps only — when showSystemProcesses is off, system apps are excluded from visibleApps, so every manual reorder silently drops their logicalIDs from the saved order; if the user later enables system processes those apps fall to the bottom regardless of any prior manual placement. (2) Manual mode's final tie-break uses raw `app1.displayName < app2.displayName` (case-sensitive, non-localized), inconsistent with the localizedCaseInsensitiveCompare used everywhere else, so unsaved/new apps with mixed-case names can order unexpectedly. Also, onMove can only be invoked in Manual mode + Running scope + empty search, so there is no way to drag-reorder while filtered/searching — acceptable but worth noting.
Errors Found:
• [HIGH/edge-case] Manual reorder silently drops system-app IDs from customAppOrder when system processes are hidden (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1345-1352) — Re-verified. reorderApps rebuilds customAppOrder from visibleApps only (AppStore.swift:1345: 'var apps = visibleApps.map { $0.logicalID }'). visibleApps excludes system-category apps whenever preferences.showSystemProcesses is false (AppStore.swift:104-106), which is the default (UserPreferences.swift:6). So any single drag while system apps are hidden overwrites the persisted customAppOrder with a list that omits every system app's logicalID. If the user later enables 'Show system processes', those apps are no longer in customAppOrder, so manualOrderComparator gives them firstIndex == Int.max (AppStore.swift:1330-1334) and they all sink to the bottom, permanently losing any earlier manual placement. This is a real persistence/data-loss bug. | Fix: Merge rather than replace: splice the reordered visible IDs back into the full saved customAppOrder, preserving entries for IDs not currently in visibleApps (hidden/system) at their relative positions.
• [HIGH/correctness] First drag in a non-Manual mode moves the wrong row — reorderApps re-sorts visibleApps to the manual fallback BEFORE applying the move (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1339-1352) — MISSED BY FIRST TESTER. reorderApps sets preferences.sortMode = .manual at line 1340-1343, and only THEN reads visibleApps at line 1345. Because visibleApps is a live computed property with no cache (invalidateVisibleAppsCache is a no-op, AppStore.swift:134-137), it immediately re-sorts using manualOrderComparator. On a first drag customAppOrder is empty, so every app ties at Int.max and the fallback order is raw case-sensitive displayName ascending (AppStore.swift:1335) — which generally differs from the Activity/Category/Name order the user was actually looking at on screen. The source/destination IndexSet came from the displayed (old-sort) list, so move(fromOffsets:toOffset:) is applied to a DIFFERENTLY ordered array and relocates the wrong app. The off-by-one fix is irrelevant here; the indices themselves point at the wrong elements. | Fix: Capture the displayed order first: snapshot let ids = visibleApps.map(\.logicalID) BEFORE changing sortMode, apply move() to that snapshot, then set sortMode=.manual and customAppOrder=ids and persist once.
• [LOW/inconsistency] Manual-mode tie-break uses case-sensitive non-localized comparison, unlike every other sort (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1335) — Re-verified. manualOrderComparator's final tie-break is 'return app1.displayName < app2.displayName' (AppStore.swift:1335), a raw String comparison that is case-sensitive and non-localized. Every other sort path (name, activity, category) tie-breaks via displayNameComparator with localizedCaseInsensitiveCompare (AppStore.swift:1281, 1289, 1296, 1323-1325). Apps absent from customAppOrder (newly launched) all tie at Int.max and are ordered by this inconsistent comparator, so e.g. 'Zoom' sorts before 'discord' (uppercase < lowercase in ASCII). | Fix: Replace the tie-break with 'return displayNameComparator(app1, app2)' to match the other sort modes.
• [LOW/ux] onMove is attached unconditionally, so rows show a drag affordance and snap back outside Manual/Running/empty-search (Sources/Waves/Features/Mixer/MainWindowView.swift:369-375) — Re-verified. The List installs .onMove on the ForEach unconditionally (MainWindowView.swift:369-375) with no .moveDisabled guard; only the closure body calls store.reorderApps, and only when filter == .running && sortMode == .manual && searchText.isEmpty. In Name/Activity/Category mode, in a non-Running scope, or while searching, the OS still presents the row as draggable, animates the drag, then snaps it back with no explanation — a dead/broken-feeling control. | Fix: Gate the affordance, not just the action: attach .onMove (or .moveDisabled(false)) only when filter == .running && sortMode == .manual && searchText.isEmpty; otherwise suppress the drag, or surface a hint that dragging requires Manual sort in the Running scope.
Fix Notes: tier1: FU-REORDER-STALE-SNAPSHOT | tier2: FU-ONMOVE-AFFORDANCE-GATING | tier3: FU-MANUAL-TIEBREAK-LOCALIZED

Feature: Sort interaction with pinned apps and scoped/searched lists
User Story: As a user who pins important apps and filters by scope, I want sorting to behave consistently across pinned, live, recent, and search results, so that ordering never feels arbitrary.
Expected Behavior:
• pinnedApps, activeApps, liveApps, and recentApps are all derived by filtering the already-sorted visibleApps, so every scope inherits the active sort order (AppStore.swift:109-128).
• Search filters across all visibleApps (not just the current scope) and preserves the active sort order, matching on displayName or category displayName (MainWindowView.swift:115-130).
• The menu-bar panel renders Pinned, Live, and Recent sections, each drawing from the sorted derived lists (MenuBarMixerView.swift:42-52).
Acceptance Criteria:
• Within the Pinned scope, apps follow the same sort mode as the Running scope.
• A search result list stays ordered per the active sort mode.
• Pinning/unpinning an app does not change the sort order of the surrounding list (pin only changes scope membership).
Impl Notes: Sorting is consistently inherited by all scopes/search, which is good. However, pinned apps do NOT float to the top of the main Running list — sortedApps never consults isPinned, so a pinned app sits wherever its name/activity/category/manual rank places it. Users may expect pins to surface to the top in the unified list (they only get their own section in the menu bar and the Pinned scope tab). This is a defensible design choice but is an easy point of confusion and is not surfaced anywhere in the UI.
Errors Found: • [LOW/ux] Menu-bar sections can show the same app twice (Pinned + Live, or Pinned + Recent) (Sources/Waves/Features/Mixer/MenuBarMixerView.swift:42-50) — Re-verified. MenuBarMixerView renders Pinned (store.pinnedApps), Live (store.liveApps), and Recent (store.recentApps) as independent CompactSections (MenuBarMixerView.swift:42-50), and CompactSection (line 177-204) just renders whatever apps it is handed with no cross-section dedup. pinnedApps is visibleApps filtered by isPinned (AppStore.swift:109-111) with no exclusion of live/recent; recentApps is visibleApps minus liveIDs (AppStore.swift:124-128) with no exclusion of pinned. So a pinned audible app appears in both Pinned and Live; a pinned idle app appears in both Pinned and Recent — two separate row instances controlling the same app. | Fix: De-duplicate across sections (e.g. exclude pinned apps from Live/Recent, or exclude live apps from Recent and pinned-but-live from Pinned), or label that sections may overlap.

Feature: Real-time per-app level metering on the row
User Story: As someone running several audio apps, I want each managed app's row to show a live cyan meter that moves with its actual output, so that I can tell at a glance which app is making sound and how loud it is.
Expected Behavior:
• A cyan capsule meter is overlaid at the bottom-leading edge of each MixerRowView, sized to proxy.size.width * meterLevel at a fixed 2pt height, drawn as a non-layout-shifting overlay (MixerRowView.swift:122-134).
• meterLevel reads store.liveLevels[app.logicalID] and returns min(1, max(rms, peak*0.7)); apps with no entry render 0 (MixerRowView.swift:177-180).
• Levels originate on the realtime IO render thread: renderTappedAudio computes peak/RMS from the original (pre-scale) input buffer via TapDSP.levels and stores them with writeLevels (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1924-1937).
• TapDSP.levels normalizes float32/int16/int32 samples to [0,1] returning (peak, sum, sampleCount); RMS is sqrt(sum/sampleCount) (TapDSP.swift:56-103).
• The backend's levelUpdateTask copies each active controller's getCurrentLevels() into the snapshot every 250ms (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1371-1403).
• audioLevels() returns AudioLevels(peak,rms) keyed by logicalID for apps in .managed or .live routing state (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1096-1102).
• The meter animates with a 0.18s linear animation, disabled under Reduce Motion (MixerRowView.swift:129).
Acceptance Criteria:
• When a managed app is actively playing audio, its row shows a non-zero cyan meter whose width tracks the audio level within ~0.3-0.5s.
• A silent managed app shows a zero-width (invisible) meter.
• TapDSP.levels of pure silence yields peak 0 and rms 0; full-scale [1,-1,1,-1] float input yields peak 1.0 and rms 1.0 (covered by TapDSPTests).
• The meter overlay never changes the row's layout/height regardless of level.
• With Reduce Motion enabled, the meter updates without animation.
Impl Notes: End-to-end path is real and wired: realtime DSP -> per-controller box -> 250ms snapshot copy -> 300ms store poll -> SwiftUI overlay. DSP math is unit-tested. Effective UI refresh latency is the sum of two un-phase-aligned timers (250ms backend + 300ms store), so worst-case meter lag is ~550ms and the visual cadence is coarse (~3 fps) for a 'real-time' meter — fine for an activity indicator, not a true VU meter. meterLevel blends rms with peak*0.7 (a smoothing choice, not standard peak metering); there is no peak-hold or decay envelope, so the bar can look jumpy/steppy at the poll rate.
Errors Found:
• [MEDIUM/inconsistency] Menu-bar mixer rows show no per-app level meter at all (only the main window does) (Sources/Waves/Features/Mixer/MixerRowView.swift:259-327) — The story's core promise is that 'each managed app's row shows a live cyan meter' and the AC says 'When a managed app is actively playing audio, its row shows a non-zero cyan meter.' The main-window MixerRowView renders the Capsule meter overlay reading store.liveLevels (MixerRowView.swift:122-134, 177-180), but the menu-bar surface uses CompactMixerRow (MenuBarMixerView.swift:191 -> MixerRowView.swift:259-327), which has NO meter overlay, no meterLevel, no showsLevelMeter, and no liveLevels read anywhere in MenuBarMixerView. A user living in the menu bar gets only a RoutingStateDot, never the moving cyan meter. Worse, MenuBarMixerView.onAppear calls beginLiveLevels() (MenuBarMixerView.swift:113), so when only the menu bar is open the 300ms poll runs and populates store.liveLevels with nothing on screen consuming it -- pure overhead that also undercuts LVL-02's near-zero-idle-CPU goal. This is a state desync between the menu-bar and main-window surfaces the first tester missed. | Fix: Render the same meter overlay (or an equivalent compact level indicator) in CompactMixerRow using store.liveLevels[app.logicalID], so the menu-bar surface honors the per-row meter feature; otherwise gate beginLiveLevels() to surfaces that actually draw meters so the menu-bar-only case does not poll needlessly.
• [LOW/inconsistency] Worst-case meter latency (~550ms) exceeds the story's ~0.3-0.5s tracking window (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:621) — End-to-end refresh is the sum of two un-phase-aligned timers: the backend levelUpdateTask sleeps 250ms (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1375) before copying controller levels into the snapshot, and the store poll sleeps 300ms (AppStore.swift:621) before reading audioLevels(). When a store poll fires just before a backend update lands, the displayed level is one full backend cycle stale, so worst-case visible lag is ~250+300 = ~550ms, beyond the ~0.3-0.5s acceptance bound. The bar also refreshes at only ~3fps (steps rather than glides) and there is no peak-hold/decay. Re-read of both timers confirms the math; the feature-status implNotes already acknowledge the ~550ms figure. | Fix: Drive the store poll from the backend's update cadence (e.g. an AsyncStream yielded on each updateAudioLevels), or shorten/align the two intervals (backend 100-150ms, store 100ms) so combined latency stays under 500ms.
