Add light peak-hold/decay so the bar reads smoothly between samples.

Feature: Visibility-gated, reference-counted level polling
User Story: As a user who keeps Waves running in the menu bar all day, I want level polling to run only while a mixer surface is actually on screen, so that the app uses near-zero CPU when I'm not looking at it.
Expected Behavior:
• beginLiveLevels() increments liveLevelsRefcount and starts a single poll Task only if none exists; multiple visible surfaces share one poller (AppStore.swift:616-630).
• The poll loops every 300ms, awaits backend.audioLevels(), and assigns store.liveLevels only when the value changed and the task isn't cancelled (AppStore.swift:619-628).
• endLiveLevels() decrements the refcount, and when it reaches 0 cancels the task, nils it, and clears liveLevels to [:] (AppStore.swift:632-637).
• Both MainWindowView and MenuBarMixerView call beginLiveLevels() onAppear and endLiveLevels() onDisappear (MainWindowView.swift:98-99, MenuBarMixerView.swift:113-114).
• Assignments are skipped when levels == self.liveLevels to avoid needless SwiftUI redraws (AppStore.swift:626).
Acceptance Criteria:
• Opening either the main window or the menu-bar panel starts exactly one poll task; opening the second surface does not start a second task.
• Closing all mixer surfaces stops the poll task and resets store.liveLevels to empty, hiding all meters.
• While no surface is visible, no audioLevels() polling occurs.
• Re-opening a surface after full teardown restarts polling cleanly.
• An unchanged levels snapshot does not trigger a store mutation/redraw.
Impl Notes: Refcounting and teardown are correct and the dual-surface sharing works. Note the gate only stops the *store* poll; the backend's 250ms levelUpdateTask keeps running unconditionally whenever any controllers exist (started in init, WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:33/1371), so the realtime metering work continues even with no UI visible — the CPU win is partial, not total. Also endLiveLevels clamps the refcount at 0, so an unbalanced extra endLiveLevels() call is harmless but a mismatched begin/end (e.g. onAppear without onDisappear) could leave the poller running.
Errors Found: • [LOW/edge-case] Unbalanced onAppear/onDisappear can leave the poll task running with no visible surface (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:632-637) — beginLiveLevels()/endLiveLevels() balance only if every onAppear is paired with exactly one onDisappear (MainWindowView.swift:98-99, MenuBarMixerView.swift:113-114). SwiftUI does not guarantee this in all teardown paths (a MenuBarExtra(.window) panel dismissed in certain ways, or a view that fires onAppear twice). endLiveLevels clamps the refcount at 0 (AppStore.swift:633), so a surplus end is harmless, but a surplus begin (onAppear without a matching onDisappear) leaves liveLevelsRefcount > 0, so levelPollTask never cancels and audioLevels() keeps polling with nothing on screen -- defeating the near-zero-idle-CPU goal. Verified: the refcount/teardown logic itself is correct; the risk is purely SwiftUI lifecycle fragility, hence 'likely' not 'confirmed'. | Fix: Make teardown idempotent against a lost onDisappear: also begin/end on scenePhase/window-occlusion, or cap the poller with a 'no consumer for N seconds' self-stop, and consider keying refcounting to surface identity rather than raw increment/decrement.

Feature: Meter suppression for muted, excluded, and unmanaged rows
User Story: As a user, I want the level meter to disappear when an app is muted, excluded, or not under Waves's control, so that the meter only ever signals genuinely live, managed audio and never gives a misleading reading.
Expected Behavior:
• showsLevelMeter is true only when the app is not muted, not excluded, and routingState is .managed or .live (MixerRowView.swift:173-175); otherwise no meter overlay is rendered.
• On mute, setMuted zeroes the app's peakLevel/rmsLevel in the snapshot (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:95-96), and the render IO proc zero-fills output and returns early without writing new levels when isMuted (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1695-1698).
• audioLevels() excludes apps that are not .managed/.live, so excluded/monitor-only/recent apps never receive a liveLevels entry (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1098).
• The meter overlay is allowsHitTesting(false) and accessibilityHidden(true), and the whole row dims to 0.55 opacity when excluded (MixerRowView.swift:131-132, 109).
• On route error the levels are forced to 0 (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:104-105).
Acceptance Criteria:
• Muting a playing app removes its meter (meter width collapses to 0) and the underlying levels report 0.
• Excluding an app hides its meter regardless of whether it is producing audio.
• An app in .monitorOnly/.recent/.error state never shows a meter.
• The meter never intercepts clicks and is invisible to VoiceOver.
• An app whose route entered .error shows no meter.
Impl Notes: Suppression is correct and defensive at multiple layers (UI gate, snapshot zeroing on mute, render-thread early return, audioLevels filtering). One subtlety: on mute the snapshot is zeroed once, but the backend levelUpdateTask only overwrites levels for active controllers via getCurrentLevels(); since the muted render path returns before writeLevels, the controller's last-written non-zero level can persist in the box until the next render — however updateAudioLevels still copies that stale value. In practice the explicit snapshot zero in setMuted plus the UI gate (showsLevelMeter requires !isMuted) mask this, so the user never sees a stale meter on a muted row.
Errors Found: • [HIGH/correctness] A managed, non-muted app at volume 0 shows a frozen non-zero meter (misleading reading) (Sources/Waves/Services/Audio/WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1702-1705) — Dragging an app's slider to 0 (not muted) keeps routingState == .managed and does NOT zero peakLevel/rmsLevel (AppStore.swift:356-382; contrast setMuted which zeroes them at WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:95-96). In the IO render proc, volume == 0.0 takes the early return at WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1702-1705, which zero-fills output and returns BEFORE renderTappedAudio -- and writeLevels is called ONLY at the end of renderTappedAudio (verified: sole call site at line 1937). So the controller's TapRenderStateBox retains its last non-zero peak/rms. The controller stays isActive (volume 0 does not call setInactive), so updateAudioLevels() keeps copying that stale value into snapshot.apps[].peakLevel/rmsLevel (1396-1401), audioLevels() returns it (1099), the 300ms poll writes it into store.liveLevels, and showsLevelMeter is true (!isMuted && !isExcluded && .managed). The main-window row therefore displays a static non-zero cyan bar for an app emitting pure silence -- the 'misleading reading' the story says must never happen. The muted and isActive==0 branches similarly skip writeLevels, but mute is masked by the snapshot zero + !isMuted gate; only the volume-0 case leaks. | Fix: Before the volume==0 early return (and ideally the isMuted and isActive==0 branches) call stateBox.writeLevels(peakLevel: 0, rmsLevel: 0). Alternatively treat volume==0 as silence in updateAudioLevels, or have setDesiredVolume(0) zero peakLevel/rmsLevel in the snapshot the way setMuted does.

Feature: Activity grouping and 'Playing audio' subtitle driven by levels
User Story: As a user scanning the mixer, I want apps that are currently producing sound to be labeled 'Playing audio' and grouped as live, so that I can find the noisy app without studying every row.
Expected Behavior:
• liveApps includes apps in .live state, or .managed-and-unmuted apps whose max(peakLevel, rmsLevel) > 0.001 (AppStore.swift:117-122).
• The row subtitle shows 'Playing audio' for .live apps, and for .managed unmuted apps with max(peakLevel,rmsLevel) > 0.001 (MixerRowView.swift:185-191).
• The .live routing state renders a distinct waveform chip and cyan tint via RoutingStateIndicator/RoutingStateDot (MixerRowView.swift:396-403, 458-464, 504-509).
• An activity-based sort/categorization path uses the same level threshold to classify live vs recent apps (AppStore.swift:124-127, 1304-1316).
Acceptance Criteria:
• A managed, unmuted app with measurable output (>0.001) is labeled 'Playing audio' and counted in liveApps.
• A .live app shows 'Playing audio' even before managed metering kicks in.
• A silent or muted managed app is not labeled 'Playing audio'.
• The .live state row shows the waveform indicator in cyan.
Impl Notes: The subtitle/grouping logic reads app.peakLevel/app.rmsLevel from the `session` snapshot, NOT from the visibility-gated store.liveLevels dict that drives the meter bar. The session's peak/rms are only refreshed by an explicit refresh() (manual or device-change), backend.start(), or specific state transitions — there is no periodic session refresh loop. So the 'Playing audio' subtitle and live-vs-recent grouping can be stale relative to the moving meter: a row's cyan meter (LVL-01) updates every ~300ms while its 'Playing audio' label may not update until the next refresh. This is a real consistency gap between two level-derived UI signals reading from two different data sources at very different cadences.
Errors Found:
• [HIGH/inconsistency] 'Playing audio' label and live grouping read stale session levels, not the live meter data (Sources/Waves/Features/Mixer/MixerRowView.swift:187) — The subtitle (MixerRowView.swift:185-191), liveApps (AppStore.swift:117-122), recentApps grouping (AppStore.swift:124-128), and activityRank (AppStore.swift:1303-1320) all gate on max(app.peakLevel, app.rmsLevel) > 0.001 read from store.session. Verified there is NO periodic loop that refreshes session levels: the only periodic loop in AppStore is the level poll at 619-628, which writes store.liveLevels and never touches session.apps[].peakLevel; session peak/rms is updated only by explicit refresh()/start()/currentSnapshot() on user actions, device changes, or termination (none timer-driven). Meanwhile the meter bar (LVL-01) reads store.liveLevels, refreshed every ~300ms. Result: when a managed app starts playing, its cyan meter moves but its subtitle stays NOT 'Playing audio' (and it is not counted in liveApps / can be mis-sorted) until the next manual/structural refresh -- violating the AC 'A managed, unmuted app with measurable output (>0.001) is labeled Playing audio.' | Fix: Feed store.liveLevels into the label/grouping: compute 'isPlaying' from store.liveLevels[app.logicalID] (falling back to session) in subtitle/liveApps/activityRank, or have the level poll also update session.apps[].peakLevel/rmsLevel (debounced) so both UI signals share one cadence.
• [MEDIUM/correctness] App that went silent can stay labeled 'Playing audio' due to stale session levels (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:120) — Because session.peakLevel/rmsLevel is not periodically refreshed (verified above), a managed app that was playing and then went silent retains peakLevel/rmsLevel > 0.001 in session until the next event-driven refresh(). The subtitle (MixerRowView.swift:185-191) and liveApps (AppStore.swift:120) therefore keep reporting 'Playing audio' for an app that is now silent -- violating the AC 'A silent or muted managed app is not labeled Playing audio.' (Mute is handled because setMuted zeroes session levels and termination zeroes them at AppStore.swift:929-930; plain silence on a still-managed app is not.) | Fix: Derive the playing state from the live-polled store.liveLevels (which decays toward 0 from the realtime path) instead of the static session snapshot, or refresh session levels on the poll cadence.
• [MEDIUM/inconsistency] Menu-bar icon's 3-wave 'live' glyph is stale for managed apps that start/stop playing (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:149-150) — menuBarIconName (AppStore.swift:143-153) returns 'speaker.wave.3.fill' only when !liveApps.isEmpty, and liveApps (AppStore.swift:117-122) classifies a managed app as live via max(session.peakLevel, session.rmsLevel) > 0.001. Since session levels are never periodically refreshed (same root cause as the two issues above), a managed app that begins playing does not flip the menu-bar icon to the 3-wave glyph until the next event-driven refresh, and one that goes silent keeps the icon in the live state. This violates the feature's AC 'The menu-bar icon reflects a live state with the 3-wave speaker glyph' and the Live-section AC for managed-audible apps. Purely .live (unmanaged) apps still flip the icon correctly; only the managed-audible path is stale. | Fix: Have liveApps/menuBarIconName consult store.liveLevels for managed-audible classification (the same cadence as the meter), or refresh session levels on the poll cadence so the icon, the Live section, and the meter share one source of truth.

Feature: Realtime-safe, lock-light level capture
User Story: As a user, I want metering to never cause audio glitches or stutters, so that watching the meters never costs me clean playback.
Expected Behavior:
• writeLevels is called on the realtime IO render thread and uses stateLock.try(); if the lock is contended it returns immediately and skips the update rather than blocking (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1578-1588).
• TapDSP is documented and implemented as allocation-free, lock-free, dispatch-free sample math safe to call on the realtime thread (TapDSP.swift:11-17, 56-103).
• getCurrentLevels()/readLevels() take the lock from the main-actor/background side to read a consistent (peak,rms) pair (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1590-1595, 1672-1673).
• rms() guards against divide-by-zero, returning 0 (never NaN) when sampleCount is 0 (TapDSP.swift:101-103).
Acceptance Criteria:
• The render-thread level write path performs no blocking lock acquisition and no allocation.
• A contended write is dropped and recovered on the next render cycle without affecting audio output.
• rms(sum:0, sampleCount:0) returns 0 and is not NaN (covered by TapDSPTests).
• Reading levels yields a coherent peak/rms pair, not a torn read.
Impl Notes: Solid realtime hygiene: try-lock on the audio thread, blocking lock only on consumer side, NaN guard tested. Minor: writeLevels updates both state.pointee and a duplicate stateBox struct under the lock, and the try-lock 'skip on contention' means under sustained main-thread lock pressure the meter could briefly freeze (acceptable, since it's cosmetic). The duplicated state storage (pointer + struct mirror) is a maintenance smell but not a correctness bug here.

Area: Menu Bar Icon & Menu Bar Mixer
Feature: Dynamic menu bar status icon
User Story: As a macOS user running several audio apps, I want the Waves menu bar icon to reflect what my audio is doing right now, so that I can tell at a glance whether something is muted or actively playing without opening the panel.
Expected Behavior:
• The menu bar extra renders a SF Symbol whose name is supplied by store.menuBarIconName (WavesApp.swift:59).
• If ANY visible app is muted, the icon is 'speaker.slash.fill' (AppStore.swift:146-147); this takes precedence over the playing state.
• Else if there is at least one live app (liveApps non-empty), the icon is 'speaker.wave.3.fill' (AppStore.swift:149-150).
• Otherwise the icon is the neutral 'speaker.wave.2.fill' (AppStore.swift:152).
• Because AppStore is @Observable (AppStore.swift:23), the icon recomputes reactively as mute state / live-level membership changes.
• Live-app membership is driven by routingState == .live OR (managed && !muted && peak/rms > 0.001) (AppStore.swift:117-122), which depends on the 300ms live-level poll while a surface is visible.
Acceptance Criteria:
• Muting any visible app changes the menu bar icon to the slash variant within one observation cycle.
• With at least one app actively producing sound (peak/rms above threshold) and none muted, the icon shows the 3-wave variant.
• With apps present but none muted and none live, the icon shows the 2-wave variant.
• When a muted app and a live app coexist, the muted (slash) icon wins.
• Unmuting the last muted app while audio is playing flips the icon from slash to 3-wave.
Impl Notes: Logic is wired and reactive, but two correctness/UX concerns: (1) The 'live' detection depends on the live-level poll (beginLiveLevels), which only runs while a UI surface is visible (MenuBarMixerView.onAppear / MixerRowView). When the menu panel and main window are both closed, peak/rms levels are not refreshed, so the 3-wave 'playing' state can be stale and the icon may sit on 2-wave even though audio is playing. (2) 'mute' uses ANY visible app muted, so muting a single background app makes the global icon show slash even while other apps play loudly, which can read as misleading. The 'speaker.slash.fill' choice also collides visually with the per-row mute glyph.
Errors Found:
• [HIGH/correctness] Menu bar 'playing' (3-wave) icon is stale: nothing refreshes session peak/routingState reactively to playback (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:117-122) — menuBarIconName -> liveApps reads store.session.apps[].routingState and .peakLevel/.rmsLevel (AppStore.swift:117-122). The store's session is only reassigned on explicit user actions (start/refresh/volume-commit/mute/boost/preset/device-change/recover — AppStore.swift:205,211,242,453,596,799,1010,1022,1184,1195,1419,1433); there is NO periodic/timer session refresh. The visibility-gated poll beginLiveLevels (AppStore.swift:616-629) writes ONLY to store.liveLevels, never into session. Confirmed in the real backend: WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.updateAudioLevels (line 1386-1402, every 250ms) mutates only the backend's internal snapshot.apps[].peakLevel/rmsLevel for active controllers and does NOT change routingState; the store never re-reads that snapshot in the background. .live is only assigned during discoverRunningApps/buildSnapshot. Net: when an app starts or stops producing sound, the icon does not flip between speaker.wave.2.fill and speaker.wave.3.fill until the user triggers a session-mutating action — even while the menu panel is open. | Fix: Drive liveApps from the data that is actually polled (consult store.liveLevels for managed-app liveness) and/or add a lightweight periodic session refresh while a surface is visible so routingState/peak reflect current playback; then align the story's expectedBehavior with the chosen source of truth.
• [MEDIUM/ux] Slash icon shown whenever ANY visible app is muted, overriding the playing state even for a silent background app (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:146-147) — menuBarIconName returns speaker.slash.fill whenever visibleApps.contains(where: \.isMuted) (AppStore.swift:146-147) with unconditional precedence over !liveApps.isEmpty. Muting one idle background app makes the global menu-bar icon read 'everything muted' even while other apps play loudly. The story explicitly documents this precedence as intended (expectedBehavior: 'this takes precedence over the playing state'), so it is a deliberate-but-debatable UX choice rather than a logic defect; still a real at-a-glance-misread risk. The chosen glyph also matches the per-row mute toggle glyph (MixerRowView.swift:317), so it reads like a control, not a status. | Fix: Prefer the 3-wave playing variant when a live app is concurrently playing; reserve the slash variant for when the muted app is the would-be-live one or nothing is playing. Consider a status glyph distinct from the per-row mute control.

Area: Menu Bar Icon & Menu Bar Mixer
Feature: Menu bar mixer panel (compact per-app rows)
User Story: As a user reacting to a sudden audio situation, I want a compact mixer to drop down from the menu bar with my pinned, live, and recent apps, so that I can adjust volume, boost, or mute in seconds without opening the full window.
Expected Behavior:
• Clicking the menu bar extra opens a 400pt-wide window-style panel hosting MenuBarMixerView (WavesApp.swift:62-66).
• The panel shows a Pinned section only when pinnedApps is non-empty (MenuBarMixerView.swift:42-44), a Live section always (line 46), and a Recent section (max 3 rows) only when preferences.showRecentApps is true (lines 48-50).
• Each row (CompactMixerRow) shows app icon, name, an 'Excluded' tag or routing-state dot, a 0...1 volume slider, a boost menu, and a mute toggle (MixerRowView.swift:266-325).
• Dragging the slider calls store.setDesiredVolume live and commits on release via commitDesiredVolume (MixerRowView.swift:283-296).
• The mute button toggles store.setMuted and bounces the symbol unless reduceMotion is set (MixerRowView.swift:313-319).
• Excluded apps render at 0.55 opacity with slider/boost/mute disabled (MixerRowView.swift:308-326).
• If a section has more apps than maxVisible (4 for Pinned/Live, 3 for Recent), an 'N more in Waves' button opens the main window (MenuBarMixerView.swift:194-205).
• Opening the panel calls store.start() and store.beginLiveLevels(); closing it calls store.endLiveLevels() (MenuBarMixerView.swift:110-114).
Acceptance Criteria:
• Opening the menu shows pinned/live/recent sections per the visibility rules above.
• Moving a row slider changes that app's desired volume and persists on release.
• Tapping a row mute button mutes/unmutes that specific app and updates the glyph.
• An excluded app's controls are visibly dimmed and non-interactive.
• A section with more than its max-visible apps shows an 'N more in Waves' affordance that opens the main window.
• Closing the menu stops the live-level poll when no other surface holds a reference.
Impl Notes: Fully functional for volume/boost/mute. Gap: the menu calls beginLiveLevels() (MenuBarMixerView.swift:113) but CompactMixerRow does NOT render the live cyan meter — the meter overlay (MixerRowView.swift:122-129, meterLevel:177) lives only in the full MixerRowView. So the poll's only visible effect in the menu is icon/section membership, not a moving meter, which is wasted work and an inconsistency with the main window. Also the 'Live' section header is always rendered logic-wise but CompactSection hides itself when empty (MixerRowView is fine), so an all-empty state is handled separately (see MENU-03).
Errors Found:
• [HIGH/logic] Pinned apps are duplicated into the Live and/or Recent section of the menu panel (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:124-128) — MenuBarMixerView renders Pinned (store.pinnedApps), Live (store.liveApps), and Recent (store.recentApps) at once (lines 42-50). recentApps subtracts only liveApps IDs, not pinned IDs (AppStore.swift:124-128); pinnedApps and liveApps are also not mutually exclusive (both filter visibleApps independently, AppStore.swift:109-122). So a pinned-not-live app appears in BOTH Pinned and Recent, and a pinned-and-live app appears in BOTH Pinned and Live. Each occurrence is a separate CompactMixerRow with its own controls. (Verified not a crash: separate ForEach scopes, so it is visual/UX duplication, not an id collision.) The main window uses mutually-exclusive tabs, so duplication is specific to the menu panel. | Fix: Exclude pinned (and live) IDs from recentApps, and exclude pinned IDs from the Live section so each app appears in exactly one section of the menu panel.
• [MEDIUM/edge-case] Menu panel shows 'No audio apps detected' empty state when Show-recent-apps is off, even with running apps (Sources/Waves/Features/Mixer/MenuBarMixerView.swift:48-52) — recentApps returns [] whenever preferences.showRecentApps is false (AppStore.swift:125), and that toggle is user-facing in Settings (SettingsView.swift:58-66, default true). The menu's only app sections are Pinned, Live, and Recent (MenuBarMixerView.swift:42-50), and the empty-state condition is pinnedApps.isEmpty && liveApps.isEmpty && recentApps.isEmpty (line 52). With Show-recent-apps disabled and nothing pinned or currently live, every running-but-idle app is suppressed, so the panel renders 'No audio apps detected' with Refresh/Settings buttons despite many apps running. The main window still lists them, producing a menu-vs-window state desync. The first tester missed this because they treated recentApps as always populated. | Fix: When showRecentApps is off, still surface non-live/non-pinned apps under a neutral section (or gate the empty state on the true count of visibleApps), so the menu never claims zero apps while apps are running.
• [LOW/inconsistency] Menu panel starts the live-level poll but compact rows render no meter (wasted wakeups) (Sources/Waves/Features/Mixer/MixerRowView.swift:259-327) — Opening the panel calls store.beginLiveLevels() (MenuBarMixerView.swift:113), starting the 300ms poll that populates store.liveLevels (AppStore.swift:616-629). CompactMixerRow (MixerRowView.swift:259-327) has no level-meter overlay — the cyan meter (overlay MixerRowView.swift:122-134, meterLevel:177-180) exists only in the full MixerRowView used by the main window. So in the menu the poll draws no meter; and per MENU-01 it does not drive the icon's live state either, making it largely wasted work while the panel is open. | Fix: Either add the meter overlay to CompactMixerRow (reuse meterLevel/showsLevelMeter), or drop beginLiveLevels/endLiveLevels from the menu and feed the icon from a cheaper source.

Area: Menu Bar Icon & Menu Bar Mixer
Feature: Empty and loading states in the menu panel
User Story: As a user who opens the menu when nothing is playing or while detection is still running, I want clear feedback instead of a blank panel, so that I know Waves is working and how to proceed.
Expected Behavior:
• While store.isLoading is true, the panel shows a small ProgressView with 'Refreshing audio sessions' caption (MenuBarMixerView.swift:30-38).
• When pinnedApps, liveApps, and recentApps are all empty, an empty state appears: a 'speaker.slash' glyph, 'No audio apps detected' text, and Refresh + Settings buttons (MenuBarMixerView.swift:52-72).
• The Refresh button (header icon and empty-state) calls store.refresh() (MenuBarMixerView.swift:19, 61).
• isLoading is set true on start/refresh only when session.apps is empty, and cleared in a defer (AppStore.swift:194, 234-239).
Acceptance Criteria:
• On first launch with no cached session, the panel shows the loading row until detection completes.
• With zero detected apps across all sections, the empty-state graphic and Refresh/Settings buttons render.
• Tapping Refresh in the empty state re-runs detection and replaces the empty state once apps appear.
• The loading row disappears once isLoading clears.
Impl Notes: Solid. Minor UX note: isLoading only becomes true when session.apps is empty, so a refresh that already has a warm cached session shows no spinner (header refresh icon stays static) — acceptable but means a slow refresh over an existing list gives no in-panel progress signal beyond the toast.
Errors Found: • [MEDIUM/ux] Loading spinner and 'No audio apps detected' empty state render simultaneously on first launch (Sources/Waves/Features/Mixer/MenuBarMixerView.swift:52) — The empty-state condition (MenuBarMixerView.swift:52) is pinnedApps.isEmpty && liveApps.isEmpty && recentApps.isEmpty and does NOT exclude store.isLoading. On first launch with no cached session, start() sets isLoading=true (AppStore.swift:194) while all sections are empty, so the panel shows BOTH the 'Refreshing audio sessions' ProgressView row (lines 30-38) AND the 'No audio apps detected' graphic with Refresh/Settings buttons (lines 52-72) at once. Verified: start() sets isLoading = session.apps.isEmpty, and the empty session is the first-launch default. | Fix: Gate the empty state on !store.isLoading (e.g. if !store.isLoading && pinnedApps.isEmpty && liveApps.isEmpty && recentApps.isEmpty), showing only the loading row while isLoading is true.

Area: Menu Bar Icon & Menu Bar Mixer
Feature: Output device switcher in the menu panel
User Story: As a user juggling speakers, headphones, and AirPods, I want to switch the system output device straight from the menu bar panel, so that I can re-route audio without opening System Settings.
Expected Behavior:
• An OutputDevicePicker menu shows the current device name with a speaker icon and up/down chevron (MenuBarMixerView.swift:120-157).
• The menu lists store.availableDevices; the active device (id == currentDeviceID) gets a checkmark Label (MenuBarMixerView.swift:125-135).
• Selecting a device calls store.selectOutputDevice, which sets the default output via the backend and shows an 'Output switched' toast, then refreshes the device list (AppStore.swift:759-772).
• Re-selecting the already-current device is a no-op (AppStore.swift:760).
• If availableDevices is empty, the menu shows 'No output devices found' (MenuBarMixerView.swift:136-138).
• currentDeviceName falls back to 'No output device' when session.currentDevice is nil (AppStore.swift:139-141).
• The picker refreshes its device list onAppear (MenuBarMixerView.swift:156).
Acceptance Criteria:
• Opening the picker lists all available output devices with a checkmark on the active one.
• Choosing a different device switches system output and shows a success toast.
• Choosing the current device does nothing.
• With no devices available, the menu shows the 'No output devices found' placeholder and the label reads 'No output device'.
• A switch failure surfaces an error toast rather than silently failing (AppStore.swift:768-770).
Impl Notes: Wired end-to-end with success/error toasts and empty-device handling. The checkmark/current-device state relies on availableDevices being refreshed after the switch and on device-change events updating session — generally consistent, though the checkmark can momentarily lag a system-initiated device change until the next refreshOutputDevices/handleDeviceChange.
Errors Found: • [MEDIUM/edge-case] Output picker checkmark/label go stale after a switch when 'Auto-restore device' is disabled (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:792-793) — selectOutputDevice (AppStore.swift:759-772) sets the system default and refreshes availableDevices but does NOT update session.currentDevice; it relies on the CoreAudio listener -> deviceChangeEvents -> handleDeviceChange. handleDeviceChange returns immediately when preferences.autoRestoreDevice is false (AppStore.swift:792-793). autoRestoreDevice defaults to true (UserPreferences.swift:9) but is user-toggleable (SettingsView.swift:78-86, verified). With it disabled, session.currentDevice is never updated after a switch, so currentDeviceID (AppStore.swift:130-132) keeps returning the old device: the picker label (currentDeviceName) and checkmark (MenuBarMixerView.swift:129) stay on the previously-selected device, and the guard device.id != currentDeviceID (line 760) compares against the stale value. | Fix: Update session.currentDevice in selectOutputDevice's success path independent of autoRestoreDevice (e.g. session = await backend.currentSnapshot()), or move the session refresh in handleDeviceChange out from under the autoRestoreDevice guard so a device change always refreshes the displayed current device.

Area: Menu Bar Icon & Menu Bar Mixer
Feature: Preset quick-apply, Open Waves, Settings, and Launch-at-login from the panel
User Story: As a returning user, I want one-tap access to presets, the full window, Settings, and a launch-at-login toggle from the menu panel, so that the menu bar is a complete control surface for the mixer.
Expected Behavior:
• A 'Presets' submenu (slider icon) lists store.presets; choosing one calls store.applyPreset, which applies it (stripping excluded apps) and shows a 'Preset applied' toast (MenuBarMixerView.swift:160-175; AppStore.swift:1001-1029).
• 'Open Waves' opens the main window by id and activates the app (MenuBarMixerView.swift:77-80).
• 'Settings' opens the Settings scene and activates the app (MenuBarMixerView.swift:83-87).
• A labels-hidden 'Launch at login' switch is bound to store.launchAtLoginEnabled (MenuBarMixerView.swift:90-100).
• Toggling launch-at-login calls loginItemService.setEnabled; if the OS reports the state didn't take (needs approval), a 'Login item needs approval' warning toast is shown, and failures show an error toast (AppStore.swift:161-186).
• A header refresh button and ⌘R shortcut both call store.refresh() (MenuBarMixerView.swift:18-25).
Acceptance Criteria:
• The Presets submenu lists all presets (defaults plus saved) and applying one changes the live mix and toasts.
• 'Open Waves' brings the main mixer window to the front.
• 'Settings' opens the preferences window to the front.
• Flipping the Launch-at-login switch persists the login-item state and reflects the OS-reported result (including an approval-needed warning).
• ⌘R or the refresh icon re-detects apps.
Impl Notes: All actions are wired with real effects and feedback. UX note: the Presets submenu has no empty-state branch (MenuBarMixerView.swift:164-168), but presets are seeded with Preset.defaults (AppStore.swift:82) so it is effectively never empty; if a user deleted all presets the submenu would render empty with no label. The launch-at-login binding's setter reconciles to the OS-reported status, which is the correct, honest behavior.
Errors Found: • [LOW/edge-case] Presets submenu renders empty with no placeholder after the user deletes all presets, and stays empty across launches (Sources/Waves/Features/Mixer/MenuBarMixerView.swift:160-175) — PresetQuickPicker (MenuBarMixerView.swift:160-175) has no empty-state branch — it just ForEach(store.presets). deletePresets (AppStore.swift:1066-1077) lets the user remove every preset, including seeded Preset.defaults, and persists the empty array. Verified PresetStore.load (PresetStore.swift:30-57) re-seeds defaults ONLY when the file is missing or unreadable/corrupt — a valid empty array decodes successfully and is returned as-is, so the empty state survives relaunch. The Presets menu then opens as an empty popover with no explanatory label. | Fix: Add an empty-state item to PresetQuickPicker (disabled 'No presets saved' / 'Save one in Waves' when store.presets.isEmpty), and/or prevent deleting the last preset or re-seed defaults when the persisted array is empty.

Area: Menu Bar Icon & Menu Bar Mixer
Feature: Menu bar extra presence / visibility control
User Story: As a user who wants control over my menu bar, I want to be able to show or hide the Waves menu bar icon, so that I can keep my menu bar uncluttered if I prefer to drive Waves from the main window.
Expected Behavior:
• The MenuBarExtra's isInserted is bound to @AppStorage('showMenuBarExtra'), defaulting to true (WavesApp.swift:11,57-60).
• When showMenuBarExtra is true the menu bar icon is present; when false it would be removed.
Acceptance Criteria:
• By default the Waves menu bar icon is visible.
• There exists a user-facing control to hide/show the menu bar icon that flips showMenuBarExtra.
• Setting the preference persists across launches via AppStorage.
Impl Notes: The plumbing exists (AppStorage-backed isInserted) and persists, but there is NO UI anywhere to toggle showMenuBarExtra — a grep across Sources finds the key only in WavesApp.swift, and SettingsView has no 'Show menu bar icon' control. So in practice the icon is always shown and the user has no supported way to hide it. Either add a Settings toggle or treat the icon as always-on by design; as written it is a half-wired preference.
Errors Found:
• [MEDIUM/dead-code] No user-facing control exists to toggle showMenuBarExtra; the preference is unreachable (Sources/Waves/App/WavesApp.swift:11) — MenuBarExtra isInserted binds to @AppStorage("showMenuBarExtra") defaulting to true (WavesApp.swift:11,57-60). Verified by grep: the key appears ONLY in WavesApp.swift across all of Sources — there is no toggle in SettingsView or anywhere else (SettingsView exposes Show-recent-apps, Show-system-processes, Auto-restore device, etc., but no 'Show menu bar icon'). So the icon is effectively always shown and the user has no supported way to hide it; the AppStorage plumbing is half-wired. | Fix: Add a 'Show Waves in the menu bar' toggle in SettingsView bound to @AppStorage("showMenuBarExtra"), or document the icon as always-on and remove the unused isInserted binding.
• [LOW/accessibility] Menu bar icon conveys no mute/playing status to VoiceOver (Sources/Waves/App/WavesApp.swift:57-60) — The MenuBarExtra is created with a fixed title 'Waves' and only the systemImage changes with state (WavesApp.swift:57-60). The whole point of MENU-01's story is at-a-glance status (muted vs playing vs idle), but a VoiceOver user hears only 'Waves' regardless of which of the three SF Symbols is showing, because the accessible label is the static title and SF Symbol name changes are not surfaced as state. This is a real gap for the at-a-glance-status story; severity is low because the full per-app state is reachable inside the panel. | Fix: Expose the status to assistive tech, e.g. give the MenuBarExtra label a state-dependent accessibility value/label (Muted / Playing / Idle) derived from menuBarIconName, so the at-a-glance status is also available non-visually.

Area: Main Window Mixer UI & Empty State
Feature: Mixer source list with sidebar scopes
User Story: As a macOS user managing app audio, I want a main window that lists my apps in a sidebar/detail layout scoped by Running / Pinned / Live / Recent, so that I can find and control the exact app I care about.
Expected Behavior:
• Window uses NavigationSplitView with a Sources sidebar (Running, Pinned, Live, Recent) and a detail pane showing the matching app list (MainWindowView.swift:14-24, 245-299)
• Each sidebar row shows an icon, title, and a count subtitle like '3 running apps' / '1 pinned app' with correct singular/plural (MainWindowView.swift:185-199, 301-322)
• Selected scope maps to a distinct store collection: Running->visibleApps, Pinned->pinnedApps, Live->liveApps, Recent->recentApps (MainWindowView.swift:102-113; AppStore.swift:103-128)
• Selected scope is persisted across launches via @SceneStorage('waves.selectedScope') so the user returns to the same scope (MainWindowView.swift:8)
• Detail pane renders one MixerRowView per app inside an inset List, and a top OutputSummaryView header plus a bottom DiagnosticsPanel (MainWindowView.swift:324-405)
• A single unique main Window (not WindowGroup) prevents duplicate mixer windows; min size 980x620, default 1100x680 (WavesApp.swift:30-39, 33)
Acceptance Criteria:
• Selecting each of the four sidebar entries swaps the detail list to the corresponding scope's apps
• Sidebar count text matches the number of rows shown for that scope and pluralizes correctly
• Closing and reopening the window restores the previously selected scope
• Only one Waves main window can exist at a time
Impl Notes: Fully wired. Note 'Recent' is gated by preferences.showRecentApps and returns [] when disabled (AppStore.swift:124-128), so the Recent scope can legitimately appear empty even when apps exist — handled by the empty state but may surprise users. The local private enum is named SourceFilter and used directly as @SceneStorage RawRepresentable; works because it is String-backed.
Errors Found: • [LOW/edge-case] Recent scope silently empty when showRecentApps preference is off, with misleading empty copy and dead recovery actions (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:124) — Re-verified: recentApps returns [] whenever preferences.showRecentApps is false (AppStore.swift:124-125), independent of how many apps exist. Selecting the Recent scope then renders the generic empty state title 'No Recent Apps' with message 'Apps appear here when they're not actively playing audio.' (MainWindowView.swift:223, 239-240) even though apps DO exist; the message gives no hint that the cause is a disabled preference. The sidebar count row shows '0 recent apps' (filter.count(in:) -> recentApps.count == 0). Compounding it, the empty-state offers a prominent 'Refresh' button (MainWindowView.swift:344-350) that can never populate the list while the preference is off, so the primary recovery affordance is a dead end. The feature-status implNotes for WIN-01 explicitly acknowledges this surprise. | Fix: When selection == .recent and preferences.showRecentApps == false, render a tailored empty message (e.g. 'Recent apps are turned off. Enable them in Settings.') and surface the Settings button as primary, or hide the Recent sidebar entry entirely while the preference is disabled.

Area: Main Window Mixer UI & Empty State
Feature: Filter/search across apps
User Story: As a user with many running apps, I want to type in the sidebar search field to filter the list by app name or category, so that I can jump to a specific app quickly.
Expected Behavior:
• A .searchable field placed in the sidebar with prompt 'Filter apps' drives searchText (MainWindowView.swift:25)
• Non-empty query matches case-insensitively against app.displayName OR app.category.displayName (MainWindowView.swift:115-130)
• Search deliberately spans ALL visibleApps rather than just the selected scope, so a match in another section is not hidden (MainWindowView.swift:123-129)
• Query is trimmed of whitespace and bounded to 100 characters to avoid performance issues (MainWindowView.swift:116-121)
• When search yields no matches, the empty state message switches to 'Try a different search term.' (MainWindowView.swift:227-230)
• While a search is active, drag-to-reorder is suppressed because filtered indices would not map back (MainWindowView.swift:369-375)
Acceptance Criteria:
• Typing part of an app name or category narrows the list to matching apps regardless of selected scope
• An empty/whitespace query shows the unfiltered scoped list
• A query longer than 100 chars is truncated and still filters
• A no-match query shows the 'Try a different search term.' description
Impl Notes: Behaves as documented. UX wrinkle: because search ignores the selected scope and always searches visibleApps, results while on Pinned/Live/Recent can include apps outside that scope; the header count (filter.detail) then reflects the filtered count but the sidebar scope label still says e.g. 'Pinned', which can read as inconsistent.
Errors Found:
• [MEDIUM/inconsistency] Search ignores selected scope but header keeps scope-specific noun, producing contradictory counts (Sources/Waves/Features/Mixer/MainWindowView.swift:447) — Re-verified: filteredApps filters store.visibleApps (ALL apps) regardless of the selected scope (MainWindowView.swift:126-129). The detail header renders filter.detail(count: apps.count) where filter is still the selected scope (MainWindowView.swift:334, 447, 185-199). So on the Pinned scope, typing a query that matches a non-pinned app lists that app while the header reads e.g. '1 pinned app' and the sidebar still highlights 'Pinned'. The noun (pinned/live/recent) does not describe the rows shown. | Fix: When a search query is active, show a neutral count label such as '\(apps.count) result(s)' instead of filter.detail(...), and/or visually neutralize the selected sidebar scope while searching.
• [LOW/inconsistency] Sidebar scope count and detail header count diverge during an active search (Sources/Waves/Features/Mixer/MainWindowView.swift:255) — Added in completeness pass. The sidebar row count is filter.detail(count: filter.count(in: store)) (MainWindowView.swift:255, 171-183), which reads from the full scope collection and is NOT affected by searchText. The detail header is filter.detail(count: apps.count) where apps == filteredApps (MainWindowView.swift:334, 447). So with a search active on the Running scope, the sidebar can show '8 running apps' while the detail header and list show '2 running apps' for the same scope at the same time — two count displays in one window disagreeing. This is a state-desync the first tester did not separately flag. | Fix: Either keep both counts unfiltered (move the header to the scoped, unfiltered count and show a separate 'N results' label during search), or thread the active query into the sidebar count so both reflect the same set.

Area: Main Window Mixer UI & Empty State
Feature: Empty-state guidance with recovery actions
User Story: As a new or stuck user who sees no apps in a scope, I want a clear empty state explaining why and offering Refresh and Settings, so that I can fix the situation without guessing.
Expected Behavior:
• When the scoped/filtered list is empty, a ContentUnavailableView shows a scope-specific title (e.g. 'No Running Apps', 'No Pinned Apps', 'No Live Apps', 'No Recent Apps') with a 'speaker.slash' icon (MainWindowView.swift:336-342, 214-225)
• Each scope has tailored guidance copy: Running='Waves hasn\'t found any running apps yet.', Pinned='Pin apps from the source list to keep them here.', Live='Start playback in an app, then refresh if it does not appear here.', Recent='Apps appear here when they\'re not actively playing audio.' (MainWindowView.swift:227-242)
• Empty state offers a prominent 'Refresh' button (calls store.refresh()) and a bordered 'Settings' button (calls openSettings()) (MainWindowView.swift:344-358)
• If a search is active the description overrides to 'Try a different search term.' regardless of scope (MainWindowView.swift:227-230)
Acceptance Criteria:
• Each of the four scopes shows its own title and guidance message when empty
• Empty-state Refresh button triggers a session refresh; Settings button opens the Settings scene
• A non-matching search shows the search-specific message instead of the scope message
Impl Notes: Solid and matches README's 'Empty State UI' promise. Minor: there is no distinct empty state for a permission-denied / backend-failed startup here — that surfaces only via the RouteHealthBadge and a startup-failed toast (AppStore.swift:221-224), so a user with denied capture permission sees 'No Running Apps' plus a red badge rather than a permissions-specific empty message.

Area: Main Window Mixer UI & Empty State
Feature: Toolbar actions: save preset, refresh, recover routes
User Story: As a user actively managing my mix, I want toolbar buttons (and shortcuts) to save a preset, refresh the app list, and recover managed routes, so that I can maintain control without digging through menus.
Expected Behavior:
• Toolbar '+' opens a Save Preset sheet (⌘S) with a name field; Save is disabled while the trimmed name is empty and validates name <=100 chars before calling store.savePreset (MainWindowView.swift:28-36, 85-91, 137-147, 592-618)
• Toolbar refresh button (arrow.clockwise, ⌘R) calls store.refresh(), which rebuilds the session, updates diagnostics, and shows a 'Library refreshed' toast (MainWindowView.swift:38-46; AppStore.swift:228-253)
• Toolbar 'Recover managed routes' (waveform.path) calls store.recoverRoutes(), reattaching active per-app routes and showing a success or failure toast (MainWindowView.swift:48-56; AppStore.swift:1181-1202)
• A duplicate Refresh command is also registered in the app menu with ⌘R (WavesApp.swift:43-48)
• Saving a preset with an existing (case-insensitive) name overwrites the existing preset in place rather than duplicating; excluded apps are stripped from saved presets (AppStore.swift:1041-1052, 1037-1040)
• All toolbar buttons carry accessibilityLabel + accessibilityHint and help tooltips (MainWindowView.swift:33-56)
Acceptance Criteria:
• ⌘S or the + button opens the Save Preset sheet; saving with a valid name adds/updates a preset and dismisses the sheet
• ⌘R or the refresh button refreshes the session and shows a confirmation toast
• Recover routes button reattaches routes and reports success/failure via toast
• Save button stays disabled for empty/whitespace names
Impl Notes: All three actions are fully wired to backend calls. Two small concerns: (1) ⌘R is bound both on the toolbar button (MainWindowView.swift:46) and as an app-menu command (WavesApp.swift:47), a harmless duplicate; (2) refresh() and recoverRoutes() have no in-flight UI affordance on the recover button (only refresh sets isLoading), so rapid clicks on Recover can stack tasks — refresh is guarded by isRefreshing but recoverRoutes is not.
Errors Found:
• [MEDIUM/ux] Save Preset button is enabled for names over 100 chars but Save then silently fails, leaving the sheet stuck (Sources/Waves/Features/Mixer/MainWindowView.swift:142) — Re-verified: SavePresetSheet disables Save only when the trimmed name is empty (MainWindowView.swift:612). The 100-char limit is enforced only inside savePreset() via 'guard trimmed.count <= maxLength else { return }' (MainWindowView.swift:142-143), which returns WITHOUT calling store.savePreset, WITHOUT dismissing the sheet, and WITHOUT any toast/feedback. A user who types a 101+ char name sees an enabled Save button (and the default-action Return key) that does nothing, with the sheet stuck open. | Fix: Mirror the length guard in the .disabled() modifier (disable when trimmed.count > 100) with inline helper text, or have savePreset() surface a warning toast and keep the field focused.
• [LOW/concurrency] Recover-routes toolbar action is unguarded, so rapid clicks stack concurrent recoverRoutes tasks with no in-flight affordance (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1181) — Re-verified: the toolbar 'Recover managed routes' button calls store.recoverRoutes() (MainWindowView.swift:49), which spawns a Task calling backend.recoverRoutes() with no in-flight guard (AppStore.swift:1181-1202). Unlike refresh(), gated by 'guard !isRefreshing' (AppStore.swift:229), recoverRoutes has no equivalent flag, so repeated clicks launch overlapping recovery tasks that each reassign session and re-query diagnostics, and the button shows no spinner/disabled state. The feature-status implNotes for WIN-04 explicitly calls this out. | Fix: Add an isRecovering flag (mirroring isRefreshing) with 'guard !isRecovering' at the top of recoverRoutes(), set it around the Task, and disable the toolbar button (or show the progress pill) while true.

Area: Main Window Mixer UI & Empty State
Feature: Output summary header and route-health badge
User Story: As a user, I want a header showing the current output device, how many apps are in view, what is currently playing, and overall routing health, so that I can trust the mixer at a glance.
Expected Behavior:
• Header shows the current output device name (store.currentDeviceName, falling back to 'No output device') with a speaker glyph (MainWindowView.swift:434-444; AppStore.swift:139-141)
• Header shows the scope count text and, when apps are live, a cyan 'X, Y, Z playing' summary capped at 3 names with '+N playing' overflow (MainWindowView.swift:446-476)
• A RouteHealthBadge reports 'Ready' (green check) when backendStatus.isRouteRecoveryHealthy, 'Needs attention' (red triangle) when lastError is set, else 'Limited' (secondary) (MainWindowView.swift:479-519)
• The badge exposes its status as help text and accessibilityLabel ('Routing status: ...') (MainWindowView.swift:489-490, 517-519)
• A small inline 'Refreshing' progress pill appears top-leading while store.isLoading is true (MainWindowView.swift:64-82)
Acceptance Criteria:
• Header device name updates when the system output device changes
• Live summary lists up to 3 playing app names with correct overflow count, and disappears when nothing is playing
• Route badge color/label correctly reflects healthy / lastError / limited states
• The refreshing indicator shows during a session load and clears afterward
Impl Notes: Correct and accessible. Edge case: the 'Limited' state (no error but not healthy) renders with .secondary color and the same exclamation-triangle symbol as the error state (MainWindowView.swift:503-515), so 'Limited' and 'Needs attention' are distinguished only by color/text, not icon — acceptable but slightly redundant.
Errors Found:
• [MEDIUM/ux] Refreshing indicator never appears on a normal refresh when apps are already loaded, failing the WIN-05 acceptance criterion (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:234) — Re-verified: the 'Refreshing' progress pill is gated on store.isLoading (MainWindowView.swift:64). Both start() and refresh() set isLoading = session.apps.isEmpty (AppStore.swift:194, 234), so isLoading is true only when there are zero apps. On any refresh while apps are already present (the common case, including the toolbar refresh button and the empty-state Refresh after first load), isLoading stays false and no spinner appears; the user gets no in-flight feedback until the terminal toast fires. WIN-05 acceptance criterion 'The refreshing indicator shows during a session load and clears afterward' is therefore not met for the typical refresh. Raising severity from the first tester's 'low' to 'medium' because it directly fails a written acceptance criterion (not merely a polish gap). | Fix: Drive the pill from isRefreshing (or 'isLoading || isRefreshing') rather than isLoading alone, so a user-initiated refresh shows the spinner even when the list is non-empty.
• [LOW/accessibility] Route-health 'Limited' and 'Needs attention' states are distinguished only by color, sharing the same warning icon (Sources/Waves/Features/Mixer/MainWindowView.swift:503) — Re-verified: RouteHealthBadge.systemImage returns the same 'exclamationmark.triangle.fill' glyph for every non-healthy state (MainWindowView.swift:503-505); only color (.red vs .secondary, lines 507-515) and the label text ('Needs attention' vs 'Limited', lines 497-500) differ. For users with color-vision deficiency or in grayscale/high-contrast modes the two routing states are hard to tell apart from the icon alone. The feature-status implNotes for WIN-05 acknowledges this redundancy. | Fix: Give 'Limited' a distinct SF Symbol (e.g. 'exclamationmark.circle' or 'minus.circle') versus the error 'exclamationmark.triangle.fill', so the states differ by shape as well as color/text.

Area: Main Window Mixer UI & Empty State
Feature: Transient toast notifications
User Story: As a user, I want brief, dismissible toast banners confirming actions and surfacing errors, so that I get feedback without modal interruptions.
Expected Behavior:
• AppToastStack renders store.toasts top-trailing over the window with spring animation (MainWindowView.swift:59-62; AppToasts.swift:3-16)
• Each banner shows a kind-specific icon/color (success green check, warning orange triangle, error red octagon, info cyan) plus title and optional 2-line detail (AppToasts.swift:18-122)
• Each banner has a manual dismiss 'x' button calling store.dismissToast(id:) and an accessibility 'Dismiss' action (AppToasts.swift:48-58, 81-83)
• Toasts auto-dismiss after their duration (default 2.0s) via a per-toast cancellable Task (AppStore.swift:1442-1469)
• At most 3 toasts are kept; older ones are trimmed and their dismissal tasks cancelled (AppStore.swift:58, 1477-1483)
• VoiceOver announces each banner on appear since transient banners are not announced automatically (AppToasts.swift:84-87)
Acceptance Criteria:
• Performing actions (refresh, save preset, recover, errors) shows a toast of the correct kind/color
• A toast auto-dismisses after its duration and can be dismissed early via the x button
• No more than 3 toasts are visible simultaneously; the oldest is removed when a 4th arrives
• Each toast is announced to VoiceOver and offers a Dismiss accessibility action
Impl Notes: Robust: refcounted auto-dismiss, trimming, and a11y announcements all present. Minor layout note: AppToastStack applies horizontal padding both in AppToasts.swift:11 and again at the call site (MainWindowView.swift:60), so banners are inset slightly more than other top chrome; purely cosmetic.
Errors Found: • [LOW/ux] Toast stack horizontal inset is applied twice (stack + call site), over-insetting banners relative to other top chrome (Sources/Waves/Shared/UI/AppToasts.swift:12) — Re-verified with a correction to the first tester's count. AppToastStack applies .padding(.horizontal, 14) at AppToasts.swift:12 and the call site applies another .padding(.horizontal, 14) at MainWindowView.swift:60; these two compound to ~28pt of OUTER inset on the toast container, more than the toolbar/refresh-pill chrome. The first tester also cited the banner-internal .padding(.horizontal, 14) at AppToasts.swift:60 as a third duplicate, but that one is the banner's own content inset (text away from the banner's rounded edge) and is correct — it is not part of the over-inset. So the real defect is the doubled outer padding (stack + call site), not a triple-application. Purely cosmetic. | Fix: Remove the redundant horizontal padding from either AppToastStack (AppToasts.swift:12) or the call site (MainWindowView.swift:60) so the container is inset once; leave the banner-internal padding intact.

Area: Main Window Mixer UI & Empty State
Feature: Keyboard-driven control and drag-to-reorder in the list
User Story: As a keyboard/power user, I want to drive the selected mixer row entirely from the keyboard and reorder apps by dragging, so that I can operate the mixer without a mouse and arrange it to my workflow.
Expected Behavior:
• The detail List supports selection; key handlers act on the selected row: Space/M toggle mute, '='/'-' nudge volume by 0.05 (clamped 0..1 and committed), 'b' cycles boost 1->4->1, 'p' toggles pin (MainWindowView.swift:362-386, 408-425)
• handleKey returns .ignored when no row is selected so the keystroke falls through (MainWindowView.swift:408-414)
• Drag-to-reorder (onMove) only takes effect for the Running scope with manual sort mode and empty search, then calls store.reorderApps which switches sortMode to manual and persists the custom order (MainWindowView.swift:369-375; AppStore.swift:1339-1354)
• Accessibility rotors expose 'Playing apps' and 'Needs attention' (error routing) entries for quick navigation (MainWindowView.swift:388-397)
Acceptance Criteria:
• With a row selected, Space/M mute, +/- volume nudge, b boost-cycle, and p pin all act on that row only
• Pressing a control key with no selection does nothing (event ignored)
• Dragging a row in Running+manual+no-search reorders and persists the order; reorder is disabled in other scopes, non-manual sort, or during search
• VoiceOver rotors list currently-playing and error apps
Impl Notes: Comprehensive keyboard support. Edge cases worth noting: (1) onMove is wired on the ForEach even when reorder is disallowed, but the guard means a drag in non-Running/sorted/searching states is silently a no-op (the row snaps back), which can feel unresponsive without explanation; (2) cycleBoost rounds and caps at 4 but relies on volumeBoost already being an integer-ish Float, fine given the 1..4 menu options.
Errors Found: • [MEDIUM/ux] Drag-to-reorder is always armed but silently no-ops outside Running+manual+no-search, so the row snaps back with no explanation (Sources/Waves/Features/Mixer/MainWindowView.swift:369) — Re-verified: the .onMove handler is attached unconditionally to the ForEach (MainWindowView.swift:369-375), so List renders drag affordances in every scope, sort mode, and during search. The reorder is committed only when 'filter == .running && store.preferences.sortMode == .manual && searchText.isEmpty' (MainWindowView.swift:372); otherwise the closure body does nothing and the dragged row animates back with no toast, alert, or disabled cursor. Users on Pinned/Live/Recent, on Running with name/activity/category sort, or while searching experience a drag that appears to work but is silently rejected. The feature-status implNotes for WIN-07 acknowledges this. Severity medium is defensible (UX confusion, no data loss), matching the first tester. | Fix: Apply .moveDisabled(true) to rows (or attach .onMove only) when filter != .running || sortMode != .manual || !searchText.isEmpty, or show a one-line toast explaining reordering requires the Running scope with Manual sort and no active search.

Feature: Global volume-up hotkey (⌘⌥↑)
User Story: As a Waves user, I want to press ⌘⌥↑ from anywhere in macOS to raise the volume of the app I'm currently using, so that I can fine-tune per-app loudness without switching to the Waves window.
Expected Behavior:
• A system-wide keyDown monitor (NSEvent.addGlobalMonitorForEvents) is installed when shortcuts are enabled and fires on every keyDown app-wide (WavesApp.swift:150-155)
• On keyCode 126 (Up arrow) with both Command and Option held, AppDelegate calls store.increaseVolumeForFrontmostApp() (WavesApp.swift:174-182)
• The target app is resolved as activeApps.first ?? visibleApps.first, i.e. the first sorted app that is audible or OS-frontmost (AppStore.swift:1212-1213)
• Volume step defaults to 0.1, is clamped to [0.01, 0.5], and the new volume is clamped to a max of 1.0 (AppStore.swift:1216-1217)
• setDesiredVolume + commitDesiredVolume are called so the change is applied to the backend and persisted (AppStore.swift:1218-1219)
• An info toast 'Volume increased — <App>: <NN>%' is shown for 1 second (AppStore.swift:1221-1226)
• Action is a no-op when preferences.enableKeyboardShortcuts is false (AppStore.swift:1211) or when there is no active/visible app (AppStore.swift:1213)
• Keystrokes are ignored while the key window's first responder is an NSTextView or NSTextField, so typing is not hijacked (WavesApp.swift:169-172)
Acceptance Criteria:
• With shortcuts enabled and an audible app present, pressing ⌘⌥↑ raises that app's desiredVolume by ~10% and shows a 'Volume increased' toast
• Repeated presses never push volume above 100% (1.0)
• Pressing ⌘⌥↑ with shortcuts disabled produces no change and no toast
• Pressing ⌘⌥↑ while focus is in a text field does not change any app volume
• With no apps in the session, pressing ⌘⌥↑ is a silent no-op (no crash, no toast)
Impl Notes: Wired end-to-end and commits/persists correctly, but 'frontmost app' is mis-resolved: the target is activeApps.first AFTER sorting (by name/activity/category, AppStore.swift:1278-1297), not the OS-focused app. activeApps is visibleApps.filter(\.isActive) where isActive = isAudible || isFrontmost (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1177-1189), so when several apps are audible the hotkey adjusts whichever happens to sort first, not the app the user is actually using. In Name sort, that's effectively alphabetical — a real correctness/UX bug versus the README's 'frontmost app' promise (README.md:122).
Errors Found:
• [HIGH/correctness] "Frontmost app" is resolved as the sort-first active app, not the OS-focused app (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1212) — increaseVolumeForFrontmostApp resolves the target as activeApps.first ?? visibleApps.first (AppStore.swift:1212). activeApps is visibleApps.filter(\.isActive) (AppStore.swift:113-115) and isActive is set to isAudible || isFrontmost in the backend (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1176-1189). visibleApps is run through sortedApps (AppStore.swift:103-107): in .name sort the comparator is purely alphabetical (AppStore.swift:1323-1325); in .activity sort audible apps (activityRank 0/1) outrank a silent frontmost app (rank 2, AppStore.swift:1303-1318). So whenever an audible app exists alongside the focused-but-silent app, ⌘⌥↑ raises the sort-first/audible app, not the focused one. Re-read and reproduced: the ordering and isActive definitions are exactly as the first tester described. | Fix: Resolve the target from NSWorkspace.shared.frontmostApplication's pid/bundleID and match it against session.apps, falling back to the first audible app only when no managed app matches. Do not rely on sorted activeApps ordering for 'frontmost'.
• [HIGH/error-handling] Hotkey re-taps and shows a false "Volume increased" toast for an EXCLUDED app (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1218) — When activeApps.first resolves to an excluded app (excluded apps remain in session.apps/visibleApps), setDesiredVolume early-returns at AppStore.swift:357 so session state and pendingVolumeTargets are NOT updated. But increaseVolumeForFrontmostApp still calls commitDesiredVolume(for: app) (AppStore.swift:1219). In commitDesiredVolume (AppStore.swift:384-392) pendingVolumeTargets[appKey] is nil, so it sets it to app.desiredVolume and schedules an immediate apply, which calls backend.setDesiredVolume (AppStore.swift:427). The backend has no exclusion concept and applyRoute sets routingState=.managed (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:65-72), re-engaging a tap on an app the user explicitly excluded. On top of that, the unconditional 'Volume increased — <App>: <NN>%' toast (AppStore.swift:1221-1226) and the async 'Managed route active' toast (AppStore.swift:433-438) both fire. So this is worse than the first tester's 'false toast' framing: the exclusion guarantee is violated. Story EXCL criterion says the excluded app's audio must not change. | Fix: Guard the hotkey on !isExcluded(app) before computing/applying and toasting (skip or show an 'App excluded' info toast). Do not call commitDesiredVolume when setDesiredVolume was a no-op; have setDesiredVolume report success so the commit and toast only run on an actual change.
• [MEDIUM/ux] Confirmation toast is invisible in the exact scenario the hotkey targets (background use) (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1221) — increaseVolumeForFrontmostApp shows a 'Volume increased' toast (AppStore.swift:1221-1226), but toasts render only inside MainWindowView (MainWindowView.swift:59) and the MenuBarExtra popover (MenuBarMixerView.swift:105) via AppToastStack (AppToasts.swift:3-14). The global hotkey is designed to fire while the user is working in another app (Waves backgrounded, popover closed), so neither toast surface is on screen — the user gets no visible confirmation at the moment the hotkey acts. This is a missed defect: the feedback the code builds is never seen in the feature's primary use case, and combined with the silent-failure paths (excluded app, denied Accessibility) the user has no signal whether anything happened. | Fix: Show an OS-level HUD/notification (or a borderless floating overlay) for hotkey-driven changes so feedback is visible while Waves is in the background; the in-window toast is insufficient for global shortcuts.
• [LOW/correctness] Text-field guard cannot protect text fields in other apps (global monitor only fires when Waves is not frontmost) (Sources/Waves/App/WavesApp.swift:169-172) — handleGlobalKeyEvent guards typing by checking NSApp.keyWindow?.firstResponder for NSTextView/NSTextField (WavesApp.swift:169-172). But the monitor is NSEvent.addGlobalMonitorForEvents (WavesApp.swift:152), which by design only delivers events while Waves is NOT the active app. While the user types in another app, Waves is backgrounded, so NSApp.keyWindow is Waves's own window or nil — never the other app's editor. The guard therefore inspects the wrong responder. Confirmed correct. Downgraded to low: the ⌘⌥+Arrow/M combos rarely collide with text editing, so real-world impact is small, but the acceptance criterion as written is unmet. | Fix: Detect the focused element in the frontmost app via the Accessibility API (AXUIElement focused-element role) rather than NSApp.keyWindow, or accept the limitation and update the spec/docs.

Feature: Global volume-down hotkey (⌘⌥↓)
User Story: As a Waves user, I want to press ⌘⌥↓ from anywhere to lower the volume of the app I'm using, so that I can quickly duck a noisy app without leaving my current window.
Expected Behavior:
• On keyCode 125 (Down arrow) with Command+Option, AppDelegate calls store.decreaseVolumeForFrontmostApp() (WavesApp.swift:183)
• Target app resolution is identical to volume-up: activeApps.first ?? visibleApps.first (AppStore.swift:1231-1232)
• Step defaults to 0.1, clamped to [0.01, 0.5]; new volume is clamped to a minimum of 0.0 (AppStore.swift:1235-1236)
• setDesiredVolume + commitDesiredVolume apply and persist the lower volume (AppStore.swift:1237-1238)
• An info toast 'Volume decreased — <App>: <NN>%' is shown for 1 second (AppStore.swift:1240-1245)
• No-op when shortcuts disabled or no app available (AppStore.swift:1230-1232); ignored while typing in a text field (WavesApp.swift:169-172)
Acceptance Criteria:
• Pressing ⌘⌥↓ lowers the resolved app's volume by ~10% and shows a 'Volume decreased' toast
• Repeated presses never push volume below 0% (0.0)
• Disabled shortcuts or empty session yields no change and no toast
• Focus in a text field suppresses the shortcut
Impl Notes: Same frontmost-app mis-resolution as KEY-01 (activeApps.first after sorting rather than the OS-focused app). Otherwise complete and symmetric with the increase path; lower bound correctly clamps at 0.0.
Errors Found:
• [HIGH/correctness] "Frontmost app" mis-resolution (same defect as KEY-01) on the volume-down path (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1231) — decreaseVolumeForFrontmostApp uses the identical resolution activeApps.first ?? visibleApps.first (AppStore.swift:1231). Confirmed against the same sort/isActive code paths as KEY-01. When an audible app coexists with a silent focused app, ⌘⌥↓ ducks the sort-first/audible app, not the focused one. | Fix: Same fix as KEY-01: resolve the target from NSWorkspace.shared.frontmostApplication and match it to a managed app before falling back to the first audible app.
• [HIGH/error-handling] Hotkey re-taps and shows a false "Volume decreased" toast for an EXCLUDED app (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1237) — Mirror of KEY-01's excluded-app issue on the down path: setDesiredVolume early-returns for excluded apps (AppStore.swift:357), but decreaseVolumeForFrontmostApp still calls commitDesiredVolume (AppStore.swift:1238), which writes a pendingVolumeTarget and schedules backend.setDesiredVolume, re-engaging a tap (routingState=.managed, WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:65-72) on the excluded app, plus shows 'Volume decreased' (AppStore.swift:1240-1245) and 'Managed route active' toasts. | Fix: Guard on !isExcluded(app) before applying/committing/toasting; do not commit when setDesiredVolume no-ops.
• [MEDIUM/ux] Confirmation toast is invisible during background use (volume-down path) (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1240) — Same as KEY-01: the 'Volume decreased' toast (AppStore.swift:1240-1245) renders only inside MainWindowView/MenuBarMixerView (AppToasts.swift), which are not on screen when the global hotkey fires from another app, so a background ⌘⌥↓ produces no visible feedback. | Fix: Use an OS-level HUD/notification or floating overlay for hotkey-driven changes.
• [LOW/correctness] Text-field guard is ineffective for typing in other apps on the volume-down path (Sources/Waves/App/WavesApp.swift:169-172) — Same root cause as KEY-01: the global monitor only fires when Waves is backgrounded, so NSApp.keyWindow?.firstResponder (WavesApp.swift:169-172) never sees the foreground app's text field and ⌘⌥↓ is not suppressed while typing in another app. Confirmed; downgraded to low because the combo rarely collides with typing. | Fix: Detect the frontmost app's focused element via the Accessibility API instead of NSApp.keyWindow, or document the limitation.

Feature: Global mute-toggle hotkey (⌘⌥M)
User Story: As a Waves user, I want to press ⌘⌥M from anywhere to instantly mute or unmute the app I'm using, so that I can silence an app without hunting for its slider.
Expected Behavior:
• On keyCode 46 (M) with Command+Option, AppDelegate calls store.toggleMuteForFrontmostApp() (WavesApp.swift:184-185)
• Target app resolution is activeApps.first ?? visibleApps.first (AppStore.swift:1250-1251)
• The new mute state is the inverse of the resolved app's current isMuted (AppStore.swift:1253)
• setMuted(newMutedState, for: app) is invoked, which marks muteSource = .user, updates appliedVolume, calls backend.setMuted, and persists (AppStore.swift:1254, 491-541)
• An info toast 'Muted'/'Unmuted' with the app name is shown for 1 second (AppStore.swift:1256-1261); setMuted also raises its own 'App muted/unmuted' success toast on backend completion (AppStore.swift:528-533)
• No mute step gate beyond enableKeyboardShortcuts; excluded apps are protected because setMuted early-returns on isExcluded(app) (AppStore.swift:492)
• Ignored while typing in a text field (WavesApp.swift:169-172)
Acceptance Criteria:
• Pressing ⌘⌥M on an unmuted resolved app mutes it and shows a 'Muted' toast; pressing again unmutes it
• A user-initiated mute via this shortcut sets muteSource to .user so auto-resume does not override it
• Pressing ⌘⌥M with shortcuts disabled does nothing
• If the resolved app is excluded, no mute change occurs
Impl Notes: Functionally complete and backend-applied. Two concerns: (1) same frontmost-app mis-resolution as KEY-01/02. (2) Double-toast — toggleMuteForFrontmostApp shows an immediate info toast (AppStore.swift:1256) and setMuted shows a second success toast asynchronously (AppStore.swift:528), so a single ⌘⌥M can surface two overlapping toasts. (3) Unlike volume, toggleMuteForFrontmostApp does not itself check enableKeyboardShortcuts redundantly at the top before reading state — it does (line 1249) — fine; but note the optimistic toast fires even if backend.setMuted later fails (which only then shows an error toast).
Errors Found:
• [HIGH/correctness] "Frontmost app" mis-resolution (same defect as KEY-01) on the mute-toggle path (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1250) — toggleMuteForFrontmostApp resolves activeApps.first ?? visibleApps.first (AppStore.swift:1250). Confirmed against the same sort/isActive paths. When several apps are audible, ⌘⌥M mutes the sort-first/audible app rather than the focused app. | Fix: Resolve via NSWorkspace.shared.frontmostApplication matched to a managed app before falling back to the first audible app.
• [MEDIUM/ux] Double toast on a single ⌘⌥M press (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1256) — toggleMuteForFrontmostApp shows an immediate info toast 'Muted'/'Unmuted' (AppStore.swift:1256-1261). The setMuted it calls then shows a second, asynchronous success toast 'App muted'/'App unmuted' on backend completion (AppStore.swift:528-533). showToast appends to the toast stack (AppStore.swift:1455), so one keypress surfaces two toasts for the same action. Confirmed by reading both toast sites and showToast. | Fix: Drop the optimistic toast in toggleMuteForFrontmostApp and rely on setMuted's success/error toast, or add a parameter to setMuted to suppress its own toast when invoked from the hotkey.
• [MEDIUM/error-handling] Optimistic 'Muted'/'Unmuted' toast fires even when the mute is rejected (excluded app) or the backend later fails (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1254) — toggleMuteForFrontmostApp shows the toast unconditionally (AppStore.swift:1256). setMuted early-returns for excluded apps before the backend Task (AppStore.swift:492) — so unlike the volume path, the mute path does NOT re-tap an excluded app, but the optimistic 'Muted'/'Unmuted' toast still claims a change that did not happen. Separately, if backend.setMuted fails the user sees the optimistic 'Muted' toast plus a later 'Mute toggle failed' error toast (AppStore.swift:539) — contradictory feedback. Confirmed. | Fix: Check isExcluded(app) in toggleMuteForFrontmostApp and skip/replace the toast; defer the success toast to setMuted's confirmed completion path instead of showing it optimistically.
• [MEDIUM/ux] Mute confirmation toast(s) invisible during background use (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1256) — Both the optimistic 'Muted'/'Unmuted' toast (AppStore.swift:1256-1261) and setMuted's 'App muted'/'App unmuted' toast (AppStore.swift:528-533) render only inside MainWindowView/MenuBarMixerView (AppToasts.swift), which are not on screen when ⌘⌥M is pressed from another app. So the user muting the focused app from another window gets no visible confirmation. | Fix: Surface hotkey mute feedback via an OS-level HUD/notification or floating overlay rather than the in-window toast stack.
• [LOW/correctness] Text-field guard ineffective for typing in other apps on the mute-toggle path (Sources/Waves/App/WavesApp.swift:169-172) — Same root cause as KEY-01: the global monitor only fires while Waves is backgrounded, so the NSApp.keyWindow?.firstResponder check (WavesApp.swift:169-172) cannot see the foreground app's text field and ⌘⌥M is not suppressed while typing elsewhere. Confirmed; downgraded to low given collision rarity. | Fix: Use the Accessibility API focused-element role of the frontmost app, or document the limitation.

Feature: Enable/disable global shortcuts toggle with lazy monitor install
User Story: As a privacy-conscious user, I want a single setting to turn global shortcuts on or off, so that Waves only observes my system-wide keystrokes when I actually want the hotkeys.
Expected Behavior:
• Settings exposes an 'Enable keyboard shortcuts' toggle bound to setKeyboardShortcutsEnabled (SettingsView.swift:98-103)
• enableKeyboardShortcuts defaults to true and is persisted via PreferencesStore (UserPreferences.swift:11, 30, 56)
• setKeyboardShortcutsEnabled writes the preference, persists it, and posts .wavesKeyboardShortcutsPreferenceChanged (AppStore.swift:1272-1276)
• AppDelegate observes that notification and calls updateGlobalHotkeysState, installing the global monitor only when enabled and removing it when disabled (WavesApp.swift:91-112)
• setupGlobalHotkeys guards against duplicate monitors (eventMonitor == nil) and removeGlobalHotkeys tears down the monitor (WavesApp.swift:150-162)
• The monitor is also removed on app termination (WavesApp.swift:118-119)
• When the toggle is on, a read-only reference card listing ⌘⌥↑ / ⌘⌥↓ / ⌘⌥M appears in Settings (SettingsView.swift:127-160)
Acceptance Criteria:
• Turning the toggle off removes the global event monitor so no system-wide keystrokes are observed
• Turning it back on re-installs exactly one monitor (no duplicate monitors after repeated toggling)
• The preference survives app relaunch (default true on first launch)
• The ⌘⌥ shortcut reference list is shown only while the toggle is enabled
• On quit, the monitor is removed
Impl Notes: Clean lazy install/teardown keyed off a NotificationCenter event, with duplicate-monitor guard and termination cleanup. The Settings reference list is purely informational (the shortcuts are hard-coded keyCodes; no remapping UI). Note the default is ON (UserPreferences.swift:11), so the global monitor is installed by default at first launch — reasonable but worth noting for a privacy-default review.

Feature: Accessibility permission requirement for global hotkeys
User Story: As a new user, I want Waves to tell me that global shortcuts need Accessibility permission and point me to System Settings, so that I understand why ⌘⌥ hotkeys don't work until I grant it.
Expected Behavior:
• Onboarding presents an optional Accessibility step stating per-app routing works without it but global shortcuts/app-control helpers need it (OnboardingView.swift:25)
• The onboarding action for that step opens the Accessibility pane in System Settings via x-apple.systempreferences URL (OnboardingView.swift:167-170)
• Permission state is read with AXIsProcessTrusted() and stored as onboarding.accessibilityPermissionGranted (AppStore.swift:1359, 1499)
• Diagnostics report an 'Accessibility permission' check, passed when AXIsProcessTrusted() is true and a warning otherwise, with guidance text about global shortcuts (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:258-262, 318-319)
• README documents that Accessibility permission is required only for global shortcuts and lists it under troubleshooting (README.md:65, 187-190)
Acceptance Criteria:
• Without Accessibility permission granted, the onboarding step and diagnostics surface a warning explaining global shortcuts need it
• Tapping the onboarding action opens the macOS Accessibility settings pane
• Granting permission flips the diagnostics check to passed and accessibilityPermissionGranted to true after the next sync/refresh
• Per-app volume routing remains usable without this permission
