Impl Notes: Permission is surfaced in onboarding and diagnostics, but there is a functional gap at the hotkey layer: setupGlobalHotkeys (WavesApp.swift:150-155) installs the global NSEvent monitor regardless of AXIsProcessTrusted(). macOS will NOT deliver global keyDown events to that monitor without Accessibility permission, so with the shortcuts toggle ON but permission denied, the hotkeys silently do nothing and no toast/warning fires at press time. Nothing re-checks permission or prompts when the user enables shortcuts, and there is no AXIsProcessTrustedWithOptions prompt-on-demand. Users must self-diagnose via the diagnostics report or README troubleshooting.
Errors Found: • [HIGH/error-handling] Global hotkeys silently do nothing when Accessibility is denied — no press-time feedback, prompt, or recheck (Sources/Waves/App/WavesApp.swift:150-155) — setupGlobalHotkeys installs the NSEvent.addGlobalMonitorForEvents keyDown monitor whenever shortcuts are enabled (WavesApp.swift:150-155), with no AXIsProcessTrusted() check. macOS does not deliver global keyDown events to that monitor without Accessibility/Input-Monitoring permission, so with the toggle ON but permission denied the monitor's callback never fires and handleGlobalKeyEvent never runs — no toast, no warning at press time. setKeyboardShortcutsEnabled (AppStore.swift:1272-1276) and updateGlobalHotkeysState (WavesApp.swift:105-112) neither recheck permission nor call AXIsProcessTrustedWithOptions to prompt when the user turns shortcuts on. AX is only read in onboarding sync (AppStore.swift:1359) and diagnostics (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:257-263) — verified there is no AX reference in WavesApp.swift. The user must self-diagnose. Confirmed. | Fix: When shortcuts are enabled (setKeyboardShortcutsEnabled / updateGlobalHotkeysState), check AXIsProcessTrusted(); if false, call AXIsProcessTrustedWithOptions([kAXTrustedCheckOptionPrompt: true]) to prompt, and/or show a warning toast linking to System Settings so the user is told the hotkeys won't fire until permission is granted.

Feature: Opt-in toggle for URL scheme automation
User Story: As a privacy-conscious Waves user, I want waves:// automation to be off by default and only enabled when I explicitly turn it on, so that no other app or link can control my audio without my consent.
Expected Behavior:
• UserPreferences exposes enableURLScheme (default false) and a separate urlSchemeAutomationAcknowledged flag (default false).
• Settings > General shows a 'URL scheme automation' Toggle bound to store.preferences.enableURLScheme.
• Flipping the toggle sets enableURLScheme to the new value, sets urlSchemeAutomationAcknowledged = true, and persists preferences immediately.
• The toggle has a help tooltip explaining it lets other apps and links control Waves through waves:// URLs (set volume, mute, apply presets) and that it is off by default.
• On AppStore init, if urlSchemeAutomationAcknowledged is false the store force-resets enableURLScheme to false and sets acknowledged = true, then persists — a one-time migration so any pre-existing 'enabled' value from before the opt-in gate cannot silently leave automation on.
• handleURLScheme bails out immediately (logging a warning) if preferences.enableURLScheme is false, so the entire command surface is inert until opt-in.
• The CFBundleURLTypes / CFBundleURLSchemes 'waves' registration is present in the shipped Info.plist so macOS routes waves:// URLs to the app even though handling is gated in code.
Acceptance Criteria:
• On a fresh install, preferences.enableURLScheme == false and the Settings toggle renders off.
• Invoking any waves:// URL while enableURLScheme == false produces no audio change and logs 'URL scheme invocation rejected because URL schemes are disabled'.
• Toggling the Settings switch on persists enableURLScheme == true and urlSchemeAutomationAcknowledged == true to disk.
• A preferences file with enableURLScheme == true but urlSchemeAutomationAcknowledged == false (or absent) loads with enableURLScheme forced back to false after init.
• Encoding/decoding round-trips enableURLScheme correctly (covered by PersistedSchemaTests / UserPreferencesTests).
Impl Notes: Solid opt-in gate. Default off, persisted, gated at both the AppDelegate routing layer and the AppStore handler. The acknowledged-migration is a thoughtful safety net. Minor UX gap: the toggle is a bare switch with only a tooltip — there is no inline subtitle or warning shown in the Settings UI itself (the security caveat lives only in README and the hover help), so a user enabling it sees no in-app explanation of the risk. Two persisted booleans (enableURLScheme + urlSchemeAutomationAcknowledged) is slightly redundant now that the migration has run, but harmless.

Feature: set-volume URL command
User Story: As an automation user, I want to set a specific app's volume via a waves://set-volume URL, so that scripts, Shortcuts, or Stream Deck buttons can adjust per-app levels without me opening the mixer.
Expected Behavior:
• waves://set-volume?app=APP_ID&volume=0.5 is routed to handleURLSetVolume when automation is enabled.
• Requires both an 'app' query item and a 'volume' query item; app value must be <= 256 chars and volume value <= 32 chars (length caps to bound parsing of hostile input).
• volume must parse as Float and lie in the closed range 0.0...1.0; otherwise the command is rejected with a 'URL command blocked — Set-volume command was invalid.' warning toast.
• The target app is resolved via first(matchingAppKey:), which matches either the process id or the logicalID (bundle-style key), so APP_ID can be either identifier.
• On a match, it calls setDesiredVolume(volume, for: app) then commitDesiredVolume(for: app) to apply and persist the level.
• If no app matches, it shows a 'URL command blocked — App not found: <appID prefix>' warning toast (appID truncated to 64 chars in the message).
Acceptance Criteria:
• waves://set-volume?app=<validKey>&volume=0.3 with automation on sets that app's volume to 0.3 and commits it.
• volume=1.5, volume=-0.1, volume=abc, or a missing volume/app param each yield no volume change and a 'Set-volume command was invalid.' toast.
• An app/volume value exceeding the length caps is rejected as invalid.
• A well-formed command targeting an unknown app yields an 'App not found' toast and no change.
• Matching works when APP_ID is supplied as either the runtime id or the logicalID.

Feature: mute URL command
User Story: As an automation user, I want to mute or unmute a specific app via a waves://mute URL, so that I can silence or restore an app from a script or hotkey tool.
Expected Behavior:
• waves://mute?app=APP_ID&muted=true is routed to handleURLMute when automation is enabled.
• Requires both an 'app' query item (<= 256 chars) and a 'muted' query item (<= 16 chars) that parses as a Swift Bool ('true'/'false'); otherwise rejected with 'URL command blocked — Mute command was invalid.' warning toast.
• Resolves the target app via first(matchingAppKey:) (id or logicalID).
• On a match, calls setMuted(shouldMute, for: app) to apply the mute state.
• If no app matches, shows 'URL command blocked — App not found: <appID prefix>' warning toast.
Acceptance Criteria:
• waves://mute?app=<validKey>&muted=true mutes that app; muted=false unmutes it.
• muted=1, muted=yes, muted=TRUE-with-extra, or missing muted/app param each yield no change and a 'Mute command was invalid.' toast (Bool() accepts only 'true'/'false').
• An over-length app or muted value is rejected as invalid.
• A valid command for an unknown app yields an 'App not found' toast and no change.

Feature: apply-preset and refresh URL commands
User Story: As an automation user, I want to apply a named preset or refresh the audio session via waves:// URLs, so that I can switch my whole mixer configuration or force a re-scan from an external trigger.
Expected Behavior:
• waves://apply-preset?name=Focus is routed to handleURLApplyPreset; requires a 'name' query item <= 256 chars or it rejects with 'URL command blocked — Preset command was invalid.' warning toast.
• Preset lookup is case-insensitive (localizedCaseInsensitiveCompare) against the saved presets list.
• On a match it calls applyPreset(preset); if no preset matches it shows 'URL command blocked — Preset not found: <name prefix>' warning toast (name truncated to 64 chars).
• waves://refresh is routed directly to refresh() to re-scan the audio session, with no parameters required.
• Any waves:// host other than set-volume / mute / apply-preset / refresh logs 'URL scheme invoked with unknown host' and does nothing else.
Acceptance Criteria:
• waves://apply-preset?name=focus applies a preset literally named 'Focus' (case-insensitive match).
• A missing name param yields a 'Preset command was invalid.' toast; an unknown name yields a 'Preset not found' toast.
• waves://refresh triggers a session refresh equivalent to the Refresh command.
• An unrecognized host (e.g. waves://foo) produces no action and only a warning log.
• The successful host name is logged at info level (host is .public).

Feature: URL command rate limiting and routing/parse safety
User Story: As a user who has enabled automation, I want Waves to throttle and sanity-check incoming waves:// URLs, so that a flood of commands or a malformed/foreign URL cannot hammer my audio state or exhaust the command budget.
Expected Behavior:
• AppDelegate registers a Get-URL Apple Event handler at launch (setupURLSchemeHandler) and also implements application(_:open:), both funneling into a private handleURLScheme that drops any URL whose scheme != 'waves' before forwarding to the store.
• The handler is removed on applicationWillTerminate (removeURLSchemeHandler).
• AppStore.handleURLScheme requires scheme == 'waves', a parseable URLComponents, and a non-nil host; malformed URLs are dropped WITHOUT charging the rate limit.
• A sliding-window rate limit allows at most maxURLSchemeRequests (10) well-formed commands per urlSchemeRequestWindow (60s); checkURLSchemeRateLimit prunes timestamps older than the window, rejects when count >= 10 (logging 'rate limit was exceeded'), else records the timestamp.
• The rate limit is charged only AFTER the URL is confirmed well-formed (has a valid host), so malformed-URL spam cannot exhaust the quota for legitimate commands.
• Input length caps (app <= 256, volume <= 32, muted <= 16, preset name <= 256) and value truncation in toasts (prefix 64) bound the impact of hostile payloads.
Acceptance Criteria:
• A non-waves:// URL delivered to the app is ignored at the AppDelegate layer and never reaches the store handler.
• After 10 valid waves:// commands within 60s, the 11th is rejected with a rate-limit warning log and no audio change; commands succeed again once older entries age out of the window.
• Sending many malformed waves:// URLs (no host, or unparseable) does not consume rate-limit slots, so a valid command sent afterward still executes.
• A waves:// URL with a missing host is dropped silently (no toast, no rate-limit charge).
• The Apple Event Get-URL handler is installed at finish-launching and removed at terminate.

Impl Notes: Thoughtful defense-in-depth: scheme check at two layers, well-formedness gate before the rate limiter, sliding-window throttle, and input length caps. The deliberate ordering (charge rate limit only for well-formed commands) is documented in a comment and is the correct choice. Gaps: (1) the rate limit is a single global counter, so legitimate bursty automation hitting >10 cmds/min is silently dropped with only a log and no toast — the caller has no feedback that throttling occurred. (2) No automated tests exercise handleURLScheme parsing/rate-limit logic (only the enableURLScheme persistence is tested), so this security-relevant path is unverified by the suite. (3) Commands execute on whatever AudioApp keys currently exist; there is no confirmation prompt, so once enabled any local process can drive volume/mute within the rate budget — acceptable for an opt-in local-automation feature but the trust boundary rests entirely on the opt-in toggle.

So 'waves://' binds host="", passes the gate, reaches checkURLSchemeRateLimit() (consuming a sliding-window slot at lines 270/345-353), then falls into the `switch host` default branch (logged as unknown host, no action). Flooding 10x 'waves://' within 60s exhausts the quota so the 11th legitimate command is rejected. Feature: Auto-pause media apps when a conferencing app becomes frontmost
User Story: As someone who keeps music playing while I work, I want Waves to automatically mute my media apps the moment I jump into a video call, so that I don't blast Spotify into a meeting and have to scramble for the mute button.
Expected Behavior:
• A frontmost-application-change observer (NSWorkspace.didActivateApplicationNotification) fires checkAutoPauseMusic() whenever the active app changes (AppStore.swift:886-897).
• checkAutoPauseMusic() reads the LIVE frontmost app (NSWorkspace.shared.frontmostApplication) rather than the periodically-refreshed session snapshot, then classifies it via AppDiscoveryPolicy.inferCategory (AppStore.swift:945-953).
• Conferencing is detected purely by keyword match on bundleID/displayName: 'zoom', 'meet', 'teams', 'webex', or 'facetime' (AppDiscoveryPolicy.swift:38-42).
• When a conferencing app is frontmost, all visible apps with category == .media that are currently unmuted and not excluded are muted via backend.setMuted(true,...) (AppStore.swift:957-965).
• Each auto-muted app is tagged muteSource = .autoConferencing and its logicalID added to the pausedMusicApps set (AppStore.swift:963-964).
• Affected apps' isMuted/muteSource/appliedVolume are updated in place (not by replacing the whole snapshot) and the session is persisted (AppStore.swift:986-997).
• An early guard returns if the frontmost bundle id equals previousFrontmostApp, so re-activating the same conferencing app does not re-run the pause loop (AppStore.swift:947-948).
• checkAutoPauseMusic also runs on startup, on refresh, and after applying a preset (AppStore.swift:219, 248, 1014).
Acceptance Criteria:
• Given the toggle is on and Spotify (category .media) is playing unmuted, when Zoom becomes the frontmost app, Spotify is muted and its muteSource becomes .autoConferencing.
• Given an app is already muted by the user, when a conferencing app activates, that app's muteSource stays .user (it is excluded by the !isMuted filter) and is not re-touched.
• Given an app is in the exclusion list, when a conferencing app activates, that app is never muted (isExcluded filter).
• Given Zoom is already frontmost and re-activates, when the notification fires again with the same bundle id, checkAutoPauseMusic returns early without re-muting.
• A media app whose category is correctly .media is the only kind of app paused; conferencing/communication/browser apps are left untouched.
Impl Notes: Core path is wired and works for native conferencing apps. Two real detection gaps: (1) browser-based calls (Google Meet, Teams-in-browser, Zoom web client) make the frontmost app Chrome/Safari, which infers .browser, so the call is never detected and music is never paused. (2) Detection is keyword-only and frontmost-only — it never inspects whether the mic is actually live, so simply clicking a Zoom window (no active call) pauses music, and a call running in a background window while another app is frontmost is not detected. There is no UI feedback (toast/badge) telling the user Waves auto-paused something, so a silenced media app can look like a bug.

• [HIGH/logic] previousFrontmostApp guard makes the refresh/startup/preset re-check paths no-ops while a conferencing app stays frontmost (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:947) — checkAutoPauseMusic() returns early at the guard currentFrontmostApp != previousFrontmostApp (AppStore.swift:947) before evaluating any pause/resume work. The function is also invoked from startup (line 219), refresh (line 248) and applyPreset (line 1014), but those paths do not change the frontmost app, so once previousFrontmostApp has latched the live conferencing bundle id the guard short-circuits every such call. Concrete trigger: already on a Zoom call (Zoom frontmost, music paused), launch Spotify and start playing; app discovery fires refresh() -> checkAutoPauseMusic() -> guard hits (Zoom == Zoom) -> returns, so the newly playing Spotify is never auto-paused and music blasts into the meeting. Same for applyPreset() unmuting a media app mid-call. Verified the guard is keyed purely on frontmost-bundle equality with no state-delta fallback. | Fix: Restrict the previousFrontmostApp dedupe to the didActivateApplication path only. For refresh/startup/applyPreset, call a variant that skips the dedupe, or replace the guard with a desired-state diff so re-entrant calls with the same frontmost still pause newly-eligible media apps.
• [HIGH/edge-case] Browser-based calls (Google Meet, Zoom web, Teams-in-browser) are never detected, so music is never paused (Sources/WavesAudioCore/Backend/AppDiscoveryPolicy.swift:30-42) — inferCategory classifies the frontmost app: the browser branch (AppDiscoveryPolicy.swift:30-36) matches safari/chrome/firefox/arc/browser and returns .browser BEFORE the conferencing branch (lines 38-42) is reached. A call running in Chrome/Safari/Arc makes the frontmost app the browser, classified .browser, so isConferencingAppActive stays false and music keeps playing during the meeting. Confirmed the branch ordering and that classification is on the browser app's own name/bundle, not the tab. This is a documented design limitation (frontmost+keyword model), but it silently defeats a very common class of calls. | Fix: Detect active microphone/CoreAudio input use (or a known meeting URL) rather than equating frontmost-app category with call state. At minimum document the browser-call limitation in the Settings copy.

Other mutations in this store surface a toast (mute toggle at 528-533, preset at 1015-1020). A user who hears Spotify go silent on entering a call gets no in-app indication Waves did it; the muted row is indistinguishable from an unexpected mute. Confirmed there is no .autoConferencing-specific UI affordance anywhere. | Fix: Show a transient toast ('Auto-paused Spotify for your call') with a 'Keep playing' / 'Disable' affordance, and/or render a distinct badge for muteSource == .autoConferencing rows.

Both bodies are MainActor-isolated but suspend at each await, so the relative ordering of pausedMusicApps.insert (line 963) versus pausedMusicApps.removeAll() (line 983) and of the two backend.setMuted calls is not deterministic, leaving pausedMusicApps potentially inconsistent with muteSource. Impact is bounded because resume reads muteSource, not the set, but the final audible mute state can reflect whichever task's setMuted completed last. | Fix: Serialize passes: retain the in-flight Task and cancel/await it before starting a new one, so each frontmost change deterministically supersedes the previous.

Feature: Auto-resume the apps Waves paused once the call ends
User Story: As a user whose music was auto-paused for a call, I want Waves to un-mute exactly the apps it paused once I leave the conferencing app, so that my music comes back on its own without restoring tracks I had intentionally muted.
Expected Behavior:
• When the frontmost app is NOT conferencing, checkAutoPauseMusic resumes apps that are muted AND have muteSource == .autoConferencing AND are not excluded, via backend.setMuted(false,...) (AppStore.swift:970-982).
• Resume is intentionally scoped to .autoConferencing-tagged apps so a user's own mutes are never overridden (comment + filter at AppStore.swift:971-973).
• After resuming, the app's muteSource is reset to .user and pausedMusicApps is cleared entirely (AppStore.swift:977, 983).
• appliedVolume is restored to desiredVolume on unmute via the in-place muteChanges application (AppStore.swift:993).
• The same previousFrontmostApp early-return guard prevents repeated resume passes when the frontmost app hasn't changed (AppStore.swift:947-948).
Acceptance Criteria:
• Given Waves auto-paused Spotify for a Zoom call, when the user switches away from Zoom to any non-conferencing app, Spotify is unmuted and its appliedVolume returns to desiredVolume.
• Given the user manually muted Apple Music before the call, when the call ends, Apple Music stays muted (muteSource .user is never resumed).
• After a resume pass, pausedMusicApps is empty.
• An excluded app is never unmuted by the resume pass even if it somehow carries an .autoConferencing tag.
Impl Notes: Resume logic is correct for the simple case but has edge-case gaps. (1) On resume the app is retagged muteSource = .user (line 977) — semantically odd: an app Waves muted and Waves unmuted is now indistinguishable from a user action, which is only safe because pausedMusicApps is also cleared. (2) 'Not conferencing frontmost' is the resume trigger, so switching from Zoom to Finder mid-call (call still running) resumes music while you are still talking — the feature equates 'conferencing app not frontmost' with 'call over', which is frequently false. (3) Because detection keys off the frontmost-change notification, if you quit the conferencing app rather than switching away (didTerminate, not didActivate), resume depends on the next frontmost activation firing; there is no resume hook in handleAppTermination.

• [HIGH/logic] Resume equates 'conferencing app not frontmost' with 'call over' — music returns mid-call (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:970-982) — The resume branch runs whenever the live frontmost app is not conferencing (AppStore.swift:970-984). During an active call users routinely switch to notes/browser/Finder while still talking. The moment focus leaves the Zoom window, isConferencingAppActive becomes false and Waves unmutes every .autoConferencing app, so music resumes while the call is still live — re-creating the problem the feature exists to prevent. Confirmed the resume trigger is purely frontmost-category, with no call/mic-state check. | Fix: Decouple resume from frontmost focus: resume only when the call truly ends (conferencing app terminated, or mic/input no longer in use).
• [MEDIUM/error-handling] No resume hook when the conferencing app is quit rather than switched away from (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:915-938) — Resume is driven solely by the didActivateApplication frontmost-change notification (observeFrontmostAppChanges, line 886-895). handleAppTermination (AppStore.swift:915-938) releases controllers and prunes pausedMusicApps but never calls checkAutoPauseMusic(). If the conferencing app quits/crashes and macOS does not promptly activate another app, no resume pass fires and music stays muted until the next manual app activation. In practice macOS usually activates another app on quit (which would fire didActivate and self-heal), so this is an edge case — hence 'likely' rather than always-reproducible. | Fix: In handleAppTermination, if the terminated app was conferencing or any .autoConferencing-muted apps remain, invoke a resume pass directly instead of waiting for the next activation.
• [LOW/inconsistency] Resume retags muteSource to .user, erasing provenance of a Waves-applied auto-pause (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:977) — On resume, an app Waves muted and Waves unmuted is tagged muteSource = .user (AppStore.swift:977, applied at :992). This is only safe because pausedMusicApps is cleared in the same pass (line 983); the two sources of truth must stay in lockstep. The codebase itself notes this is harmless for an app being unmuted (its provenance reverts to the default .user). A code smell rather than a functional bug, but if the two representations ever diverge (e.g. via the PAUSE-01 interleaving) an app could appear user-controlled when it was machine-controlled. | Fix: Leave muteSource at a neutral value on auto-resume (or drop the duplicate pausedMusicApps set and derive paused state from muteSource alone so the two can never disagree).

Feature: Enable/disable the auto-pause behavior from Settings
User Story: As a user who sometimes wants music to keep playing during calls, I want a single settings toggle to turn auto-pause on or off, so that I stay in control of whether Waves ever silences my media apps.
Expected Behavior:
• Settings exposes a 'Auto-pause music during calls' toggle bound to preferences.autoPauseMusicForConferencing (SettingsView.swift:88-96).
• Changing the toggle immediately persists preferences via store.persistPreferences() (SettingsView.swift:93-94).
• The preference defaults to true (UserPreferences.swift:10) and is decoded defensively so older preference files missing the key still load (UserPreferences.swift:46-47, 55).
• checkAutoPauseMusic guards on the preference and returns immediately when it is false, so no muting/resuming occurs while disabled (AppStore.swift:941).
Acceptance Criteria:
• Given the toggle is off, when a conferencing app becomes frontmost, no media app is muted (checkAutoPauseMusic returns at the guard).
• When the toggle is changed, the new value is written to the preferences store immediately (no app restart required).
• A preferences file written before this key existed loads with autoPauseMusicForConferencing == true (default).
• The toggle reflects the currently persisted value when Settings is opened.
Impl Notes: Toggle is fully wired and persisted. The gap is the disable-mid-call case: turning the toggle OFF while music is currently auto-paused does NOT immediately resume anything — the setter only persists the value and never calls a resume pass, so any already-.autoConferencing-muted apps stay silent until the next frontmost change moves off the conferencing app. A user toggling it off to 'bring my music back now' will see nothing happen. The settings copy ('during calls') also overstates capability given detection is frontmost+keyword only, not actual call state.
Errors Found:
• [MEDIUM/ux] Turning the toggle OFF mid-call leaves auto-paused apps muted indefinitely with no resume path (Sources/Waves/Settings/SettingsView.swift:92-95) — The Settings toggle setter only writes preferences.autoPauseMusicForConferencing and calls persistPreferences() (SettingsView.swift:92-95); it never triggers a resume pass. Worse than 'lingers until you leave the call app': once the preference is off, checkAutoPauseMusic returns at its first guard (AppStore.swift:941) on EVERY subsequent frontmost change, so the resume branch (970-984) is never reached. The .autoConferencing-muted apps therefore stay silent indefinitely — the user must manually unmute each one. A user toggling the option off to 'bring my music back now' sees nothing happen and has no automatic way to recover. Verified the early guard precedes the resume branch. | Fix: In the toggle's set closure, when turning the preference off, run a one-shot resume of all muteSource == .autoConferencing apps (the resume-branch logic), clear pausedMusicApps, then persist.
• [MEDIUM/edge-case] Auto-paused mute persists across relaunch and can re-mute (and stick) after the call is long over (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:820-844) — The session snapshot persists muteSource == .autoConferencing with isMuted == true (persistSessionSnapshot writes the full session). On relaunch, reapplyRestoredAudioState (AppStore.swift:824-844) re-applies isMuted=true for those apps, audibly re-muting them. The only resume path on launch is checkAutoPauseMusic() at line 219, which (a) requires the toggle on — if it is off, line 941 bails and the app stays muted forever; and (b) requires a non-conferencing app to be frontmost at that instant. If Waves restarts (update/crash/logout) hours after a call, a previously auto-paused media app comes back muted and, with the toggle off, never auto-resumes. This persistence/restore desync was missed by the first tester. | Fix: Do not persist .autoConferencing mutes as durable state (treat them as session-only and clear them on shutdown), or on launch always resume .autoConferencing-tagged apps that no conferencing app currently justifies, independent of the toggle state.
• [LOW/inconsistency] Toggle copy 'Auto-pause music during calls' overstates capability (Sources/Waves/Settings/SettingsView.swift:88-89) — The label promises behavior keyed to actual calls (SettingsView.swift:88-89), but detection is frontmost-app + keyword only with no notion of an active call (see PAUSE-01). It misses browser calls and fires on mere window focus, so 'during calls' is misleading. Verified label text and detection model. | Fix: Reword to reflect the heuristic, e.g. 'Pause media when a video-call app is in front', with a help note that browser-based calls are unsupported.

Feature: Respect user intent and app lifecycle during auto-pause
User Story: As a user, I want auto-pause to never fight my manual choices or get confused when apps quit, so that I can override Waves at any time and trust that stale paused-state never lingers.
Expected Behavior:
• A direct user mute/unmute always sets muteSource = .user so the auto-resume pass will never override it (AppStore.swift:501).
• If the user manually unmutes an app Waves auto-paused, it is removed from pausedMusicApps so resume does not double-act (AppStore.swift:504-506).
• Excluding an app removes it from pausedMusicApps and resets its muteSource to .user, so an excluded app is dropped from auto-pause bookkeeping (AppStore.swift:707, 711).
• On refresh, pausedMusicApps is filtered to only ids still present in the session, and again filtered after a session merge, so quit apps don't leak into the set (AppStore.swift:478-479, 934-936).
• muteSource is persisted in the session snapshot (SessionStore.swift:78) and re-merged on launch (AppStore.swift:1403), with a legacy-decode default of .user (AudioApp.swift:139).
Acceptance Criteria:
• Given Waves auto-paused an app, when the user manually unmutes it, it is removed from pausedMusicApps and a later resume pass does not re-toggle it.
• Given a user manually mutes an app during a call, that mute survives the call ending (muteSource .user not resumed).
• When an auto-paused app quits, its id is purged from pausedMusicApps on the next refresh/merge.
• An app moved to the exclusion list while auto-paused is removed from pausedMusicApps and no longer participates in resume.
• A session file written before muteSource existed decodes with muteSource == .user.
Impl Notes: This is the most carefully handled part of the feature: user-intent precedence, exclusion cleanup, and stale-id pruning are all consistent, and muteSource persistence has round-trip + legacy-default test coverage. Minor redundancy: pausedMusicApps duplicates information already carried by muteSource == .autoConferencing — resume reads muteSource, not the set, so the set is mostly bookkeeping and a potential source of drift if the two ever disagree. No correctness bug observed, but it is dead-weight state that could be derived from muteSource alone. There is no unit test exercising checkAutoPauseMusic itself (only the muteSource codec is tested).
Errors Found:
• [LOW/dead-code] pausedMusicApps is redundant bookkeeping that duplicates muteSource == .autoConferencing and can drift (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:961-984) — Resume selects apps via the muteSource filter (AppStore.swift:973), not via pausedMusicApps. The set is only inserted to (963), pruned (479, 711, 934-936) and cleared (983); it carries no information not derivable from muteSource == .autoConferencing on session apps. Verified resume never reads the set. Its only risk is silently diverging from muteSource (e.g. under the PAUSE-01 interleaving) without affecting resume — a debugging hazard rather than a functional input. | Fix: Remove pausedMusicApps; derive the paused set on demand from session.apps.filter { $0.muteSource == .autoConferencing } and delete the prune/clear sites.
• [LOW/edge-case] No unit test exercises checkAutoPauseMusic; only the muteSource codec is covered (Tests/WavesTests/AppDiscoveryPolicyTests.swift:62-72) — AppDiscoveryPolicyTests.swift covers only muteSource round-trip and legacy-decode default (the two @Test functions around lines 62-72). There is no test for pause/resume selection, user-intent precedence, exclusion cleanup, the previousFrontmostApp guard, or stale-id pruning — none of the acceptance criteria for this stateful behavior are guarded, so the PAUSE-01/02/03 regressions can land undetected. Confirmed via grep: the only test file referencing this feature is AppDiscoveryPolicyTests, and it touches only the codec. | Fix: Extract a pure helper from checkAutoPauseMusic and test: user-muted app skipped on pause, excluded app skipped, resume restricted to .autoConferencing, manual unmute removing from the paused set, and stale-id pruning.

Feature: Exclude an app from Waves management
User Story: As a user running a DAW, VoIP/echo-cancellation app, or other tap-sensitive audio tool, I want to exclude that app from Waves in one click, so that Waves never taps or alters its audio and the app keeps working normally.
Expected Behavior:
• Each mixer row's context menu shows 'Exclude from Waves' (or 'Manage with Waves' when already excluded) as the last item below a divider (MixerRowView.swift:158-161).
• Selecting it calls store.setExcluded(!isExcluded, for: app), which inserts/removes the app's logicalID in preferences.excludedAppIDs (a sorted, de-duplicated set) and persists preferences immediately (AppStore.swift:686-694).
• On exclude, Waves tears down any active route: it calls backend.releaseControllers(forBundleID:pid:) and locally resets the row to routingState=.monitorOnly, appliedVolume=nil, isMuted=false, muteSource=.user (AppStore.swift:696-708).
• On exclude, any pending volume-apply task is cancelled, the pending volume target is cleared, and the app is removed from the auto-paused-music set (AppStore.swift:709-711).
• A confirmation toast ('Excluded from Waves' / 'Managed by Waves' with the app name) appears for 1.4s (AppStore.swift:714-719).
• The same toggle is exposed as a VoiceOver custom action named 'Exclude from Waves' / 'Manage with Waves' (MixerRowView.swift:166-168).
Acceptance Criteria:
• After choosing 'Exclude from Waves', the app's logicalID is present in preferences.excludedAppIDs and the preferences file on disk reflects it.
• After exclusion, backend.releaseControllers is invoked and the row's routingState becomes .monitorOnly with appliedVolume nil and isMuted false.
• Choosing 'Manage with Waves' on an excluded app removes its logicalID from excludedAppIDs.
• excludedAppIDs round-trips through encode/decode preserving entries (covered by UserPreferencesTests).
• A toast is shown reflecting the new state.
Impl Notes: Core flow is fully wired and persisted. Edge case: setExcluded passes pid = app.pid ?? -1 to releaseControllers, and the backend matches by (bundleID == bundleID) OR (pid == pid). If a row has no bundleID and no pid, the -1 sentinel matches nothing, so teardown is a no-op (the app would simply re-show monitorOnly state, which is acceptable). Secondary concern: companion-audio processes get a pid-suffixed logicalID (bundleID::name::pid-N) from AppDiscoveryPolicy.logicalAppID, so excluding such a process stores a pid-bound key that will NOT match after a relaunch — exclusion of helper/renderer-type processes does not persist across restarts. Normal apps (logicalID == bundleID) persist correctly.

Feature: Excluded apps are never tapped or altered by any control path
User Story: As a user who excluded a sensitive app, I want every Waves control and automation to leave that app completely untouched, so that I can trust the exclusion as a real escape hatch rather than a cosmetic flag.
Expected Behavior:
• setDesiredVolume, setMuted, setVolumeBoost, and setOutputDevice all early-return via 'guard !isExcluded(app) else { return }' before touching the backend (AppStore.swift:357, 492, 545, 650).
• The launch-time reapplyRestoredAudioState loop skips excluded apps with 'guard !isExcluded(app) else { continue }', so saved volume/mute/boost/device is never re-applied to an excluded app (AppStore.swift:824-826).
• Auto-pause-music for conferencing filters out excluded apps when selecting music apps to mute (AppStore.swift:959).
• Auto-resume after conferencing filters out excluded apps when selecting resumable apps (AppStore.swift:973).
• isExcluded(app) is a simple membership check against preferences.excludedAppIDs keyed by logicalID (AppStore.swift:678-680).
Acceptance Criteria:
• Calling setDesiredVolume/setMuted/setVolumeBoost/setOutputDevice on an excluded app produces no backend call and no state change.
• On relaunch, an excluded app that previously had customized volume/mute/boost/device is not re-tapped (no setVolumeBoost/setMuted/setDesiredVolume backend calls for it).
• When a conferencing app becomes frontmost, an excluded media app is not auto-muted.
• When conferencing ends, an excluded app is not auto-resumed.
Code Refs:
• Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:357
• Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:492
• Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:545
• Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:650
• Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:824-826
• Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:959
• Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:973
Impl Notes: Exclusion guards are consistently placed at every per-app mutation entry point and both automation paths, so the escape-hatch promise ('never tapped, audio left completely untouched') holds. No automated tests cover these guards — only preference persistence is tested — so regressions (e.g. a future new control path that forgets the guard) would not be caught. The global keyboard-shortcut handlers (increaseVolumeForFrontmostApp/decreaseVolumeForFrontmostApp/toggleMuteForFrontmostApp) do not check isExcluded themselves; they rely on the downstream setDesiredVolume/setMuted guards (see EXCL-04 for the resulting misleading-toast gap).
Errors Found:
• [CRITICAL/correctness] Per-device volume-preset restore re-taps excluded apps on output-device change (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:851) — CONFIRMED. restoreDeviceVolumePresets iterates every app in session.apps and, for any app with a saved per-device preset, calls backend.setDesiredVolume / backend.setMuted / backend.setVolumeBoost directly with NO isExcluded guard (AppStore.swift:848-867). It is invoked from handleDeviceChange when the output device changes (AppStore.swift:802-803). setExcluded does not clear an app's saved deviceVolumePresets (AppStore.swift:686-720), and setDesiredVolume/setMuted/setVolumeBoost write presets before exclusion (AppStore.swift:380, 514, 561). So an app customized on a device and later excluded gets its saved settings re-applied straight to the backend on the next switch to that device — re-tapping the excluded app. This bypasses the early-return guards because it hits the backend directly. Contrast reapplyRestoredAudioState (AppStore.swift:826) which correctly guards with 'guard !isExcluded(app) else { continue }'. | Fix: Add 'guard !isExcluded(app) else { continue }' at the top of the loop in restoreDeviceVolumePresets (after reading session.apps[index]), mirroring reapplyRestoredAudioState. Optionally also drop the app's deviceVolumePresets entries in setExcluded.
• [MEDIUM/correctness] No automated coverage for the exclusion guards; an unguarded control path bypasses the escape hatch undetected (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:357) — CONFIRMED. The only test touching exclusion is UserPreferencesTests (codec round-trip of excludedAppIDs at UserPreferencesTests.swift:35-49). None of the AppStore guards (setDesiredVolume:357, setMuted:492, setVolumeBoost:545, setOutputDevice:650, reapplyRestoredAudioState:826, checkAutoPauseMusic:959/973) are tested, and the now-confirmed restoreDeviceVolumePresets (EXCL-02-1) and commitDesiredVolume (EXCL-04-1) gaps prove the risk is real, not hypothetical — at least two backend-facing paths already omit the guard and would not be caught. | Fix: Add unit tests against a spy backend asserting zero backend calls for an excluded app across setDesiredVolume/commitDesiredVolume/setMuted/setVolumeBoost/setOutputDevice, the launch restore, the device-preset restore, and the auto-pause/resume loops.

Feature: Excluded apps presented clearly and read-only in the mixer
User Story: As a user, I want excluded apps to stay visible in the mixer but clearly marked and with their controls disabled, so that I can see what I excluded and re-enable it without losing track of the app.
Expected Behavior:
• Excluded rows remain in the list (visibleApps does not filter them out; it only filters system processes), so the app is still shown rather than hidden (AppStore.swift:103-107).
• Full row shows an 'Excluded' capsule badge next to the app name with accessibilityLabel 'Excluded from Waves' (MixerRowView.swift:29-37).
• For excluded rows the routing-state chip/dot is hidden because Waves isn't controlling them (MixerRowView.swift:46-50; compact: 273-279).
• Slider, boost menu, and mute button are .disabled(isExcluded) in both full and compact rows (MixerRowView.swift:81,94,107,308,311,324).
• Excluded rows are dimmed to 0.55 opacity (MixerRowView.swift:109,326).
• The 'Output Device' submenu is omitted from the context menu for excluded apps (MixerRowView.swift:139-157).
• No live level meter is drawn for excluded rows (showsLevelMeter requires !isExcluded) (MixerRowView.swift:173-175).
Acceptance Criteria:
• An excluded app still appears in the mixer list (not removed).
• The 'Excluded' badge/text and dimmed appearance render for excluded rows in both full and compact layouts.
• Volume slider, boost menu, and mute button are non-interactive for excluded rows.
• No routing-state indicator and no level meter is shown for excluded rows.
• The context menu hides the Output Device submenu for excluded apps.
Impl Notes: Presentation is thorough and consistent across full and compact rows. UX gap: the menu-bar surface uses CompactMixerRow, which has NO context menu and no exclude/re-include affordance at all (MenuBarMixerView.swift:191; the .contextMenu only exists on MixerRowView, the main-window full row at MixerRowView.swift:135). A user who lives in the menu bar can see an app is 'Excluded' but cannot toggle exclusion from there — they must open the main window. The exclude action is also discoverable only via right-click/VoiceOver action, with no visible button or hint.
Errors Found:
• [LOW/ux] Menu-bar mixer offers no way to exclude or re-include an app (Sources/Waves/Features/Mixer/MenuBarMixerView.swift:191) — CONFIRMED (severity lowered to low). CompactMixerRow (MixerRowView.swift:259-327, rendered by MenuBarMixerView via CompactSection at MenuBarMixerView.swift:191) has no .contextMenu and no exclude/re-include control. The 'Exclude from Waves' / 'Manage with Waves' toggle and its accessibility action exist only on the main-window MixerRowView (MixerRowView.swift:159-168). A menu-bar-centric user sees the 'Excluded' label but cannot toggle exclusion without opening the main window. Lowered from medium to low because the escape hatch remains fully reachable from the main window and feature-status documents the toggle as a main-window affordance (the re-include capability still exists; only the menu-bar surface lacks the control). | Fix: Add a .contextMenu (or small toggle) with 'Exclude from Waves' / 'Manage with Waves' to CompactMixerRow mirroring MixerRowView, or document that exclusion is managed from the main window only.
• [LOW/accessibility] Compact 'Excluded' badge lacks the descriptive accessibility label used in the full row (Sources/Waves/Features/Mixer/MixerRowView.swift:274) — CONFIRMED. The full row's Excluded badge sets .accessibilityLabel('Excluded from Waves') (MixerRowView.swift:36). The compact row's Excluded Text (MixerRowView.swift:273-276) has no accessibilityLabel, so VoiceOver in the menu-bar layout reads the bare word 'Excluded' instead of 'Excluded from Waves'. An inconsistency in the 'clearly marked' presentation between the two surfaces. | Fix: Add .accessibilityLabel('Excluded from Waves') to the compact Excluded Text to match the full row.

Feature: Presets and keyboard shortcuts respect exclusions
User Story: As a user, I want saving and applying presets (and using global volume shortcuts) to never silently re-tap an app I excluded, so that automation can't defeat my escape hatch.
Expected Behavior:
• applyPreset strips entries whose appID is in excludedAppIDs before sending the preset to the backend, so an excluded app is never re-tapped by a preset (AppStore.swift:1001-1007).
• savePreset filters out excluded entries from the saved preset so they aren't baked in for later application (AppStore.swift:1035-1040).
• Global shortcut handlers (increase/decrease/toggle for frontmost app) route through setDesiredVolume/setMuted, which are guarded by isExcluded, so an excluded frontmost app's audio is not changed (AppStore.swift:1210-1262 → 357/492).
Acceptance Criteria:
• Applying a preset that contains an excluded app's appID does not tap or alter that app.
• A saved preset does not contain entries for currently-excluded apps.
• Pressing the global volume-up/down or mute shortcut while an excluded app is frontmost does not change that app's audio.
Impl Notes: Preset apply/save correctly strip excluded entries, and shortcut mutations are blocked by the downstream guards, so no audio is altered. However the keyboard-shortcut handlers compute newVolume / newMutedState and unconditionally show a success toast ('Volume increased', 'Volume decreased', 'Muted'/'Unmuted') BEFORE/around the guarded call (AppStore.swift:1221-1226, 1240-1245, 1256-1261). When the frontmost app is excluded, setDesiredVolume/setMuted no-op but the toast still claims the change happened — a misleading-feedback bug. Preset stripping only filters by exact appID equality against excludedAppIDs, so a pid-suffixed companion logicalID (see EXCL-01) would not be matched after relaunch.
Errors Found:
• [CRITICAL/correctness] Unguarded commitDesiredVolume taps an excluded app — reachable from the global volume shortcut, the main-window =/- keys, and the set-volume URL scheme (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:386) — CONFIRMED, and broader than originally filed. increaseVolumeForFrontmostApp / decreaseVolumeForFrontmostApp call setDesiredVolume (guarded → no-op, never sets pendingVolumeTargets) then commitDesiredVolume (AppStore.swift:1218-1219, 1237-1238). commitDesiredVolume has NO isExcluded guard: with pendingVolumeTargets[appKey] nil it sets it to app.desiredVolume (AppStore.swift:386-388) and calls scheduleVolumeApply(immediate: true), whose task runs ensureBackendHasApp then backend.setDesiredVolume directly (AppStore.swift:426-427). An excluded app stays in visibleApps→activeApps (visibleApps filters only on system category, AppStore.swift:103-107), so it is the shortcut's frontmost target and gets tapped, showing a 'Managed route active' toast. COMPLETENESS: the same unguarded commitDesiredVolume is also reached by (a) the main-window '='/'-' keyboard shortcut via nudgeVolume (MainWindowView.swift:383-384, 416-420) on a selected excluded row, and (b) the waves://set-volume URL scheme via handleURLSetVolume (AppStore.swift:308-309) — the latter is exactly the kind of programmatic automation the escape hatch must block. | Fix: Add 'guard !isExcluded(app) else { return }' to commitDesiredVolume itself (single fix covering all three callers), and early-return in increaseVolumeForFrontmostApp/decreaseVolumeForFrontmostApp before showing any toast.
• [MEDIUM/ux] Volume/mute shortcuts show a success toast even when the excluded app should be untouched (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1256) — CONFIRMED. increaseVolumeForFrontmostApp/decreaseVolumeForFrontmostApp/toggleMuteForFrontmostApp unconditionally show 'Volume increased'/'Volume decreased'/'Muted'/'Unmuted' toasts (AppStore.swift:1221-1226, 1240-1245, 1256-1261). For toggleMuteForFrontmostApp, setMuted genuinely no-ops on an excluded app (guard at AppStore.swift:492) yet the toast still claims the mute changed — a direct lie. For the volume shortcuts the toast accompanies the (wrong) real tap from EXCL-04-1, so fixing that path also fixes the toast. | Fix: Guard the shortcut handlers on isExcluded and return before showing any toast (or show an 'App excluded from Waves' info toast instead).

Feature: Settings window structure & tabbed navigation
User Story: As a Waves user, I want a standard macOS Settings window with clearly separated tabs, so that I can find and adjust preferences, setup status, audio routing, presets, diagnostics, and help in one familiar place.
Expected Behavior:
• Opening Settings (⌘, via the standard Settings scene) presents a TabView with six tabs: General, Setup, Audio, Presets, Advanced, Help, each with an SF Symbol label (SettingsView.swift:8-37)
• The window is provided by the SwiftUI `Settings { SettingsView() }` scene wired in WavesApp and constrained to a minimum size of 720x500 (WavesApp.swift:51-55)
• The whole window is padded 20pt and painted with the app's dark window gradient background (SettingsView.swift:38-39)
• The entire app (including Settings) is pinned to darkAqua appearance so the dark gradient never sits under light-mode label colors that would make text invisible (WavesApp.swift:88)
• When the Settings window disappears, store.persistPreferences() is called to flush any pending preference changes to disk (SettingsView.swift:40-42)
• All tab subviews read shared state from the same AppStore via @Environment(AppStore.self) injected at the scene (SettingsView.swift:5,47; WavesApp.swift:53)
Acceptance Criteria:
• Pressing ⌘, opens a single Settings window showing exactly six tabs in the order General, Setup, Audio, Presets, Advanced, Help
• Each tab shows its labeled SF Symbol icon and switches content when clicked
• The window cannot be resized below 720x500
• Text in every tab is legible (light-on-dark) regardless of the system's Light/Dark appearance setting
• Closing the Settings window triggers a preferences save
Impl Notes: Cleanly wired. Note the Setup tab embeds OnboardingView and the Help tab embeds HelpView — those are full features owned by other audit areas (Onboarding, Help) and are only surfaced here as tabs; I scope them out of dedicated stories below. Minor: persistPreferences() on onDisappear is redundant because every General toggle already persists inline on change, but it is harmless.

Feature: General preferences toggles & sort mode
User Story: As a Waves user, I want a General settings panel with toggles for app-list visibility, automation, and an app sort order, so that I can tailor which apps appear and how Waves behaves to my workflow.
Expected Behavior:
• GeneralSettingsView renders a grouped Form with toggles: Launch at login, Show recent apps, Show system processes, Auto-restore device, Auto-pause music during calls, Enable keyboard shortcuts, Per-device volume presets, URL scheme automation, plus a 'Sort apps by' picker (SettingsView.swift:50-176)
• 'Show system processes' off filters out apps whose category is .system from visibleApps; on includes them (AppStore.swift:103-107)
• 'Show recent apps' off makes recentApps return an empty list; on returns visible apps that are not currently live (AppStore.swift:124-128)
• 'Auto-restore device' gates handleDeviceChange so routes are only re-read on device change when enabled (AppStore.swift:792-793)
• 'Auto-pause music during calls' gates checkAutoPauseMusic so media apps are only auto-muted when a conferencing app is frontmost while enabled (AppStore.swift:940-941, 955-984)
• 'Per-device volume presets' gates restoring saved per-device volumes on device switch (AppStore.swift:802-803)
• 'Sort apps by' offers Activity/Name/Category/Manual (SortMode.allCases) and drives sortedApps ordering; manual uses the saved customAppOrder (SettingsView.swift:162-175; UserPreferences.swift:116-136; AppStore.swift:1278-1337)
• Each of these toggles/picker (except Launch-at-login and keyboard shortcuts) sets the preference and immediately calls store.persistPreferences() in its setter (SettingsView.swift:62-176)
• Defaults: showRecentApps=true, showSystemProcesses=false, autoRestoreDevice=true, autoPauseMusicForConferencing=true, enableKeyboardShortcuts=true, enablePerDeviceVolumePresets=true, sortMode=.name (UserPreferences.swift:4-12)
• Preferences decode field-by-field with per-key fallbacks so a file written by an older build (missing keys) loads cleanly instead of wiping all settings (UserPreferences.swift:40-61)
Acceptance Criteria:
• Toggling 'Show system processes' on/off adds/removes system-category apps from the mixer list
• Toggling 'Show recent apps' off hides the recent-apps section (recentApps is empty)
• Changing 'Sort apps by' reorders the app list per the selected mode immediately
• Selecting Manual sort honors a previously saved drag order; choosing it with no saved order still produces a stable name-based order
• Every General toggle persists across an app relaunch (written to Application Support/Waves/preferences.json)
• Loading a preferences file missing newer keys keeps existing values and fills new keys with defaults rather than resetting everything
Impl Notes: All toggles are genuinely wired to behavior (verified each gating call site), not placeholders. One UX gap: the 'Sort apps by' picker lets the user choose Manual even when no custom order exists; on next launch the init() silently downgrades manual→name if customAppOrder is empty (AppStore.swift:88-90), so a user who selects Manual but never drags will find it reset to Name after relaunch — mildly surprising. Bindings persist inline AND onDisappear, so writes are duplicated but consistent.
Errors Found:
• [LOW/ux] Selecting Manual sort with no drag order silently reverts to Name after relaunch (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:88-90) — Verified in code: the 'Sort apps by' picker persists Manual immediately (SettingsView.swift:162-175), but AppStore.init downgrades manual->name when customAppOrder is empty (AppStore.swift:88-90). A user who selects Manual but never drags any row finds the setting reset to Name after relaunch with no explanation. In-session the manualOrderComparator (AppStore.swift:1327-1337) falls back to displayName, so the live order is stable; only the persisted choice is silently discarded. | Fix: Keep sortMode == .manual across relaunch even with an empty customAppOrder (the comparator already falls back to name), or disable/annotate the Manual option until the user performs at least one drag.
• [LOW/edge-case] Manual sort order silently loses hidden / not-running apps on every reorder (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1345-1351) — reorderApps rebuilds the persisted order from the *filtered* set: preferences.customAppOrder = visibleApps.map { $0.logicalID } (AppStore.swift:1345,1351). visibleApps excludes system-category apps when 'Show system processes' is off, and excludes any app not currently running. So any app that was previously placed in the manual order but is hidden or quit at the moment of the next drag is permanently dropped from customAppOrder. When it reappears it falls to Int.max in manualOrderComparator (AppStore.swift:1330-1331), i.e. sorted to the end by name, losing its prior manual position. The first tester missed this; it is a genuine persistence/desync edge for the 'honors a previously saved drag order' criterion when the visible set changes between reorders. | Fix: Merge the reordered visible IDs back into the existing customAppOrder (preserve entries for apps not currently visible) instead of replacing the whole array with visibleApps only.

Feature: Launch at login (login item) with approval handling
User Story: As a Waves user, I want a 'Launch at login' toggle that registers Waves as a macOS login item, so that the mixer starts automatically when I log in.
Expected Behavior:
• The 'Launch at login' toggle binds to store.launchAtLoginEnabled, whose setter calls LoginItemService.setEnabled and registers/unregisters via SMAppService.mainApp (SettingsView.swift:51-56; AppStore.swift:161-186; LoginItemService.swift:29-35)
• After attempting to enable, the store reads back the real SMAppService status and stores preferences.launchAtLoginEnabled from that actual status (not the requested value) (AppStore.swift:166-169)
• If macOS requires user approval (status .requiresApproval) so the effective state differs from requested, a warning toast 'Login item needs approval' is shown with the status description (AppStore.swift:170-177; LoginItemService.swift:18-19)
• If register/unregister throws, the store falls back to the real status, persists it, and shows an error toast 'Login item update failed' (AppStore.swift:178-184)
• On launch and on session sync, the persisted launchAtLoginEnabled and onboarding state are reconciled with the live login-item status so the toggle reflects reality (AppStore.swift:95-99, 1356-1365)
Acceptance Criteria:
• Enabling the toggle registers Waves as a login item via SMAppService
• If the OS requires approval, the toggle reflects the real (still-disabled) state and a warning toast appears telling the user approval is needed
• A failure to register/unregister surfaces an error toast and does not leave the toggle showing a false 'on' state
• Reopening Settings shows the toggle matching the actual current login-item registration status
Impl Notes: Robust: it reflects the OS-reported status rather than the requested value and gives toast feedback for the approval and error cases — good handling of the .requiresApproval edge case. No way to deep-link the user to System Settings > Login Items from the warning toast, which would smooth the approval flow, but that is a minor enhancement.
Errors Found: • [LOW/edge-case] Login-at-login toggle can show stale state if changed externally while Settings stays open (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1356-1365) — Verified: store.launchAtLoginEnabled getter reads preferences.launchAtLoginEnabled (AppStore.swift:161-162), which is reconciled with the real SMAppService status only at init (AppStore.swift:95) and inside syncOnboarding (AppStore.swift:1356-1365), called on start/refresh/device-change/recover. start() is guarded by isBootstrapped so it runs once. GeneralSettingsView and SettingsView have no .onAppear that re-reads login status (only the Advanced tab has an onAppear, SettingsView.swift:310). If the user toggles the login item externally (System Settings > Login Items) or grants pending approval there, then reopens Waves Settings without triggering a refresh/device-change, the toggle shows the stale persisted value. | Fix: Re-read loginItemService.status and update preferences.launchAtLoginEnabled in GeneralSettingsView.onAppear (or SettingsView.onAppear) so the toggle reflects the real registration status each time Settings opens.

Feature: Keyboard shortcuts toggle, global hotkey lifecycle & cheat-sheet
User Story: As a Waves user, I want to enable or disable global volume/mute hotkeys and see what they are, so that I can control the frontmost app's audio without leaving my current app while keeping Waves from monitoring keystrokes when I don't want it to.
Expected Behavior:
• The 'Enable keyboard shortcuts' toggle routes through store.setKeyboardShortcutsEnabled, which sets the preference, persists it, and posts .wavesKeyboardShortcutsPreferenceChanged (SettingsView.swift:98-103; AppStore.swift:1272-1276)
• AppDelegate observes that notification and installs the global key monitor only while shortcuts are enabled, removing it otherwise so Waves never observes global keystrokes when disabled (WavesApp.swift:91-112, 150-162)
• While enabled, the General tab reveals a 'Keyboard shortcuts' cheat-sheet listing Increase volume ⌘⌥↑, Decrease volume ⌘⌥↓, Toggle mute ⌘⌥M (SettingsView.swift:127-160)
• The global monitor maps keyCode 126→increaseVolumeForFrontmostApp, 125→decrease, 46→toggleMute, requiring Command+Option, and ignores events while a text field/text view is first responder (WavesApp.swift:164-189)
• Each shortcut handler re-checks preferences.enableKeyboardShortcuts before acting and targets the frontmost active app (falling back to first visible app), showing a confirmation toast (AppStore.swift:1210-1262)
Acceptance Criteria:
• Disabling the toggle removes the global event monitor (Waves stops receiving global keystrokes)
• Enabling the toggle installs the monitor and shows the shortcut cheat-sheet in the General tab
• ⌘⌥↑/↓ adjust, and ⌘⌥M toggles mute on, the frontmost app, with a toast
• Hotkeys are ignored while the user is typing in a text field
• The shortcut list is informational only and matches the documented keys
Impl Notes: The enable/disable lifecycle for the global monitor is correctly gated through a notification, which is good for privacy. The shortcuts are hard-coded and non-rebindable — the cheat-sheet is display-only, so a user cannot customize keys. Also the listed shortcuts are static text and would silently drift if the keyCode mapping in WavesApp ever changed; no single source of truth ties the cheat-sheet to the handler.
Errors Found:
• [HIGH/correctness] Shortcuts target first-sorted active (audible-or-frontmost) app, not the true frontmost app (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1212) — Verified: increaseVolumeForFrontmostApp / decreaseVolumeForFrontmostApp / toggleMuteForFrontmostApp all resolve the target as `activeApps.first ?? visibleApps.first` (AppStore.swift:1212,1231,1250). activeApps = visibleApps.filter(\.isActive) (AppStore.swift:113-115), and the backend sets isActive = isAudible || isFrontmost (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:1189). So `activeApps.first` is the first app *in current sort order* that is audible OR frontmost, not the OS frontmost app. Concrete trigger: background music (audible->active) sorts before the foreground app under Name/Category sort, so pressing the hotkey changes the music app's volume/mute instead of the foreground app. isActive is also snapshot-derived and only refreshed periodically (checkAutoPauseMusic at AppStore.swift:940-943 deliberately uses NSWorkspace.shared.frontmostApplication *instead of* the snapshot for exactly this reason), so the shortcut handlers ignore the more-correct pattern already present in the codebase. When nothing is active, the visibleApps.first fallback mutes/adjusts an arbitrary alphabetically-first app. | Fix: Resolve the frontmost app from NSWorkspace.shared.frontmostApplication (match bundleIdentifier/pid against session.apps as checkAutoPauseMusic already does) and act on that app; fall back to activeApps.first only when no managed match exists for the OS-frontmost app.
• [MEDIUM/logic] Text-field guard is ineffective: a global monitor never sees the foreign app's first responder (Sources/Waves/App/WavesApp.swift:169-172) — Verified: handleGlobalKeyEvent guards via NSApp.keyWindow?.firstResponder being an NSTextView/NSTextField (WavesApp.swift:169-172). But the monitor is installed only via NSEvent.addGlobalMonitorForEvents (WavesApp.swift:152) with no addLocalMonitorForEvents companion (grep confirms only one monitor). A global monitor delivers key events destined for OTHER apps; when another app is frontmost (the only time these events fire) NSApp.keyWindow is generally nil, so the guard reads nil and never blocks. Result: pressing the hotkeys (incl. Cmd+Option+M) while typing in another app's text field still fires. Practical severity is tempered because the shortcuts use Command+Option, which rarely collides with text entry, but the acceptance criterion is not met. | Fix: Detect typing in the frontmost foreign app via the Accessibility API (AXUIElement focused-element role) rather than NSApp.keyWindow, or explicitly document the guard as only covering Waves' own windows.
• [LOW/ux] Each volume/mute hotkey emits two stacked toasts (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1221-1226) — Verified: increaseVolumeForFrontmostApp shows a 'Volume increased' info toast (AppStore.swift:1221-1226) and then calls commitDesiredVolume, whose apply path shows a second 'Managed route active - <App> set to N%' success toast (AppStore.swift:433-438). Likewise toggleMuteForFrontmostApp shows 'Muted'/'Unmuted' (AppStore.swift:1256-1261) while the setMuted it calls shows 'App muted'/'App unmuted' (AppStore.swift:528-533). With maxToasts = 3, a single keypress consumes two of the three toast slots with duplicated information. The first tester did not flag this. | Fix: Suppress the per-action confirmation toast in setMuted/commit when invoked from a shortcut handler (e.g. a silent variant), or drop the handler-level toast and rely on the apply-path toast.
• [LOW/inconsistency] Cheat-sheet shortcut text is decoupled from the keyCode handler (silent drift risk) (Sources/Waves/Settings/SettingsView.swift:132-157) — Verified: the cheat-sheet strings '⌘⌥↑','⌘⌥↓','⌘⌥M' in GeneralSettingsView (SettingsView.swift:136,145,154) are hard-coded literals with no shared source of truth with the keyCode switch (126/125/46 + Command+Option) in WavesApp.handleGlobalKeyEvent (WavesApp.swift:179-188). Today the values are consistent (keyCode 46 = ANSI M, 126 = up, 125 = down) so there is no user-facing error, but if the handler mapping changes the displayed cheat-sheet would silently misstate the real keys. | Fix: Define the shortcut set (keyCode, modifiers, label) once in a shared enum/struct and drive both the global-monitor switch and the cheat-sheet rows from it.

Feature: URL scheme automation opt-in (security default)
User Story: As a privacy-conscious user, I want URL scheme automation to be off by default and only enabled by an explicit choice, so that other apps cannot drive Waves via waves:// links unless I deliberately allow it.
Expected Behavior:
• The 'URL scheme automation' toggle defaults off (enableURLScheme=false) and carries a help tooltip explaining it lets other apps/links control Waves via waves:// URLs (UserPreferences.swift:13; SettingsView.swift:115-125)
• Turning the toggle on sets enableURLScheme and also marks urlSchemeAutomationAcknowledged=true, then persists (SettingsView.swift:117-123)
• On startup, if the user has never acknowledged the automation choice, the store force-disables URL schemes and marks them acknowledged — a fail-safe so automation is never silently on after an upgrade (AppStore.swift:91-94)
• handleURLScheme rejects (and logs a warning for) any incoming waves:// invocation while enableURLScheme is disabled (AppStore.swift:256-258; WavesApp.swift:197-200)
• The AppDelegate registers/unregisters the Apple Event GetURL handler around app lifecycle so the scheme is only handled while the app runs (WavesApp.swift:124-148, 191-200)
Acceptance Criteria:
• On a fresh install the URL scheme toggle is off and waves:// commands are ignored
• Enabling the toggle persists both enableURLScheme=true and the acknowledgment flag
• While the toggle is off, sending a waves:// URL produces no audio change and is logged as rejected
• After an app update where the user had never made a choice, automation remains disabled until explicitly enabled
Impl Notes: Sensible security posture: opt-in, acknowledgment-gated, and force-disabled when never acknowledged. One subtlety: the acknowledgment flag is set true the first time the toggle is touched (even when turning it back off) and the startup fail-safe at AppStore.swift:91-94 only fires when not yet acknowledged — so the 'reset to off on upgrade' protection only applies to users who never interacted with the toggle, which is the intended scope but easy to misread.
Errors Found: • [LOW/dead-code] application(_:open:) URL path is effectively unreachable because a custom GetURL AppleEvent handler is installed (Sources/Waves/App/WavesApp.swift:191-195) — Verified: applicationDidFinishLaunching installs a custom kAEGetURL AppleEvent handler via setupURLSchemeHandler (WavesApp.swift:89,124-131), which overrides NSApplication's default GetURL handler that would otherwise dispatch to application(_:open:). With the override in place, incoming waves:// URLs are delivered to handleGetURLEvent (WavesApp.swift:140-148) and AppDelegate.application(_:open:) (WavesApp.swift:191-195) is effectively dead. Both ultimately call handleURLScheme, so behavior is correct, but the open(urls:) implementation is redundant/unreachable and could mislead a maintainer into thinking there are two live entry points (or, if both ever fired, double-dispatch the command). Info.plist does register the waves scheme, so the launch path matters; the AppleEvent handler simply wins. | Fix: Remove the redundant application(_:open:) (or remove the manual setEventHandler and rely on application(_:open:)) so there is one well-defined URL entry path; if both must coexist, guard against double handling.

Feature: Audio tab: current output device & route recovery
User Story: As a Waves user, I want the Audio settings tab to show my current output device, explain how managed routing works, and let me re-establish broken routes, so that I can understand and recover my audio routing without leaving Settings.
Expected Behavior:
• AudioSettingsView shows 'Current output device' with store.currentDeviceName, which resolves to the session's current device name or 'No output device' when none is present (SettingsView.swift:186-189; AppStore.swift:139-141)
• A 'Managed routing' explainer describes that Waves uses Core Audio process taps to capture, process (volume/mute/boost) and replay app audio locally to the current output device (SettingsView.swift:191-196)
• A 'Recover Routes' button calls store.recoverRoutes(), which asks the backend to reattach managed routing paths, refreshes diagnostics and the session, and shows a 'Routes recovered' success toast — or a 'Recovery failed' error toast with fallback to the current snapshot (SettingsView.swift:198-200; AppStore.swift:1181-1202)
• A caption guides boost usage (1x transparent, 2x–4x for quiet apps to avoid clipping) (SettingsView.swift:202-204)
Acceptance Criteria:
• The Audio tab displays the actual current output device name, or 'No output device' when none is connected
• Tapping 'Recover Routes' reattaches managed routes and shows a success toast on success
• If recovery fails, an error toast is shown and the view falls back to the current backend snapshot rather than leaving a broken state
• The managed-routing and boost explainer text is present and accurate
Impl Notes: Functional but read-only: the Audio tab shows the current device name and a Recover Routes action but provides no output-device picker here (device selection lives elsewhere in the app, e.g. selectOutputDevice at AppStore.swift:759). That is a deliberate split, but a user expecting to change the output device from the 'Audio' settings tab will not find a control. The 'No output device' empty state is handled.

Feature: Presets management (import / export / delete / empty state)
User Story: As a Waves user, I want to manage my saved mix presets from Settings — importing, exporting, and deleting them — so that I can back up, share, and curate reusable per-app volume mixes.
Expected Behavior:
• PresetSettingsView lists store.presets, each row showing the preset name and an entry count with correct singular/plural ('1 app' vs 'N apps') (SettingsView.swift:234-242)
• When no presets exist, an empty-state message 'No presets yet. Save a mix from the main window, or import one.' is shown (SettingsView.swift:227-232)
• An 'Import' button triggers store.importPreset(), which opens an NSOpenPanel for a JSON file, validates size (≤10MB), non-empty name, and ≤1000 entries, then either replaces a same-named preset (preserving id/createdAt) or appends with a fresh UUID, saves, and shows a success/failure toast (SettingsView.swift:221-224; AppStore.swift:1112-1179)
• Each row's 'Export' button calls store.exportPreset, which encodes pretty-printed JSON and presents an NSSavePanel defaulting to '<name>.json', writing atomically and toasting success/failure (SettingsView.swift:246-249; AppStore.swift:1079-1110)
• Each row's 'Delete' button (and swipe-to-delete via onDelete) calls store.deletePresets(at:), which removes by offset, persists via presetStore.save, and shows a 'Preset removed' toast; the delete button carries an accessibility label 'Delete preset <name>' (SettingsView.swift:252-262; AppStore.swift:1066-1077)
Acceptance Criteria:
• With zero presets, the empty-state copy is shown and the list is empty
• Importing a valid preset JSON adds it (or updates a same-named one) and shows a success toast
• Importing an oversized (>10MB), empty-named, or >1000-entry file is rejected with a specific error toast
• Exporting a preset writes a pretty-printed JSON file at the chosen location and confirms via toast
• Deleting a preset (button or swipe) removes it from the list, persists the change, and confirms via toast
• Each delete control exposes an accessible label naming the preset
Impl Notes: Thorough import validation (size/name/entry-count caps) and identity handling (fresh UUID on append, id/createdAt preserved on same-name overwrite) are solid and guard against list-identity bugs. Both export and import depend on NSApp.mainWindow being present to anchor the panel; if the main mixer window is closed while only Settings is open, exportPreset/importPreset bail with a 'No main window available' error toast (AppStore.swift:1091-1093, 1120-1122) — a real edge case where the feature is unusable from a standalone Settings window.
Errors Found: • [HIGH/edge-case] Import/Export unusable from a standalone Settings window (no main window to anchor the panel) (Sources/Waves/Stores/AppStore.swift:1091-1093) — Verified: exportPreset and importPreset both require NSApp.mainWindow to anchor the NSSavePanel/NSOpenPanel sheet and bail with a 'No main window available' error toast if it is nil (AppStore.swift:1091-1093 and 1120-1123). Waves is a menu-bar utility whose mixer is a single closable Window; Settings is opened independently (Cmd-, or the menu bar). With the main mixer window closed (a normal state), NSApp.mainWindow is nil, so neither Import nor any row's Export works - the core function of the Presets tab is dead in that state. | Fix: Fall back to NSApp.keyWindow (the Settings window) when NSApp.mainWindow is nil, or present the panel modeless via begin {}; apply the same fix to importPreset at AppStore.swift:1120-1123.

Feature: Advanced diagnostics report, copy-to-clipboard & unavailable state
User Story: As a user troubleshooting audio routing, I want an Advanced tab showing route-health diagnostics and a one-click way to copy a plain-text report, so that I can self-diagnose or file a useful bug report.
Expected Behavior:
• DiagnosticsSettingsView shows a 'Running apps' header with store.sourceInventorySummary (e.g. 'N running apps', singular/plural aware) and refreshes diagnostics on appear (SettingsView.swift:275-289, 310-312; AppStore.swift:155-159)
• When diagnostics are available, each check is rendered as a card with its title and detail over an ultraThinMaterial rounded background (SettingsView.swift:291-303)
• A 'Copy Diagnostics' button calls store.copyDiagnosticsToPasteboard(), which builds a plain-text report (macOS version, output device, audio component installed, capture permission granted/not, route-recovery health, last error, per-app routing/volume/mute/boost lines, and each check) and copies it, toasting 'Diagnostics copied' (SettingsView.swift:283-289; AppStore.swift:725-751, 774-779)
• When diagnostics are nil, DiagnosticsUnavailableView shows 'Diagnostics are not loaded' plus the backend's lastError (or guidance text), with 'Refresh Diagnostics' and 'Recover Routes' buttons (SettingsView.swift:304-347; AppStore.swift:324)
• Diagnostics are produced by backend.diagnosticsReport() and refreshed on many state changes (start, refresh, device change, recover) (AppStore.swift:215, 246, 807, 1186, 1204-1207)
Acceptance Criteria:
• Opening the Advanced tab triggers a diagnostics refresh and renders one card per check when a report exists
• The 'Running apps' summary count matches the number of visible apps and uses correct singular/plural
• 'Copy Diagnostics' places a complete plain-text report on the clipboard including OS, device, permission status, route health, per-app state, and checks, and shows a confirmation toast
• When no diagnostics are loaded, the unavailable view shows the last backend error (when present) and offers Refresh and Recover actions
• Tapping Refresh or Recover in the unavailable state reloads/repairs and updates the panel
Impl Notes: Genuinely useful: the copied report is comprehensive and the nil/unavailable state is handled with actionable buttons and surfaces the backend's lastError. The diagnostics cards are read-only text with no severity color/icon per check status (DiagnosticsCheck.status is only emitted in the copied text via [rawValue], not visually in the cards at SettingsView.swift:291-303), so a failing check is not visually distinguished from a passing one in the UI — a minor scannability gap relative to the plain-text export.
Errors Found:
• [MEDIUM/inconsistency] Diagnostics 'unavailable' state with lastError is practically unreachable (Sources/Waves/Settings/SettingsView.swift:304-306) — Verified: DiagnosticsSettingsView shows DiagnosticsUnavailableView only when store.diagnostics is nil (SettingsView.swift:291,304-306). backend.diagnosticsReport() is non-throwing and always returns a fully-populated DiagnosticsReport with 5 checks even in degraded states (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:241-278), onAppear immediately calls refreshDiagnostics (SettingsView.swift:310-312), and grep confirms diagnostics is never set back to nil anywhere in AppStore. So the unavailable branch - the only place session.backendStatus.lastError is surfaced in this tab (SettingsView.swift:324) - is shown only during the brief async gap before the first report resolves (or if start() failed entirely and Advanced is opened first). A genuinely degraded backend still yields a report with warning/failed checks, so the user lands on the cards view, not the lastError view. | Fix: Surface session.backendStatus.lastError within the cards/available view (e.g. a prominent error banner when lastError is non-nil) rather than gating it solely on diagnostics == nil.
• [LOW/accessibility] Diagnostics check cards do not convey status (passed/warning/failed) visually or to VoiceOver (Sources/Waves/Settings/SettingsView.swift:291-303) — Verified: each DiagnosticsCheck carries a status (passed/warning/failed/informational) and diagnosticsReport assigns meaningful statuses (WorkspaceAudioControlBackend.swift:245-275). The copied plain-text report includes it as [rawValue] (AppStore.swift:747). But the in-app cards render only check.title and check.detail over a uniform ultraThinMaterial background with no color, icon, or accessibilityLabel per status (SettingsView.swift:291-303). A failing check is visually indistinguishable from a passing one, and VoiceOver users get no status cue. | Fix: Add a status SF Symbol + accent color per check (checkmark.circle/exclamationmark.triangle/xmark.octagon) and an accessibilityLabel including the status text, mirroring the plain-text export.
